products.title: Nymphaea Golden West | Pink Tropical Water Lily
products.body_html:
Nymphaea 'Golden West' is a captivating tropical water lily hybridized by Martin E. Randig in 1936. This medium-sized aquatic plant is renowned for its profuse flowering and beautiful autumn-pink blooms that add a vibrant touch to any pond or water garden.

Key Features:

  • Vibrant Pink Blooms: The flowers of Nymphaea 'Golden West' exhibit a charming autumn-pink color, adding a stunning visual appeal to your water garden. Each flower is cup-shaped to star-shaped, enhancing its aesthetic charm.
  • Distinctive Foliage: The lily features green leaves with new growth emerging mottled with red, maturing to a lush green. These leaves float gracefully on the water’s surface, providing a beautiful backdrop for the flowers.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Sunlight: Thrives in full sun, requiring at least 6 hours of direct sunlight daily for optimal blooming.
  • Water Depth: Best planted at a depth of 12 to 36 inches (30-90 cm) in still or slow-moving water.
  • Soil Type: Prefers heavy clay or loam soils, typically planted in containers submerged in the pond for best results.

Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Enhances the visual appeal of any pond with its vibrant pink flowers and lush green foliage.
  • Wildlife Attraction: Attracts beneficial wildlife such as bees and dragonflies, contributing to a healthy pond ecosystem.
  • Profuse Bloomer: Known for its abundant flowering, ensuring a long-lasting display of color throughout the growing season.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization during the growing season with aquatic plant fertilizer promotes healthy growth and abundant blooming.
  • Pruning: Remove yellowing leaves and spent flowers to encourage new growth and maintain plant health.
  • Winter Care: As a tropical water lily, it requires special care in colder climates. In regions with cold winters, it is best to lift the plant and store it in a warm, frost-free area until spring.

Conclusion:

Nymphaea 'Golden West' is an exceptional tropical water lily that brings vibrant color and elegance to any pond or water garden. Its stunning autumn-pink flowers, distinctive foliage, and ease of care make it an excellent choice for enhancing your aquatic landscape. Add the enchanting Nymphaea 'Golden West' to your pond today and enjoy the continuous beauty it brings to your water garden environment.

  • You will get 1 young and freshly harvested water lily tuber
  • All of our water lilies tubers/rhizomes have been inspected before ship out and guaranteed they are viable and perfect to grow in a proper pond environment

PLANTING INSTRUCTIONS FOR WATER LILY

 

There are two main types of water lilies, tropical and hardy. The differences between tropical and hardy water lilies are a growing habit. Tropical water lilies don't do well in winter. Hardy water lilies, on the other hand, can tolerate the cold. Tropical water lilies tubers must be removed from the pond and stored in moist conditions to come back in the future years. In warm climates, they can remain in the pond.

products.title: Nymphaea Tina | Violet Tropical Water Lily
products.body_html:
🌸 Nymphaea ‘Tina’ – Tropical Day-Blooming Waterlily

Nymphaea ‘Tina’ is one of the most beloved tropical waterlily cultivars, renowned for its deep violet-purple blooms with a distinctive cup-like shape and a sweet, pleasant fragrance. The flowers rise elegantly above the water’s surface, contrasted by light green leaves that display a bronze tint when young.

This free-flowering and viviparous variety not only produces abundant blooms but also creates new plantlets on its leaves, making it an exceptional choice for both beginners and seasoned collectors. Its adaptability to various water depths and pond sizes makes ‘Tina’ a versatile and rewarding addition to any water garden.

🌟 Key Features

  • Striking Blooms: Cup-shaped flowers in rich violet-purple with golden-yellow centers.

  • Unique Foliage: Light green pads, with new leaves emerging in a bronze hue.

  • Fragrance: Produces a delightful, sweet floral scent.

  • Free-Flowering: Blooms profusely throughout the season.

  • Viviparous Growth: Develops plantlets directly on leaves for natural propagation.

  • Adaptable: Suitable for a range of pond sizes and water depths.

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions

  • Lighting: Full sun (6+ hours daily) for maximum flowering.

  • Water Depth: Ideal planting depth is 12–24 inches (measured from soil surface to water surface).

  • Temperature Range: Thrives in warm water between 75–85°F (24–29°C).

  • Fertilization: Use aquatic fertilizer tablets monthly for continuous blooming.

🖼️ Pond & Garden Benefits

  • 💜 Color & Contrast: Adds dramatic violet tones to water features.

  • 🌊 Versatile Use: Grows well in ponds, water gardens, and containers.

  • 🌱 Self-Propagation: Viviparous growth offers natural expansion over time.

📦 What You’ll Receive

You will receive 1 young, freshly harvested waterlily tuber.

⚠️ Disclaimer

All water lilies are shipped bare-root (as tubers) and should be planted immediately upon arrival for best results. Bloom and leaf color, size, and shape may vary slightly depending on lighting, water quality, and plant maturity.

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium or pond plants into the wild.

products.title: Bolbitis Heudelotii Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Bolbitis heudelotii in Vitro Culture® – African Water Fern for Elegant, Low-Light Aquascapes

Bolbitis heudelotii, also known as the African Water Fern, is a graceful and slow-growing aquatic fern admired for its deep green, transparent, and finely divided fronds. Native to tropical Africa, this plant adds a lush, natural look to midground or background aquascapes and is perfect for low-tech setups or nature-style layouts. Its flowing, feather-like foliage sways gently in the current, providing a tranquil, jungle-like aesthetic to any freshwater tank.

Now available in a compact 2.5-inch Vitro Culture® cup, this plant is grown in vitamin-enriched gel using sterile tissue culture technology. Each cup contains healthy, pest-free plantlets that are 100% free from snails, algae, pesticides, and pathogens, making it ideal for shrimp tanks, nano aquariums, and sensitive livestock environments.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Bolbitis heudelotii

  • Common Name: African Water Fern

  • Growth Habit: Rhizome-based fern with soft, pinnate fronds

  • Color: Deep translucent green

  • Vitro Culture® Format: 2.5-inch sterile cup with multiple plantlets

  • Placement: Midground to background or attached to hardscape

  • Growth Rate: Slow

  • Difficulty Level: Easy to moderate – best with stable conditions

💧 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Low to medium (prefers shaded or indirect light)

  • CO₂: Optional, but enhances health and frond growth

  • Substrate: Do not bury rhizome—attach to rocks, driftwood, or decor

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.5 | Temperature: 22–28°C (72–82°F)

  • Fertilization: Occasional liquid fertilization for optimal color and form

🧪 Vitro Culture® Advantages:

  • 100% Free from Snails, Algae, Pesticides, and Pathogens

  • Sterile & Safe: Excellent for shrimp and sensitive fish tanks

  • Long Shelf Life: Can be stored under moderate light until planting

  • Easy to Use: Rinse off gel, divide gently, and attach rhizomes with thread or glue

🐠 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Natural Jungle Look: Adds movement and texture to planted layouts

  • Low-Tech Friendly: Thrives without CO₂ or intense light

  • Hardscape Compatible: Best used on driftwood or lava rock

  • Great for Shrimp Tanks: Provides natural cover and aesthetic appeal

📦 What You’ll Receive:

One 2.5-inch sealed Vitro Culture® cup containing multiple Bolbitis heudelotii plantlets, ready for acclimation and attachment in your aquarium.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Images are for reference only. Actual appearance may vary depending on aquarium conditions. Avoid burying the rhizome—attach only to hardscape for proper growth.

products.title: Myriophyllum Elatinoides Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Myriophyllum elatinoides in Vitro Culture® – Feather-Like Stem Plant for Flowing Aquascapes

Myriophyllum elatinoides is a graceful, fast-growing aquatic stem plant known for its soft, feather-like leaves and dynamic, bushy growth. Native to Central and South America, this plant creates a stunning, flowing effect in midground to background placements and is particularly prized in nature-style and Dutch-style aquascapes for its ability to add texture, contrast, and movement.

Now available in a Vitro Culture® 4-inch sterile bag, this plant is carefully grown in vitamin-enriched gel or liquid using advanced tissue culture techniques. Each portion yields multiple healthy plantlets and is 100% free from snails, algae, pathogens, and pesticides, making it ideal for shrimp tanks, nano aquariums, and clean aquascaping layouts.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Myriophyllum elatinoides

  • Common Name: Parrot Feather or Red-Leaf Milfoil (in its emersed form)

  • Leaf Texture: Soft, feathery, whorled around the stem

  • Coloration: Bright green to reddish tones under high light

  • Vitro Culture® Format: 4-inch sterile bag with multiple plantlets

  • Placement: Midground to background

  • Growth Rate: Fast

  • Difficulty Level: Easy to moderate

💧 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate or root tabs

🧪 Vitro Culture® Advantages:

  • 100% Free from Snails, Algae, Pathogens, and Pesticides

  • Sterile Propagation: Perfect for shrimp-safe and pest-free aquascapes

🐠 Aquascaping Benefits:

One 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag containing multiple Myriophyllum elatinoides plantlets, ready to be acclimated and planted in your aquarium.

products.title: Alternanthera Ficoidea Rainbow
products.body_html:
Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Rainbow' is a vibrant terrarium, vivarium, or bog plant that is sometimes used as an aquatic plant decoration, despite not being a true aquatic species. This plant can be submerged for several months under the right conditions, but it requires periodic emersed growth to restore its energy and continue thriving.

Key Features:

  • Colorful Appearance: Features a stunning array of colors, including shades of red, yellow, and green, adding a rainbow-like effect to any setup.
  • Versatile Habitat: Can be used in terrariums, vivariums, bog gardens, and temporarily in aquariums.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Submerged Growth: Can survive submerged in aquariums for several months. After this period, it should be transitioned to emersed growth.
  • Emersed Growth: Requires high humidity and consistently wet soil when grown above water, especially during recovery phases.
  • Lighting: Thrives in moderate to high lighting conditions, which helps maintain its vibrant color palette.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Temporary Aquatic Decoration: Provides an attractive and colorful addition to aquariums for several months, developing roots and sometimes growing a few inches taller during its submerged period.
  • Alternative to Aquatic Plants: Its ability to survive underwater for extended periods makes it a popular alternative for hobbyists seeking unique and colorful decorations.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Periodic Transition: To ensure longevity, alternate the plant between submerged and emersed conditions every few months.
  • High Humidity: When grown emersed, maintain consistently high humidity and keep the soil wet to support recovery and growth.

Conclusion:

Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Rainbow' is a versatile and visually stunning plant that can enhance the beauty of terrariums, vivariums, bog gardens, and aquariums. Its ability to survive underwater for extended periods makes it a unique choice for aquarists looking to add a splash of color to their setups. By alternating between submerged and emersed growth, you can enjoy the vibrant presence of Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Rainbow' in various environments.

Enhance your terrarium, vivarium, or aquarium with the stunning and versatile Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Rainbow', and enjoy its vibrant colors and adaptability.

products.title: Java Moss Coconut Cave
products.body_html:
Java Moss is a fairly undemanding freshwater aquarium moss. It can be left floating or attached to a surface like rock or driftwood by using a fine fishing line or cotton thread. It is adaptable to a wide range of aquarium conditions but it will do best in a cool temperature aquarium, between 20 °C to 25°C and they preferred low to medium light.

products.title: Bucephalandra Skeleton King
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Kishii, commonly known as Bucephalandra Skeleton King, is a rare and highly coveted aquatic plant that brings a touch of majestic elegance to any aquarium. Known for its unique skeletal-like leaf structure and vibrant hues, this plant is a favorite among aquarists aiming to create a stunning and diverse aquascape. Ideal for both beginners and experienced aquarists, Bucephalandra Skeleton King is a must-have for any planted tank.

Key Features of Bucephalandra Kishii (Skeleton King)

  1. Unique Skeletal-Like Leaves: Bucephalandra Kishii features distinctive leaves with a skeletal-like pattern and vibrant colors ranging from deep green to shades of blue and purple. This intricate leaf structure adds a dramatic and eye-catching element to any aquascape.

  2. Compact and Versatile Growth: This plant has a compact growth habit, making it perfect for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a foreground or midground plant, providing depth and texture to your aquarium.

  3. Hardy and Adaptable: Bucephalandra Skeleton King is known for its resilience and adaptability. It thrives in a range of water conditions and requires minimal maintenance, making it suitable for both low-tech and high-tech aquarium setups.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, Bucephalandra Kishii grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. Its natural growth habit makes it an excellent choice for creating biotope aquariums that mimic its native environment.

  5. Easy Propagation: This plant is easily propagated through rhizome division. Simply cut a section of the rhizome with leaves and roots, and attach it to a new location in your aquarium. Each section will quickly establish itself and continue to grow.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Kishii (Skeleton King)

  • Lighting: Low to moderate lighting is ideal for Bucephalandra Skeleton King. While it can tolerate higher light levels, too much light can promote algae growth on its leaves.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in both soft and moderately hard water.
  • Substrate: Bucephalandra Skeleton King often does best when attached to rocks or driftwood, mimicking its natural growing conditions. It can also be planted in the substrate if desired.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer supports healthy growth and vibrant colors. CO2 supplementation can enhance its development but is not strictly necessary.
  • Water Flow: Moderate to strong water flow helps mimic the plant’s natural environment, keeping its leaves clean and free from debris.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Kishii (Skeleton King) to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The unique skeletal-like leaves of Bucephalandra Skeleton King create a stunning visual effect, enhancing the beauty of any aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides natural hiding spots and surfaces for biofilm, benefiting fish and invertebrates.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Like other aquatic plants, Bucephalandra Skeleton King helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water.

Bucephalandra Kishii (Skeleton King) is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.

products.title: Hornwort
products.body_html:
🌿 Ceratophyllum demersum – Hardy, Fast-Growing Floating or Rootless Plant

Sold as loose fresh-cut plants: 2–3 stems without root

Ceratophyllum demersum, commonly known as Hornwort or Coontail, is a hardy and versatile aquarium plant prized for its rapid growth and ability to thrive in a wide range of conditions. Naturally rootless, this species floats freely or anchors itself by entangling in substrate or decor. Its feathery, deep green foliage provides excellent shelter for fish and shrimp while helping to absorb excess nutrients and reduce algae growth.

Whether you're looking to establish a lush background, add floating plant cover, or control algae, Hornwort is an easy and effective solution for both beginners and experienced aquarists.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Ceratophyllum demersum

  • Synonyms: Coontail

  • Common Names: Hornwort, Coontail

  • No Root System Needed: Floats freely or can be gently tucked into substrate

  • Algae-Reducing Properties: Absorbs excess nutrients and releases allelopathic chemicals that inhibit algae

  • Low Maintenance: Thrives in a wide range of lighting and water parameters

  • Great for Breeding Tanks: Provides shelter for fry and shrimp

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Low to high lighting

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Not required but will promote faster growth in high-tech tanks

  • Growth Method: Floating or anchored (non-rooted)

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: No

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • 🌾 Adds vertical depth and texture in background or floating setups

  • 🛡️ Ideal as a natural refuge for fry and small fish

  • 🌿 Excellent natural nutrient absorber for algae control

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose fresh-cut plants:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual, loose-cut stems without root

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Hornwort is a delicate, rootless plant and may shed some needles during shipping or after introduction. This is normal and it will regrow quickly under stable conditions.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Please check your local and state regulations before purchasing, as Hornwort is considered invasive in certain regions and may be restricted or prohibited. Never release aquarium plants into natural waterways.

featured_image.alt: Ceratophyllum demersum, commonly known as Hornwort, is a popular and versatile aquatic plant that enhances the beauty and health of any aquarium. Known for its hardy nature and rapid growth, Hornwort is an excellent choice for both beginner and experienced aquarists.

products.title: Limnophila Aquatica
products.body_html:
Limnophila aquatica – Vibrant and Fast-Growing Stem Plant for Lush Aquascapes

Limnophila aquatica, commonly known as Giant Ambulia, is a striking and fast-growing aquatic plant prized for its delicate, feather-like leaves and vibrant green coloration. Ideal for adding depth, volume, and dynamic movement to freshwater aquascapes, this stunning stem plant thrives in well-lit, nutrient-rich environments. Whether you're a beginner starting your first planted tank or an expert designing an elaborate nature aquarium, Limnophila aquatica brings lush, dense beauty with ease.

Key Features, Benefits, and Growing Tips:

🌿 Feathery, Vibrant Green Leaves:
Creates a lush, full look with fine, soft foliage that sways gracefully in water currents.

🚀 Fast Growth Rate:
Quickly fills out the midground or background, perfect for establishing a thriving aquascape.

💧 Ideal for High-Tech Setups:
Thrives under strong lighting, CO₂ injection, and consistent fertilization, though adaptable to medium-tech tanks with care.

🌱 Easy Propagation:
Simply trim healthy stems and replant directly into nutrient-rich substrate for rapid expansion.

🐠 Enhances Aquatic Habitats:
Provides natural shelter for shrimp and small fish, helping to create a balanced, stress-free environment.

🧑🌾 Perfect For:
Dutch-style aquascapes, jungle-style tanks, community tanks, and aquarists seeking to add volume and movement.

Why Choose Limnophila aquatica for Your Aquarium?

Aquascapers seeking lush greenery and natural flow will find Limnophila aquatica the perfect addition to their planted tank. Its fast growth rate allows for quick tank establishment and vibrant displays, while its soft, flowing leaves provide crucial shelter and breeding spaces for shrimp and small fish. This plant also absorbs excess nutrients efficiently, helping to stabilize water quality and reduce algae outbreaks.

Although it thrives best with strong lighting and CO₂ supplementation, Limnophila aquatica can adapt to moderate conditions, making it a versatile choice for both ambitious hobbyists and beginners ready to explore high-growth aquascaping. Regular trimming not only promotes bushier, more compact growth but also allows easy propagation to fill larger spaces beautifully.

Bring life, motion, and dense greenery to your aquarium with the refreshing beauty of Limnophila aquatica.

Product Specifications:

  • Scientific Name: Limnophila aquatica

  • Common Name: Giant Ambulia

  • Position: Midground to Background

  • Light Requirements: Medium to High

  • CO₂ Requirement: Recommended for optimal growth

  • Growth Rate: Fast

  • Difficulty: Easy to Moderate

  • Temperature Range: 72°F–82°F (22°C–28°C)

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in convenient formats:

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.

products.title: Pogostemon Helferi

products.title: Ceratopteris Siliquosa Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Ceratopteris Siliquosa, commonly known as Water Sprite, is a highly versatile and fast-growing aquatic plant that can transform any aquarium into a lush, green oasis. Known for its delicate, fern-like leaves and easy care requirements, Water Sprite is an excellent choice for both beginner and experienced aquarists. This plant can be used as a floating plant, a midground anchor, or as a background plant, offering flexibility in aquascaping designs.

Key Features of Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite)

  1. Delicate, Fern-Like Leaves: Water Sprite boasts finely divided, lace-like leaves that create a beautiful and airy appearance. The light green foliage adds a refreshing and natural look to any aquarium, making it an attractive focal point.

  2. Versatile Growth Options: This plant can be grown either floating on the water surface or anchored in the substrate. Its adaptability allows for creative aquascaping, whether you want a floating canopy or a rooted background plant.

  3. Fast Growth Rate: Ceratopteris Siliquosa is known for its rapid growth, helping to quickly fill in empty spaces in your tank. This fast growth rate also makes it effective at outcompeting algae for nutrients, contributing to a cleaner and healthier aquarium environment.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to tropical regions around the world, Water Sprite thrives in warm, freshwater environments. It naturally grows in shallow waters, making it an ideal choice for replicating tropical biotope aquariums.

  5. Easy Propagation: Propagating Water Sprite is simple and straightforward. New plantlets form along the leaves and can be separated and replanted. This makes it easy to expand your collection and maintain a lush, green aquarium.

Care Tips for Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite)

  • Lighting: Moderate to high lighting is ideal for Water Sprite. Adequate light supports its rapid growth and helps maintain its vibrant green color.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-82°F (20-28°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in a variety of water conditions, making it highly adaptable.
  • Substrate: While Water Sprite can be grown floating, it can also be anchored in the substrate. A nutrient-rich substrate will support its growth, but it can also absorb nutrients directly from the water.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer will promote healthy growth and vibrant foliage. CO2 supplementation is not strictly necessary but can enhance its development.
  • Water Flow: Moderate water flow is ideal for keeping the leaves of Water Sprite clean and free from debris, mimicking its natural environment.

Benefits of Adding Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite) to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The delicate, fern-like leaves of Water Sprite add a stunning visual effect, enhancing the natural beauty of any aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides excellent cover and hiding spots for fish and invertebrates, creating a more dynamic and balanced ecosystem.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Water Sprite helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water, reducing the risk of algae outbreaks.

Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite) is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance, fast growth, and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.

  • Sold as a Tissue Culture package with vitamin liquid/gel

products.title: Green Tiger Lotus
products.body_html:
Nymphaea Zenkeri 'Green' also is known as the Green Tiger Lotus. It's a flowering plant of the family Nymphaeaceae that grows in various parts of East Africa. This species is known for its brilliant reddish-purple-tinted heart-shaped leaves. This is the most popular tropical waterlily in the aquarium industry.
The Tiger Lotus is undemanding. It should be planted in a rich-based nutrient substrate and good lighting. When grow floating with strong or natural sunlight, it can bloom with white, fragrant flowers that are open from evening to late morning.

products.title: Aponogeton Longiplumulosus
products.body_html:
Aponogeton Longiplumulosus – Majestic Ruffled Beauty for Large Aquariums

Aponogeton longiplumulosus is one of the most stunning bulb plants available to aquarists, prized for its long, flowing, deeply ruffled leaves that create dramatic movement and texture in the aquarium. Native to Madagascar, this fast-growing species develops large, elegant foliage that makes an outstanding centerpiece or background plant in spacious aquascapes.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Aponogeton longiplumulosus

  • Common Name: Longiplumulosus Bulb Plant

  • Origin: Madagascar

  • Leaf Length: Up to 60 cm (24 inches) or more

  • Color: Bright green to deep green

  • Growth Rate: Fast

  • Light Demand: Medium to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: Optional but beneficial

  • Placement: Midground to background (best suited for larger aquariums)

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Water Temperature: 72–82°F (22–28°C)

  • pH Range: 6.0–7.5

  • Tank Size Recommendation: 20 gallons or larger

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required (primarily feeds from bulb and water column)

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: No

💧 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Creates dramatic movement with long, wavy leaves

  • Excellent focal point for large aquariums

  • Provides natural shelter for fish and shrimp

  • Adds height and texture to nature-style aquascapes

  • Produces an impressive, lush appearance with minimal maintenance

🌼 Propagation & Growth:

Aponogeton longiplumulosus reproduces through bulb division and may occasionally flower in established aquariums. Like many Aponogeton species, it may enter a temporary dormant period after extended growth. During dormancy, growth may slow or leaves may decline. Simply reduce fertilization and maintain stable conditions until new growth emerges from the bulb.

📦 What You'll Receive:

At Aquarium Plants Factory®, we offer two options to suit every aquarist's preference:

  • Sprouted Bulb With Leaf – A healthy bulb that has already sprouted and includes at least one leaf for a guaranteed head start.

  • Bulb Only Without Leaf – A healthy dormant bulb for experienced hobbyists who prefer to grow the plant from its resting stage.

⚠️ Important Note:

Bulbs without leaves are healthy and capable of sprouting under proper aquarium conditions. Sprouting time varies depending on water parameters, lighting, and aquarium stability. If you're new to bulb plants or prefer immediate results, we strongly recommend selecting the Sprouted Bulb With Leaf option.

This description is accurate for Aponogeton longiplumulosus and avoids copying the Ulvaceus listing while maintaining the same APF format.

products.body_html:
Helanthium bolivianum 'Quadricostatus', also known as Echinodorus bolivianus 'Quadricostatus', is a popular aquatic plant among aquarium enthusiasts. It is a species of the Echinodorus genus and is native to Bolivia, Brazil, and Peru.

The plant is characterized by its long and narrow leaves that grow up to 20 inches in height and have a distinct reddish-brown coloration. Its leaves are arranged in a rosette pattern, and it forms runners that allow it to propagate and spread.

Helanthium bolivianum 'Quadricostatus' is a hardy plant that can thrive in a wide range of water conditions. It prefers moderate to high lighting and a nutrient-rich substrate. It can also tolerate a wide range of water hardness and pH levels.

In aquariums, Helanthium bolivianum 'Quadricostatus' is often used as a foreground or mid-ground plant, where its striking coloration and dense foliage can create a lush and vibrant underwater landscape. It also provides a natural habitat for small fish and invertebrates to hide and forage.

Myriophyllum sp. “Roraima” is a stunning fine-leaf aquarium stem plant prized for its vibrant orange, reddish, and brown tones under proper lighting. Native to the Brazilian region of Roraima, this fast-growing milfoil creates beautiful feathery foliage that sways gently in the water current, giving aquascapes a soft and natural appearance.

With its delicate texture and rapid growth, this species quickly develops into lush bushes when trimmed regularly. It is an excellent plant for filling space in the midground or background of larger aquariums and works especially well as a color accent among green plants.

products.title: Hottonia Palustris Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌿 Hottonia Palustris – Water Violet Vitro Culture Aquarium Plant

Hottonia palustris, commonly known as Water Violet, is a delicate and elegant aquarium stem plant loved by aquascapers for its soft, feathery foliage and unique star-shaped leaf arrangement. Its bright lime-green leaves grow in organized whorls around the stem, creating a beautiful snowflake-like texture that adds depth and contrast to planted aquariums.

This plant is especially popular in Dutch-style aquascapes, where its fine leaf structure contrasts beautifully with broader-leaf plants. As it grows, Hottonia palustris forms dense, bushy clusters that gently sway with water movement, bringing a natural and dynamic look to the aquarium.

Our vitro culture plants are grown in sterile laboratory conditions, ensuring they are 100% pest-free, algae-free, and snail-free, making them perfect for shrimp tanks and carefully maintained planted aquariums.

🌟 Key Features

Common Name: Water Violet
Family: Primulaceae

• Beautiful feathery lime-green foliage with a snowflake-like appearance
• Unique whorled leaf structure that creates soft texture in aquascapes
• Excellent midground or background plant for planted aquariums
• Forms dense, bushy clusters with regular trimming

🌱 Growth & Care

Lighting Needs: Medium to high lighting
CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ recommended for fuller growth but not required
Growth Method: Stem plant propagated by cuttings
Growth Rate: Medium

Nutrient Substrate: Beneficial but not required
Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

With proper lighting and nutrients, Hottonia palustris develops lush bushy groups that can be trimmed and replanted to create fuller aquascapes.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Adds fine, feathery texture to planted aquariums
• Creates soft movement as leaves sway in water flow
• Ideal for midground or background planting in aquascapes

Its bright green coloration and delicate structure make it an excellent contrast plant alongside broader-leaf species or darker green plants.

Healthy Hottonia Palustris Vitro Culture plants grown by Aquarium Plants Factory®, available in convenient pack sizes.

Each 4-inch tissue culture bag contains multiple sterile plantlets ready to be separated and planted.

Available pack options:

All tissue culture plants are lab-grown, pest-free, and safe for shrimp tanks.

products.title: Saururus Cernuus (Lizard's Tail)
products.body_html:
Saururus cernuus – Unique Heartleaf Emerged Plant for Aquariums & Paludariums

Saururus cernuus, commonly known as Lizard’s Tail, is a versatile and visually striking aquatic plant native to North America. Known for its distinctive heart-shaped leaves and elegant upright growth, this plant thrives in high-humidity environments and shallow water conditions, making it an excellent choice for paludariums, ripariums, Wabi-Kusa setups, and transitional zones in aquariums.

This plant is typically offered in its emersed form, allowing it to adapt quickly to a variety of semi-aquatic and marginal aquatic environments. Its foliage can develop a rich, bright green color with a graceful arching habit, and in emersed conditions, it may even produce delicate white flower spikes resembling a lizard’s tail.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Saururus cernuus

  • Common Names: Lizard’s Tail, Heartleaf

  • Leaf Shape: Heart-shaped, bright green, lush foliage

  • Growth Form: Upright stem plant with arching stems and broad leaves

  • Light Requirement: Low to medium

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required

  • Temperature Range: 60–82°F (16–28°C)

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.5; soft to moderate hardness

  • Growth Rate: Medium to fast

  • Placement: Ideal for emersed sections in paludariums, ripariums, and marginal aquarium zones

🧪 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives under low to moderate light; high humidity enhances leaf health

  • Temperature: Best between 68–78°F for vigorous growth

  • Substrate: Can grow in wet soil or rooted in rockwool; prefers nutrient-rich base

  • Water Depth: Shallow or marginal; thrives in transitional wet zones

  • Fertilization: General liquid or substrate fertilization helps support lush foliage

💡 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Emersed Appeal: Perfect for paludariums, rimless tanks, and shallow water displays

  • Architectural Form: Adds height, structure, and soft movement to aquascapes

  • Natural Accent: Pairs beautifully with mosses, ferns, and hardscape

  • Seasonal Bloomer: May produce fragrant, white tail-like flowers in emersed setups

🌱 Propagation & Growth:

  • Growth Speed: Moderate to fast

  • Propagation Method: Via stem cuttings or underground rhizome division

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – optimal in emersed form

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Available in three convenient planting formats to suit your aquatic or semi-aquatic design:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Approx. 3–4 loose, fresh-cut stems without roots

  • Bunched Plants: Approx. 3–4 fresh-cut stems bundled with plant weight for easy placement

  • Potted Plants: Approx. 3–4 fresh-cut stems potted in rockwool within a net pot

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Please do not release Saururus or any aquatic plant into natural waterways. Always dispose of trimmings responsibly. While not restricted in the U.S., always follow local guidelines.

🔒 Disclaimer:

Photos shown are sample representations only. Plants may arrive in emersed form and adapt once planted. We do not guarantee plants are free from pests or pesticides unless otherwise specified.

products.title: Azolla Red
products.body_html:
🍃 Azolla Red (Azolla filiculoides) – Vibrant Floating Water Fern

Azolla Red (Azolla filiculoides) is a striking floating water fern known for its deep red to burgundy coloration under bright light. This fast-growing plant forms a dense mat across the water surface, making it an excellent choice for natural filtration, shade, and visual contrast in aquariums, ponds, and water gardens.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Azolla filiculoides
  • Common Names: Red Azolla, Fairy Moss, Water Fern
  • Growth Rate: Fast
  • Coloration: Green to deep red under medium–high lighting
  • Placement: Floating (no substrate required)

🌱 Growth & Care

Azolla Red thrives in calm water with moderate to high lighting. Under strong light and nutrient-rich conditions, the plant develops intense red tones. It does not require CO₂ injection and is beginner-friendly, making it suitable for both aquariums and outdoor ponds.

  • Lighting: Medium to High
  • CO₂: Not required
  • Water Movement: Low (prefers still or gentle flow)
  • Difficulty: Easy

🖼️ Aquascaping & Functional Benefits

  • Provides natural shade, helping reduce algae growth
  • Absorbs excess nutrients, improving water quality
  • Offers shelter for fish, shrimp, and fry
  • Adds vibrant color and texture to floating plant layouts

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as a small 1 × 1 inch floating portion, containing approximately 10+ healthy Azolla Red plants, carefully selected and packed for safe transit.

⚠️ Disclaimer

As a floating plant, Azolla Red grows quickly under favorable conditions and may require routine thinning. Color intensity may vary depending on lighting, nutrients, and environmental conditions.

products.title: Alternanthera Pink Sunset
products.body_html:
🌸 Alternanthera reineckii ‘Pink Sunset’ – A Radiant Mutation of Underwater Elegance

Alternanthera reineckii ‘Pink Sunset’ is a breathtaking new mutation of the beloved Alternanthera reineckii, first introduced to the United States by Aquarium Plants Factory nursery in 2021. At first glance, this hybrid resembles Alternanthera reineckii 'Rosanervig', but its finer stems and elegantly narrower leaves set it apart as a distinct masterpiece of color and form.

Under strong light and nutrient-rich conditions, ‘Pink Sunset’ reveals a stunning gradient of magenta and rose-pink hues that shimmer through the water—making it one of the most striking stem plants for vibrant, artistic aquascapes.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Alternanthera reineckii ‘Pink Sunset’

  • Common Name: Pink Sunset Alternanthera

  • Origin: Cultivar; introduced by Aquarium Plants Factory nursery (U.S.A., 2021)

  • Form & Texture: Upright stem plant with slender, lanceolate leaves featuring glossy pink-to-magenta undersides and greenish tops.

  • Growth Height: 20–40 cm – perfect for midground to background placement.

  • Growth Rate: Moderate – responds well to trimming and replanting.

  • Temperature Tolerance: 20–28°C – ideal for tropical aquariums.

  • Water Conditions: pH 6–7.5 | Soft to moderately hard water.

  • Difficulty Level: Moderate – thrives with consistent lighting and nutrients.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Medium to high light required to maintain vivid pink coloration.

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Supplemental CO₂ and regular fertilization enhance color and density.

  • Propagation: Simply cut and replant healthy stem tops; roots form easily in substrate.

  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate recommended for vigorous root growth.

  • Can Grow Emersed: Yes – produces compact leaves in humid terrarium or paludarium conditions.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds a radiant pop of pink and magenta contrast against green backgrounds.

  • Ideal for creating a vibrant focal point in Dutch or nature-style aquascapes.

  • Complements Ludwigia palustris, Rotala H’ra, and Bucephalandra varieties beautifully.

  • Excellent for colorful mid- to background groupings or accent highlights.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Choose from our selection of fresh, nursery-grown Alternanthera reineckii ‘Pink Sunset’:

  • Loose Fresh-Cut Plants: Approximately 6 unrooted, freshly trimmed stems.

  • Fresh Cut Bunched: About 6 stems, neatly bundled with foam and a plant weight, without root.

  • Potted Plants: Around 6 fresh-cut stems, without root in a net pot with rock wool.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Some initial leaf melt or slight color fading may occur during shipping or acclimation. This is normal—new growth will quickly regain vivid pink hues under stable lighting and nutrient conditions.

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Please check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways.
Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Didiplis Diandra Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Didiplis Diandra, commonly known as Peplis Diandra or Water Hedge, is a delicate and charming stem plant that brings a touch of finesse to any freshwater aquarium. Its fine-textured leaves and vibrant green color make it a favorite among aquarists aiming to create a dense, bushy backdrop or midground cluster.

Key Features:

  • Fine Leaf Texture: Offers a soft, feathery appearance with its small, needle-like leaves that grow densely along its stems.
  • Vibrant Green Hue: The bright green coloration of the leaves adds a fresh, lively contrast to darker greens and reds in the aquarium.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Prefers moderate to high lighting to maintain its dense growth and vibrant color.
  • Water Parameters: Thrives in slightly acidic to neutral pH (6.0-7.5) with temperatures ranging from 22°C to 28°C (72°F to 82°F).

Aquarium and Pond Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds depth and texture to any planted aquarium setup, perfect for creating lush, underwater landscapes.
  • Water Quality: Like many aquatic plants, Didiplis Diandra helps absorb excess nutrients and contaminants, enhancing overall water quality and clarity.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Growth Rate: Fast-growing, which allows for quick filling of spaces but requires regular pruning to maintain shape and promote denser growth.
  • Propagation: Easily propagated by cutting and replanting the stem cuttings, making it an excellent choice for aquarists who enjoy grooming and cultivating their plant arrangements.

Conclusion:

Didiplis Diandra is an excellent choice for both novice and experienced aquarists. Its quick growth rate and beautiful, feathery foliage make it an attractive and functional addition to any aquatic setup. Whether used to create a lush green backdrop or a vibrant midground cluster, Didiplis Diandra enhances both the beauty and the health of your aquarium.

products.title: Myriophyllum Guyana Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Myriophyllum Guyana is a beautiful and versatile aquatic plant that adds elegance to any aquarium. Known for its delicate, feathery foliage, this plant is a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts looking to create lush, green underwater landscapes.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Myriophyllum Guyana
  • Common Name: Myriophyllum Guyana
  • Family: Haloragaceae
  • Leaf Color: Bright green
  • Growth Form: Feathery, bushy

Unique Characteristics

Myriophyllum Guyana is renowned for its finely divided, bright green leaves that create a soft, feathery appearance. This plant’s graceful and bushy growth makes it an excellent choice for adding texture and depth to your aquarium, creating a natural and serene environment.

Ideal Conditions

To ensure optimal growth for Myriophyllum Guyana, provide the following conditions:

  • Light: Moderate to high intensity
  • CO2: Beneficial but not essential
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate preferred
  • Water Temperature: 20-28°C (68-82°F)
  • pH Range: 6.0-7.5

Care and Maintenance

Myriophyllum Guyana is relatively easy to care for, making it suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists:

  • Lighting: Ensure moderate to high light levels to promote healthy growth.
  • CO2 Injection: While not required, CO2 supplementation can enhance growth and overall health.
  • Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its shape and encourages bushier growth.
  • Fertilization: Occasional fertilization supports vibrant growth and health.

Aquascaping Uses

Myriophyllum Guyana is highly versatile and can be used in various ways:

  • Midground Plant: Ideal for adding density and vibrant green color to the midground area.
  • Background Plant: Can be used to create a lush green backdrop.
  • Accent Plant: Its feathery leaves make it an excellent focal point in aquascapes.

The bright green, feathery foliage of Myriophyllum Guyana provides excellent contrast and visual interest, making it a great choice for both Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes.

Propagation

Propagation of Myriophyllum Guyana is simple and effective. This plant propagates through stem cuttings. Simply cut a healthy stem and replant it in the substrate, where it will develop roots and grow into a new plant.

Myriophyllum Guyana is a must-have for any aquarist looking to enhance their aquarium with a graceful and eye-catching plant. Its delicate, feathery leaves and bushy growth make it a standout choice for creating a beautiful, natural underwater landscape.

  • Sustainable growth aquarium plants species

products.title: Cryptocoryne Parva Mini Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Because of its tiny size, Cryptocoryne Parva is considered the smallest Cryptocoryne species. Cryptocoryne Parva Mini, which is even smaller than the renowned Cryptocoryne Parva, is currently the tiniest Cryptocoryne.

Minimalist in size, Cryptocoryne Parva Mini is a nano-sized plant. As a carpet foreground plant, it is green in color and grows very compactly and low to the ground. Rumors have it that Cryptocoryne Parva Mini originated in Indonesia, however, we have not been able to confirm this. Our knowledge of its origin is limited, but we suspect it is a 'lab-made' product, which means it is derived from tissue culture and may have been altered by humans to achieve its amazing size.

Regardless, this is a good plant for creating a carpet appearance in the aquarium.
Due to the plant's tiny size, freshwater shrimp, such as Amano Shrimp and Red Cherry Shrimp, are great for cleaning and eating algae off it.

  • Smallest Cryptocoryne species in the market
  • Sold as a Tissue Culture Plants with vitamin liquid/gel

products.title: Dwarf Baby Tears
products.body_html:
🌿 Dwarf Baby Tears (Micranthemum callitrichoides) – Iconic Foreground Carpet Plant for High-End Aquascapes

Dwarf Baby Tears, also known as HC Cuba, is one of the most sought-after carpet plants in the aquascaping world. With its tiny, vibrant green leaves and dense, creeping growth, HC creates a lush, lawn-like foreground that adds realism, texture, and depth to Nature, Iwagumi, and ADA-inspired aquascapes.

Perfect for nano tanks and high-tech setups, this species rewards aquascapers with one of the most beautiful carpets achievable in planted aquariums.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Micranthemum callitrichoides
  • Common Names: Dwarf Baby Tears, HC Cuba
  • Growth Form: Dense, compact, low-growing carpet
  • Leaf Size: Extremely small, bright green, rounded leaves
  • Placement: Foreground

💧 Ideal Growing Conditions

  • Light: Medium–High to Very High
  • CO₂: Strongly recommended for tight, healthy carpeting
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate required
  • Water Parameters: pH 5.5–7.5 | 68–82°F (20–28°C)
  • Propagation: Spreads through runners and stolons

Regular trimming encourages a thicker, more uniform carpet.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Select the format that best fits your aquascape:

  • 🌱 Fresh-cut Plants
    Small loose clusters of HC (approx. 1"x1"), ideal for immediate planting and the most efficient way to establish a healthy HC carpet.
  • 🌿 Rooted Bunched Plants
    Healthy stems tied with weight for quick anchoring and faster establishment.
  • 🪴 Rooted Potted Plants
    Fully rooted plants grown in rockwool — the easiest to transition and quickest to carpet.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Creates a true micro-leaf carpeting effect
  • Perfect for foreground in nano tanks and display aquascapes
  • Enhances scale, depth, and natural realism
  • Provides cover for shrimp and small fish fry

⚠️ Disclaimer

Photos are for reference only. Actual plant size, shape, and density may vary based on growth stage and tank conditions. Some initial melting may occur during transition.

products.title: Micranthemum Monte Carlo
products.body_html:
🌿 Micranthemum tweediei 'Monte Carlo' – Lush, Fast-Spreading Carpet Plant

Micranthemum tweediei 'Monte Carlo', commonly known as Monte Carlo, is one of the most popular carpeting plants for planted aquariums. Its small, round, bright green leaves create a dense, vibrant carpet that brings a lush, natural look to any aquascape. Unlike some other carpeting species, Monte Carlo is relatively fast-growing and adaptable, making it an excellent choice for both beginners and experienced aquascapers.

At Aquarium Plants Factory nursery, we cultivate Monte Carlo emersed for stronger growth and easier adaptation to your tank. When submerged with good light and CO₂, this plant will quickly spread across the substrate, creating a beautiful foreground or midground carpet.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Micranthemum tweediei 'Monte Carlo'

  • Common Names: Monte Carlo, MC, New Large Pearl Grass

  • Leaf Shape: Small, round

  • Leaf Color: Bright green

  • Growth Form: Low-growing carpet, creeping runners

  • Placement: Foreground to midground

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Growth Rate: Fast

  • Light Requirement: Medium to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: Strongly recommended for dense carpeting

  • Propagation: Trim runners and replant

Nutrient Substrate: Required
Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🪴 What You’ll Receive

  • Fresh-cut Plants: Loose portion of fresh-cut stems, unrooted, approx. 8+ stems

  • Fresh-cut Bunched: Approx. 8+ stems, bunched with foam and plant weight

  • Fresh-cut Potted: Approx. 8+ stems, potted with net pot and rockwool

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Creates a lush, vibrant green carpet across your substrate

  • Provides natural shelter and foraging areas for shrimp and nano fish

  • Enhances depth and perspective in aquascapes

  • Excellent for Iwagumi and Nature Aquarium styles

⚠️ Disclaimer

products.title: Gratiola Viscidula Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌿 Gratiola viscidula – Unique Thorny-Look Hedge Hyssop for Aquascaping

Gratiola viscidula, commonly known as Hedge Hyssop, is a distinctive North American native that has quickly gained attention in the aquarium hobby for its rare and “thorny” submerged appearance. Naturally found in wetland areas, pond margins, and stream edges, this hardy species is adaptable and can thrive fully submerged for extended periods. Its compact, multi-stemmed form makes it a perfect choice for adding texture and contrast to aquascapes.

When provided with good lighting, Gratiola viscidula can be trimmed low to create a dense, carpet-like effect, or allowed to branch and fill in as a lush midground accent. Its stems, approximately 1–2 cm wide, can reach over 10 cm tall, developing into a dense display of intricate foliage. In nature, it produces charming white tubular flowers with yellow centers during summer and fall.

Now available in our premium Vitro Culture® 4-inch bag, each portion yields many healthy plantlets grown in a sterile environment—100% free of snails, algae, pathogens, and pesticides—making it safe for shrimp tanks and sensitive aquariums.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Gratiola viscidula Pennell

  • Common Names: Hedge Hyssop, Short’s Hedgehyssop, Viscid Hedgehyssop

  • Plant Type: Stem Plant

  • Placement: Midground / Foreground

  • Unique Look: Distinctive “thorny” foliage when submerged

  • Adaptability: Can grow fully submerged for long periods

  • Environmental Benefit: Helps absorb ammonia and nitrogenous waste

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Medium–High

  • CO₂: Medium demand (6–14 mg/L) – Not strictly required but beneficial

  • Growth Method: Stem plant with branching form

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

  • pH Range: 6.0–8.0

  • Growth Rate: Medium

  • Difficulty Level: Beginner

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Creates a rare texture and unique focal point in aquascapes

  • Works well for both low-lying carpets or taller bushy clusters

  • Complements other green and red plants with contrasting form

products.title: Pogostemon Deccanensis Vitro Culture (Pogostemon Erectus)
products.body_html:

Enhance your aquarium with the striking beauty of Pogostemon deccanensis, also known as the Indian Star plant. Formerly mislabeled in the hobby as Pogostemon erectus, this stunning species has since been correctly identified as Pogostemon deccanensis. Now cultivated using our Vitro Culture® method, it arrives completely pest-free, algae-free, and pesticide-free, ensuring a clean and healthy start for any aquascape.

This stem plant is known for its fine, needle-like foliage that resembles conifer branches, creating compact vertical clusters of vibrant green. Its lush, feathery texture adds motion and contrast when planted among broader-leaved or red-hued plants—making it a dynamic choice for both midground and background areas of the aquarium.

  • Scientific Name: Pogostemon deccanensis

  • Synonym: Pogostemon erectus (obsolete)

  • Common Name: Indian Star Plant

  • Leaf Appearance: Fine, needle-like green foliage, dense and bushy

  • Growth Form: Upright vertical stems with compact foliage clusters

  • Placement: Midground to background

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

  • Difficulty Level: Easy to Medium

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Adds vibrant light green contrast to darker or red plant species

products.title: Cryptocoryne Flamingo
products.body_html:
Cryptocoryne Flamingo is a highly sought-after aquatic plant known for its striking pink and white variegated leaves, making it a standout addition to any freshwater aquarium. This unique plant, often referred to simply as "Crypt Flamingo" adds a touch of vibrant color and elegance to aquascapes, enhancing the overall aesthetic appeal.

  • Compact Growth: Grows in a compact form, making it ideal for foreground or midground placement in aquascapes.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Prefers moderate to high lighting conditions to maintain its vibrant coloration.
  • Nutrient-Rich Substrate: Thrives in a nutrient-rich substrate, which supports healthy growth and vibrant leaf color.
  • Water Parameters: Adaptable to a range of water conditions, but prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0 to 7.5) and temperatures between 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C).

  • Versatile Placement: Suitable for both foreground and midground placement, providing flexibility in aquascaping designs.
  • Easy Care: Known for its hardiness, making it suitable for both beginner and experienced aquarists.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Regular Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its shape and encourages new growth.
  • Stable Environment: Ensure consistent water quality and nutrient availability for optimal growth.

Conclusion:

Cryptocoryne Flamingo is an exceptional choice for aquarists looking to add a splash of color and uniqueness to their freshwater aquariums. Its vibrant pink and white variegated leaves and easy care requirements make it a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts. By incorporating Cryptocoryne Flamingo into your aquarium, you can enjoy a stunning and dynamic underwater landscape.

Enhance your aquascape with the exquisite beauty of Cryptocoryne Flamingo and create a captivating aquatic environment that stands out.

products.title: Samolus Parviflorus Red
products.body_html:
Samolus parviflorus 'Red' — A Rare Splash of Red for Your Aquascape

Samolus parviflorus 'Red' is a unique and visually striking aquatic plant prized for its vivid coloration and easy adaptability. Native to the limestone-rich rivers of South China, this rare beauty offers a rosette-style growth pattern and deep red hues when grown submerged—making it an exceptional choice for aquascapers who want to introduce bold, natural tones into their underwater landscapes.

Unlike many "red" plants that require high maintenance, Samolus 'Red' thrives with moderate care, making it accessible for both beginners and experienced hobbyists.

Key Features, Benefits & Growing Tips:

🔴 Stunning Red Coloration:
Emerges green when grown emersed but transitions into brilliant shades of red when fully submerged under moderate to high light.

🌿 Rosette Growth Form:
Creates low, compact clusters perfect for midground accents or foreground highlights in planted aquariums.

💧 Adaptable & Easy to Care For:
Tolerates a wide range of water parameters and grows well with or without CO₂ injection (though CO₂ and strong lighting intensify the red coloration).

✂️ Simple Propagation:
Can be propagated easily by replanting side shoots or separated stems, allowing for fast carpet expansion.

🐠 Perfect For:
Nature aquariums, contrast-driven layouts, biotope-style tanks, and hobbyists wanting a rare, standout species in their collection.

Why Choose Samolus parviflorus 'Red' for Your Planted Aquarium?

Adding depth and vibrant contrast to a planted tank can be challenging—but Samolus parviflorus 'Red' makes it effortless. Its brilliant submerged coloration immediately draws the eye, providing a bold counterbalance to green foliage plants. Hardy, undemanding, and visually captivating, it’s an excellent choice for aquascapers aiming to create a dynamic and colorful aquascape without the intense care often associated with red plants.

Whether you’re crafting a lush nature aquarium, designing a contest-level tank, or simply want something rare and beautiful, Samolus 'Red' is a rewarding and impactful addition.

products.title: Java Fern Narrow Mini
products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus ‘Narrow Mini’ – Ultra-Compact Java Fern with Slender Leaves

Microsorum pteropus ‘Narrow Mini’, also known as Java Fern Narrow Mini, is an exceptionally compact cultivar of the classic Java Fern, featuring slender, upright leaves and a tight, miniature growth form. This rare variety is ideal for aquascapers working with nano tanks, foreground accents, or detailed hardscape compositions where precision and scale are key.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we grow this variety emersed to ensure a healthy and adaptable start. Once introduced to your aquarium, Narrow Mini transitions smoothly into submerged growth. Its thin, upright leaves create a graceful yet unobtrusive visual effect, adding refined texture to your aquascape without overpowering surrounding plants.

As a rhizome plant, Java Fern Narrow Mini should be attached to driftwood, stone, or hardscape, and never buried in substrate. This slow-growing, low-maintenance fern is perfect for low-tech aquariums and makes an excellent choice for aquarists seeking elegance in miniature form.

Echinodorus Cordifolius 'Yellow Sun', also known as Echinodorus Golden Sun or Echinodorus Yellow Sun, is a radiant and eye-catching aquatic plant that brightens any aquarium with its vivid yellow-green foliage. Its heart-shaped leaves, tinged with golden hues, make it a standout feature, offering a fresh and vibrant look to aquascapes. With its dynamic colors and strong growth, this variety is perfect for creating a lush background or serving as a bold centerpiece in larger tanks.

Key Features:

  • Bright, Golden Foliage: The heart-shaped leaves have a striking yellow-green color, sometimes with a hint of light red or orange when grown under higher light, adding a warm and inviting glow to your aquarium.
  • Robust and Adaptable: A resilient plant that adapts to a variety of conditions, Yellow Sun is suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists, thriving in different water parameters.
  • Ideal for Background or Centerpiece: Due to its size and vibrant colors, this plant is best used as a background element in larger aquariums or as a focal point in medium-sized tanks.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Prefers moderate to high lighting, which helps bring out its bright yellow-green coloration and supports healthy growth.
  • Water Parameters: Favors temperatures between 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C) and a pH range of 6.5 to 7.5. A nutrient-rich substrate and regular fertilization are recommended to encourage its lush growth.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Visual Brightness: Its golden-yellow leaves bring a lively and warm touch to aquascapes, providing a striking contrast against green or red plants.
  • Fish Habitat: The broad and dense leaves offer natural shelter for fish, shrimp, and other aquatic creatures, creating a more balanced and secure environment.
  • Water Quality Enhancement: Like other Echinodorus species, the Yellow Sun helps improve water quality by absorbing excess nutrients, reducing algae growth and promoting overall health in the tank.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Moderate Growth: The Yellow Sun grows moderately fast under optimal conditions, and occasional trimming will help maintain its shape and size within your aquascape.
  • Simple Propagation: Can be propagated through side shoots or runners, allowing easy expansion of this vibrant plant throughout your aquarium.

Conclusion:

Echinodorus Cordifolius 'Yellow Sun' is a stunning choice for aquarists looking to add a touch of brightness and elegance to their aquarium. With its distinctive yellow-green leaves and hardy nature, this plant not only enhances the visual appeal of any aquascape but also provides practical benefits for both fish and water quality. Bring the sunny glow of Yellow Sun into your aquarium and transform your aquatic environment into a vibrant and dynamic underwater landscape.

products.title: Bolbitis Heudelotii Difformis
products.body_html:
Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis'

A compact, ruffled twist on the African Water Fern

Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis' is a rare and compact variant of the classic African Water Fern, cherished by aquascapers for its distinctive, finely dissected fronds and slower growth habit. With lush green, feathered foliage and a delicate, curly structure, it brings a graceful, forest-like ambiance to any aquarium setting—especially in shaded or low-flow areas.

This species is epiphytic by nature and thrives when attached to hardscape such as driftwood or porous rock. Its small, wavy-edged leaves and moderate pace make it ideal for nano tanks, low-tech aquascapes, or aquariums with shrimp and delicate fish species.

🌿 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis'

  • Common Name: Mini African Water Fern or Dwarf Bolbitis

  • Growth Form: Rhizomatous epiphyte

  • Leaf Size: Smaller and more dissected than standard Bolbitis heudelotii

  • Placement: Midground to background; best attached to rock or wood

  • Growth Rate: Slow to moderate

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions

  • Lighting: Low to medium light preferred; high light may encourage algae on leaves

  • CO₂: Not required, but enhances growth and coloration

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 5.5 – 7.5

    • Temperature: 68 – 79°F (20 – 26°C)

    • Water Hardness: Soft to moderately hard

  • Flow: Gentle to moderate; avoid high turbulence

🌱 Aquascaping Benefits

  • Creates a lush, natural look with minimal maintenance

  • Ideal for shady or low-tech tanks

  • Provides excellent shelter for shrimp and fry

  • Pairs beautifully with mosses, Anubias, and Bucephalandra

✂️ Propagation & Growth

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required (rhizome should not be buried)

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes, under high humidity

  • Propagation occurs by dividing the rhizome. Simply cut between leaf clusters and reattach to hardscape for new growth to begin.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Available in three formats:

  • Bare-Root Plants: 4+ healthy fronds with rhizome, loose for flexible placement

  • Bunched Plants: 4+ fronds tied with foam and weight for easy setup

  • Potted Plants: 4+ fronds rooted in rockwool and net pot for secure anchoring

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner


Please check your state regulations before purchasing. Bolbitis species are generally unrestricted, but always verify for local compliance.

📢 Disclaimer

As a slow-growing fern, Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis' may take time to adjust and establish in new environments. Avoid burying the rhizome in substrate, as it may rot. Attach with thread or glue to wood or rock for best results.

featured_image.alt: Bolbitis heudelotii difformis is a variation of the Bolbitis heudelotii species that is characterized by its unique frond shapes and compact growth habit. It is a popular choice for aquascaping due to its attractive appearance and ease of care.
Discover the elegant Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis', a rare dwarf variant of the African Water Fern. Ideal for aquascaping with low light and gentle flow setups.

products.title: Mixed Lotus Seeds – Assorted Colors for Ponds, Water Gardens, and Aquascapes
products.body_html:
Mixed Lotus Seeds – Assorted Colors for Ponds, Water Gardens, and Aquascapes

Transform your pond, water garden, or patio container into a vibrant aquatic paradise with our premium Mixed Lotus Seeds (Nelumbo nucifera)!
This curated seed mix features a stunning range of lotus flowers in pink, white, yellow, and multi-colored varieties — perfect for anyone looking to add breathtaking beauty, tranquility, and color to outdoor spaces. Whether you’re a beginner starting your first water garden or a seasoned aquatic plant enthusiast, these seeds make it easy to grow your own personal oasis.

Key Features and Benefits:

🌸 Assorted Color Varieties:
Enjoy a natural mix of pink, white, yellow, and multi-colored lotus blooms for dynamic, eye-catching displays.

🌿 High Sprouting Potential:
Hand-selected seeds with very high sprout rates when properly scarified and soaked before planting.

💧 Versatile Planting Options:
Perfect for ponds, aquascapes, decorative water bowls, or patio container gardens.

🌱 Beginner and Expert Friendly:
Simple four-step germination process: scarify, soak, sprout, and plant in shallow water.

🐠 Aquatic Ecosystem Benefits:
Provides natural shelter for fish and shrimp while helping to stabilize pond water quality.

How to Grow Lotus Seeds (Nelumbo nucifera) – Step-by-Step Guide:

1. Scarify the Seed:
Lotus seeds are oval with one pointed end and one rounded end.
Do not file the pointed (embryo) end.
Instead, gently file the rounded end until you see the white inner layer.
This helps water enter the seed and encourages germination.

2. Soak the Seed:
Place the scarified seed in a container of warm, clean water (around 75–85°F or 24–29°C). Change the water daily to keep it fresh.

3. Sprouting:
Within 1–7 days, the seed will swell and a sprout will emerge. Allow the sprout to grow 4–6 inches before planting.

4. Transfer to Soil:
Plant the sprouted seed into a pot filled with heavy clay soil or aquatic planting media. Submerge the pot under 2–4 inches of water.

5. Growing in Water:
Place the potted seedling in a sunny, warm location. As it matures, the lotus will send leaves above the water's surface and eventually produce stunning blooms.

Read the blog post: A Complete Guide on How to Grow Lotus Flowers from Mixed Lotus Seeds

Important Note:

🌿 Please be aware:
While our lotus seeds (Nelumbo nucifera) are selected for very high sprouting rates, we do not guarantee the flower color or that every seed will successfully grow. Flower coloration may vary naturally. Proper care, growing conditions, and patience are essential for best results.

Product Specifications:

  • Scientific Name: Nelumbo nucifera

  • Common Name: Lotus

  • Product Type: Mixed Lotus Seeds (Hardy and Tropical Varieties)

  • Light Requirement: Full Sun

  • Planting Medium: Heavy clay soil or aquatic planting media

  • Germination Time: 1–7 days after soaking

  • Ideal Use: Outdoor ponds, decorative water gardens, patio water containers

Perfect for ponds, aquascapes, or decorative water features, these seeds are easy to germinate and grow into breathtaking blooms. Each packet includes a natural mix of hardy and tropical lotus seeds, ideal for adding vibrant color, tranquility, and beauty to your outdoor space. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced gardener, growing lotus from seed is

Lotus seeds are incredibly hardy and can remain viable for years when stored properly. Each seed is enclosed in a hard shell that protects it from harsh conditions, but this tough exterior must be prepared correctly before planting to ensure germination.

Nymphaea 'Moonbeam' is a truly unique tropical waterlily that brings a touch of ethereal beauty to any water garden. products.title: Tropical Water Lilies Packages [Grower's Choice]
products.body_html:
Tropical water lilies are a beautiful addition to any pond or water garden, and they come in a wide variety of colors and sizes. When purchasing Tropical Water Lilies Packages [Grower's Choice], you will receive a selection of water lilies chosen by the grower based on availability and the best performers for your growing zone.

The packages typically include a variety of colors, such as pink, red, white, and yellow, and may also include various sizes ranging from small to large. The grower will choose the best selection for you based on the zone that you live in, as well as the specific conditions of your pond or water garden.

Tropical water lilies are called "tropical" because they are native to warmer climates and cannot tolerate cold temperatures. They require full sun and warm water to thrive, making them ideal for warmer regions. Tropical water lilies are also known for their large, showy blooms that can reach up to 12 inches in diameter.

When you receive your Tropical Water Lilies Packages [Grower's Choice], you should follow the instructions that come with them for planting and care. It is essential to make sure that you choose the right location for your water lilies, as they require warm water and at least 6 hours of direct sunlight each day to thrive.

Overall, purchasing a Tropical Water Lilies Packages [Grower's Choice] is an excellent way to add beauty and color to your water garden or pond, and you can trust that the grower has chosen the best selection of water lilies for your growing zone and conditions. However, if you live in a colder region, it is important to note that tropical water lilies may not survive the winter outdoors, and you may need to bring them indoors or replace them with hardy water lilies.

products.title: Micranthemum Monte Carlo Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌿 Micranthemum tweediei 'Monte Carlo' Vitro Culture® – Lush, Fast-Growing Carpet for Vibrant Aquascapes

Enhance your aquarium with the lush, fast-growing beauty of Micranthemum tweediei 'Monte Carlo', one of the most popular modern carpeting plants in the aquascaping world. Commonly known as Monte Carlo, MC, or New Large Pearl Grass, this versatile species forms a dense, bright green carpet that brings a natural meadow-like feel to both small and large planted tanks.

A favorite for Iwagumi layouts, Dutch aquascapes, and nature-style aquariums, Monte Carlo offers a perfect balance of easy growth and high visual impact. Compared to more demanding carpets like Hemianthus callitrichoides (HC), Monte Carlo is much easier to manage, making it an excellent choice for aquarists of all skill levels.

Now available in Vitro Culture® 4-inch bag, this offering is lab-grown under sterile conditions to ensure a pest-free, algae-free start—perfect for sensitive tanks, shrimp aquariums, and pristine aquascapes. 

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Micranthemum tweediei 'Monte Carlo'

  • Common Names: Monte Carlo, MC, New Large Pearl Grass

  • Origin: South America (Argentina)

  • Leaf Characteristics: Small, round to oval bright green leaves on creeping stems

  • Growth Rate: Moderate to fast; forms dense carpet under good conditions

  • Light Demand: Medium to high; promotes compact, low growth

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required but greatly improves density and speed of carpet formation

  • Water Parameters: Adaptable; pH 6.0–7.5

  • Temperature: 20–28°C (68–82°F)

🧪 Vitro Culture® Advantages:

  • Sterile lab-grown in enriched gel medium

  • Free from pests, algae, and pathogens

  • Safe for shrimp tanks and sensitive species—no hitchhikers

  • Sustainably propagated—no wild collection required

🎨 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Creates dense, lush green carpet in foreground

  • Ideal for Iwagumi, Nature, and Dutch style aquascapes

  • Pairs beautifully with hardscape, mosses, and background plants

  • Perfect for nano tanks and large display tanks alike

  • Provides grazing area and shelter for shrimp and microfauna

🌱 Propagation & Growth:

  • Propagation: Easily propagated by cutting and replanting creeping stems

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required — thrives with enriched root zone

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes — excellent for paludariums, terrariums, and Wabi-Kusa setups

📦 What You’ll Receive:

  • Select your preferred pack size: 1 BAG, 10 BAGS, or 20 BAGS of our lab-grown Vitro Culture®.
  • Each sealed 4-inch bag provides approximately 2–3 small clump plantlets, ready to transform your aquascape.

🔒 Disclaimer:

Pictures shown are for reference only. Actual plant size, shape, and color may vary depending on growing conditions. This Vitro Culture® bag is sealed for freshness—please follow proper sterilization and acclimation procedures upon opening.

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner:

Please never release Micranthemum—or any aquatic plant or animal—into local waterways. Responsible disposal helps protect native ecosystems.

featured_image.alt: Micranthemum tweediei 'Monte Carlo' can be compared with the very popular Hemianthus Calitrichoides, in that the leaves look very similar and it carpets very well too. Micranthemum Monte-Carlo leaves are slightly bigger, ranging from 5mm-10mm. It is a bright shade of green and is a wonderful plant for the carpet.

products.title: Nymphaea Islamorada | Purple & White Speckles Tropical Water Lily

products.body_html:
Nymphaea Evelyn Randig is a captivating hybrid water lily cultivar, highly valued for its stunning and fragrant blooms. Developed by hybridizer Martin Randig in the mid-20th century, this cultivar is a cross between Nymphaea 'Mrs. C.W. Thomas' and Nymphaea 'James Brydon', resulting in a unique and beautiful addition to any water garden or pond.

  • Striking Pink Blooms: The flowers of Nymphaea Evelyn Randig are a vibrant pink, featuring pointed and slightly cupped petals. Each flower can grow up to 6 inches in diameter, providing a striking visual display on the water's surface.

  • Lush Green Foliage: The leaves are green, circular, and can grow up to 10 inches in diameter, creating a lush backdrop that enhances the beauty of the flowers.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Water Type: Thrives in still or slow-moving water, making it ideal for ponds and water gardens.
  • Sunlight: Prefers full sun, requiring at least 6 hours of direct sunlight daily for optimal blooming.
  • Water Depth: Best planted at a depth of 12 to 24 inches, ensuring the plant is adequately submerged but not too deep.

Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Enhances the visual appeal of any water garden with its vibrant, fragrant flowers and lush green foliage.
  • Wildlife Attraction: Attracts beneficial wildlife such as bees and dragonflies, contributing to a healthy ecosystem.
  • Easy Maintenance: Once established, this water lily requires minimal care, making it suitable for both novice and experienced gardeners.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization during the growing season with aquatic plant fertilizer promotes healthy growth and abundant blooming.

  • Winter Care: Hardy enough to withstand winter; simply lower the container to the deepest part of the pond to protect it from freezing.

Nymphaea Evelyn Randig is a popular choice for water gardens and ponds due to its attractive and fragrant blooms. Its vibrant pink flowers, lush foliage, and strong fragrance make it a standout feature in any aquatic landscape. Easy to maintain and beneficial for the ecosystem, Nymphaea Evelyn Randig is a delightful addition to any water garden.

Enhance your pond with the exquisite beauty of Nymphaea Evelyn Randig and enjoy the vibrant, aromatic transformation it brings to your water garden environment.

products.title: Nymphaea Hilary | Purple Tropical Water Lily

Care and Maintenance:
