products.title: Myriophyllum Mattogrossense Golden Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Myriophyllum mattogrossense 'Golden' Vitro Culture® – Rare Golden Aquascape Stem Plant

Myriophyllum mattogrossense 'Golden' Vitro Culture® is a rare and elegant whitish variety of Myriophyllum mattogrossense, prized for its delicate feather-like foliage and striking pale coloration. While the standard species displays light green tones, the “Golden” variety develops extremely pale yellow to whitish leaves, creating a dramatic contrast in planted aquariums.

This beautiful stem plant adds softness, texture, and brightness to aquascapes, making it an excellent choice for background placement or large planted layouts where its unique coloration can stand out against darker green plants.

Because of its delicate nature, Myriophyllum “Golden” requires stable and well-maintained aquarium conditions to thrive. It performs best in aquariums with a consistent nutrient supply, adequate carbon availability, and a mature biological environment.

Under strong lighting and optimal conditions, the plant develops very pale golden to almost white foliage with subtle reddish tips, creating a striking visual highlight in planted aquariums. In lower lighting conditions, the leaves may appear more greenish.

This plant grows relatively quickly when provided with proper nutrients and CO₂. As a branching stem plant, it responds well to trimming and can be easily propagated by cutting and replanting healthy stems. Regular pruning helps maintain a dense, attractive plant group while preventing overcrowding.

A mature tank environment is highly recommended for this species. Newly established aquariums often experience unstable nutrient levels and algae outbreaks, and Myriophyllum “Golden” is particularly susceptible to algae. When algae attach to the leaves, the plant may lose its signature pale coloration. Maintaining strong plant growth and good algae control is key to successfully growing this variety.

Grown using Aquarium Plants Factory® Vitro Culture® technology, these plants are cultivated in sterile laboratory conditions and delivered free from algae, pests, snails, and harmful pathogens, providing a clean and healthy start for your aquarium.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Myriophyllum mattogrossense 'Golden'
Common Name: Golden Mattogrossense Watermilfoil
Growth Type: Stem Plant
Placement: Midground / Background

• Beautiful golden-green feathered foliage
• Creates soft flowing texture in aquascapes
• Fast-growing under proper lighting
• Excellent plant for nature-style aquariums
• Ideal for both beginner and advanced aquascapers

🌱 Growth & Propagation

Lighting Needs: Medium to High
CO₂: Not Required (but recommended for faster growth)
Growth Rate: Medium to Fast
Placement: Midground to Background

Myriophyllum mattogrossense grows vertically and produces many side shoots as it matures. Simply trim the stems and replant the cuttings to propagate and create dense plant groups.

This species adapts well to many aquarium conditions and responds beautifully to nutrient-rich water and strong lighting.

Nutrient Substrate: Not Required
Can Be Grown Emersed: No

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Creates soft, flowing texture that contrasts with broad-leaf plants
• Adds bright golden color highlights to planted aquariums
• Perfect for nature aquariums and Dutch-style layouts
• Helps create depth and background layering in aquascapes

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Myriophyllum mattogrossense 'Golden' Vitro Culture® grown in sterile laboratory conditions and sealed in 4-inch tissue culture bags.

Choose your preferred pack size:

Pack 1
1 × 4-inch Vitro Culture® bag containing many small plantlets ready to separate and plant.

Pack 5
5 × 4-inch Vitro Culture® bags, each yielding many small plantlets.

Pack 10
10 × 4-inch Vitro Culture® bags, each producing multiple plantlets for larger aquascaping projects.

Each 4-inch bag contains numerous small plants grown in nutrient gel. Once rinsed and separated, the culture typically yields many individual plantlets ready for planting in your aquarium.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Images shown are for reference only. As a natural product, plant size, shape, and coloration may vary depending on growing conditions and aquarium environment.

Vitro Culture® plants are grown emersed in sterile laboratory conditions. When introduced to an aquarium, the plants may undergo a short transition period as they adapt to submerged growth.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner.

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Nymphaea Daubenyana | Blue Tropical Water Lily
products.body_html:
Nymphaea × daubenyana is an exquisite aquatic plant that brings a touch of elegance to ponds and water gardens. This natural hybrid, originating from the cross between Nymphaea micrantha and Nymphaea nouchali var. caerulea, is notable for its vibrant blue flowers and unique vegetative characteristics.

Key Features:

  • Vibrant Blue Flowers: The star-shaped flowers of Nymphaea × daubenyana are a striking blue, measuring about 10 cm in diameter. The petals are narrow with an acute apex, and the flowers emit a pleasant fragrance, making them a delightful addition to any water garden.
  • Distinctive Foliage: The leaves are cordate to elliptical-roundish, up to 30 cm wide, with a bright green adaxial surface marked by red, and a pale brownish-red abaxial surface. These leaves provide a lush, green backdrop that highlights the vibrant flowers.
  • Tuberous Rhizome: This water lily grows from a tuberous rhizome, which helps it anchor firmly in the pond substrate.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Sunlight: Prefers full sun to partial shade, requiring at least 6 hours of direct sunlight daily to thrive.
  • Water Depth: Best planted at a depth of 12 to 36 inches in still or slow-moving water.
  • Soil Type: Thrives in heavy clay or loam soils, typically planted in containers submerged in the pond for best results.

Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Enhances the visual appeal of any pond with its vibrant blue flowers and lush green foliage.
  • Wildlife Attraction: Attracts beneficial wildlife such as bees and dragonflies, contributing to a healthy pond ecosystem.
  • Cold Tolerance: Despite being a tropical water lily, Nymphaea × daubenyana is relatively cold-tolerant, making it suitable for a variety of climates.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization during the growing season with aquatic plant fertilizer promotes healthy growth and abundant blooming.
  • Pruning: Remove yellowing leaves and spent flowers to encourage new growth and maintain plant health.
  • Winter Care: In cooler climates, it is best to lift the plant and store it in a warm, frost-free area until spring.

Reproduction:

  • Vegetative Reproduction: This water lily reproduces through foliar proliferation, where new plantlets develop on the leaf blade above the attachment point of the petiole. Once the leaf detaches, the plantlets develop fully.

Conclusion:

Nymphaea × daubenyana is a stunning and resilient water lily that brings vibrant color and elegance to any pond or water garden. Its beautiful blue flowers, distinctive foliage, and relative cold tolerance make it an excellent choice for enhancing your aquatic landscape. Add the enchanting Nymphaea × daubenyana to your pond today and enjoy the continuous beauty it brings to your water garden environment.

Hardy water lilies, on the other hand, can tolerate the cold. Tropical water lilies tubers must be removed from the pond and stored in moist conditions to come back in the future years. In warm climates, they can remain in the pond.

products.title: Nymphaea August Koch | Violet-Blue Tropical Water Lily
products.body_html:
Nymphaea August Koch is a hybrid water lily renowned for its large violet-blue flowers and rich history. Named after August Koch, a German water lily hybridizer who, along with George H. Pring, created many new cultivars in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. This tropical lily, introduced in 1922, stands out for its stunning blooms and easy-care requirements, making it a popular choice among water gardeners.

Key Features:

  • Violet-Blue Flowers: Produces large, vibrant violet-blue flowers that are both beautiful and fragrant.
  • Olive Green Pads: Features olive green leaves that provide a lush backdrop for the striking flowers.
  • Historical Significance: Named after August Koch, the hybrid was created in 1922 at Garfield Park, Chicago, Illinois, and is highly viviparous.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Sunlight: Thrives in full sun, requiring at least 6 hours of direct sunlight daily.
  • Water Temperature: Prefers water temperatures between 70°F and 80°F (21°C to 27°C).
  • Planting Depth: Ideal for ponds or containers with still water.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with water lily fertilizer tablets is necessary to ensure healthy growth and blooming. Add tablets to the soil every few weeks during the growing season.
  • Pruning: Remove dead leaves and flowers occasionally to maintain plant health and encourage new growth.
  • Winter Care: In cooler climates, it is best to lift the plant from the container and store the rhizome in damp sand and peat moss at a temperature of 50-55°F (10-12°C).

Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Enhances the visual appeal of any pond or water garden with its stunning violet-blue flowers and lush green foliage.
  • Wildlife Attraction: Attracts pollinators such as bees and butterflies, contributing to a healthy ecosystem.
  • Fragrance: The flowers emit a pleasant fragrance, enhancing the sensory experience of your garden.

Historical and Botanical Notes:

  • Parentage: The parentage is likely Nymphaea ‘Blue Beauty’ x Nymphaea ‘Mrs. Woodrow Wilson’.
  • Creation: Developed by August Koch and George H. Pring, notable water lily hybridizers of their time.

Conclusion:

Nymphaea August Koch is a spectacular hybrid water lily that brings vibrant color and historical charm to any water garden or pond. Its robust nature, striking violet-blue flowers, and ease of care make it an excellent choice for both novice and experienced gardeners. Enhance your aquatic landscape with the exquisite beauty of Nymphaea August Koch and enjoy the vibrant transformation it brings to your environment.

products.title: Micranthemum Glomeratus (Pearl Weed)
products.body_html:
Micranthemum glomeratus – The True Pearl Weed for Vibrant Aquascapes

Micranthemum glomeratus, commonly known as Pearl Weed, Baby Tears, or Pearl Grass, is a delicate and fast-growing stem plant originating from Florida. Previously misidentified as Hemianthus micranthemoides or Micranthemum micranthemoides, this species was correctly identified in 2011, and has since earned popularity among aquascapers for its fine, curved light-green leaves and bushy growth form.

This versatile stem plant adapts beautifully to a variety of aquascaping styles—whether used as a foreground carpet, a dense midground bush, or even as a nano tank background. Its small size and responsive growth habits make it a favorite for trimming, shaping, and layering within complex layouts.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Micranthemum glomeratus

  • Synonyms: Hemianthus micranthemoides, Micranthemum micranthemoides (outdated)

  • Common Names: Pearl Weed, Pearl Grass, Baby Tears

  • Family: Plantaginaceae

  • Leaf Color: Light green

  • Growth Form: Compact, creeping to upright depending on light intensity

  • Growth Rate: Fast

  • Placement: Foreground, midground, or background (nano tanks)

  • Difficulty: Intermediate

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Medium to high (0.5+ watts per liter recommended)

  • Temperature: 68–82°F (20–28°C)

  • pH Range: 5.5–7.5

  • CO₂: Recommended for lush, compact growth

  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate is essential

✂️ Care & Maintenance:

  • Trimming: Regular trimming encourages dense, bushy growth and prevents legginess

  • CO₂ Injection: Promotes horizontal carpeting in high-light setups

  • Fertilization: Occasional macro and micro dosing ensures healthy foliage and fast recovery from pruning

  • Adaptability: Can adapt from upright to creeping form based on light intensity and trimming technique

🪷 Aquascaping Uses:

  • Foreground Carpet: Forms a lush green carpet under strong lighting and CO₂

  • Midground Feature: Easily shaped into tight bushy clumps

  • Nano Tank Background: In small tanks, its tiny leaves make it an attractive background filler

  • Detail Plant: Adds fine texture and contrast in Dutch-style and nature aquariums

🌱 Propagation & Growth:

Micranthemum glomeratus is easily propagated via stem cuttings. Simply trim healthy stems and replant them in the substrate. Roots form readily at the nodes, and new shoots will emerge quickly under proper conditions.

  • Nutrient Substrate: ✅ Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: ✅ Yes (ideal for paludariums and humid terrestrial setups)

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Enhance your aquascape with our premium-quality Micranthemum glomeratus, available in three flexible planting formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Approx. 6+ unrooted, loose fresh-cut stems – flexible for custom layout

  • Fresh-Cut Bunched: Approx. 6+ unrooted stems bundled with foam and plant weight for easy placement

  • Fresh-Cut Potted: Approx. 6+ unrooted stems securely potted in a net pot with rockwool for stable growth

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Images shown are sample representations only. Live plants may vary in size, color, and form based on growing conditions. Unless stated otherwise, plants may arrive in emersed form and are not guaranteed to be pest- or pesticide-free. Proper quarantine is recommended for sensitive setups.

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner:


Never release aquarium plants into natural waterways. Always dispose of trimmings and extras responsibly to protect local ecosystems.

Previously misidentified as Hemianthus micranthemoides (or Micranthemum micranthemoides), it was correctly identified in early 2011. This plant is ideal for creating dense group plantings and can be used in various aquascaping styles.

Micranthemum glomeratus, commonly known as Pearl Weed or Baby Tears, is a delicate stem plant from Florida, known in the hobby for its beautiful, small, light green curved leaves. Previously misidentified as Hemianthus micranthemoides (or Micranthemum micranthemoides), it was correctly identified in early 2011. This plant is ideal for creating dense group plantings and can be used in various aquascaping styles.

products.title: Cyperus Helferi Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌿 Cyperus Helferi – Vitro Culture Aquarium Grass Plant

Cyperus helferi is a graceful grass-like aquatic plant admired for its long, narrow, bright green leaves that gently sway with water movement. Native to Southeast Asia, this elegant species brings natural height and flowing texture to aquascapes, making it an excellent choice for midground to background placement in planted aquariums.

Unlike many typical rosette plants, Cyperus helferi develops tall ribbon-like foliage that creates a natural underwater grass effect. When planted in groups, it forms beautiful clumps that move softly with the aquarium current, adding life and depth to aquascape layouts.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Cyperus helferi
Common Name: Helfer’s Cyperus / Aquarium Umbrella Grass
Family: Cyperaceae

• Elegant grass-like leaves that sway naturally in water flow
• Bright green coloration that complements most aquascapes
• Unique rosette plant with tall flowing foliage
• Ideal for midground to background planting in aquariums

🌱 Growth & Propagation

Lighting Needs: Medium to high lighting
CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ recommended for optimal growth; benefits from nutrient-rich substrate and fertilizers
Growth Method: Rosette plant propagated through runners and division
Growth Rate: Medium

Nutrient Substrate: Recommended
Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

Under stable aquarium conditions, Cyperus helferi develops longer flowing leaves and gradually forms beautiful clusters. As the plant matures, it may produce side shoots that can be separated and replanted.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Creates natural flowing movement in aquascapes
• Excellent plant for adding vertical height and soft texture
• Perfect for midground or background placement in planted tanks

Cyperus helferi is especially popular in nature-style aquascapes, where its long grass-like leaves help create a natural riverbank or meadow effect.

Each 4-inch bag contains multiple healthy plantlets grown in sterile nutrient gel, allowing you to separate and plant many individual plants throughout your aquarium.

Vitro Culture® plants are pest-free, algae-free, and snail-free, making them a safe and clean option for aquariums.

products.title: Mini Pellia / Mini Coral Moss
products.body_html:
Riccardia Chamedryfolia – Rare Mini Moss with Coral-Like Texture

Riccardia chamedryfolia, commonly known as Mini Pellia, Mini Coral Moss, or Mini Liverwort, is a rare and visually stunning addition to any aquascape. Its intricate, branching fronds resemble tiny underwater coral, forming dense, cushion-like growths that bring texture and richness to foregrounds, hardscapes, or vertical moss walls.

Loved by aquascaping professionals and hobbyists alike, this miniature liverwort adds a refined elegance to nano tanks, shrimp aquariums, and planted layouts. Its slow, compact growth and rich green hue make it a standout feature in both high-tech and carefully managed low-tech setups.

🌿 Key Features of Riccardia Chamedryfolia

  • Scientific Name: Riccardia chamedryfolia

  • Common Names: Mini Pellia, Mini Coral Moss, Mini Liverwort

  • Appearance: Dark green, branching fronds resembling fine coral structures

  • Growth Habit: Compact, moss-like cushions with slow, controlled growth

  • Versatile Use: Ideal for attaching to rocks, driftwood, mesh, or hardscape elements

✅ Optimal Growing Conditions

  • Lighting: Moderate to high recommended for best coloration and growth

  • CO₂: Strongly recommended for healthy development and dense form

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required but benefits from water column fertilizers

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 6.0–7.4

    • Temperature: 64–75°F (18–24°C)

🐠 Aquascaping Benefits

  • Distinct Visual Texture: Delivers a unique coral-like appearance that contrasts beautifully with finer mosses and broad-leaf plants

  • Microhabitat Creation: Offers safe refuge and grazing surfaces for shrimp, fry, and microfauna

  • High Design Versatility: Can be used in fine detail work or as a statement mat in nano aquariums

  • Stable & Controlled Growth: Low maintenance and doesn’t require frequent trimming

🌱 Why Choose Riccardia Chamedryfolia?

If you’re looking to elevate your aquascape with a rare and elegant texture, Mini Pellia is the perfect plant. Its coral-like structure, dark green tone, and sculptural form make it a designer’s favorite for modern and nature-style aquariums. With a little patience and care, it forms a lasting, natural feature that transforms your layout into a serene underwater haven.

📦 What You’ll Receive

products.body_html:

Cabomba Super Magenta is one of the most striking stem plants available in the aquarium hobby. This rare variant of Cabomba furcata has been carefully selected for its deep purple and neon-magenta coloration, offering a color profile rarely seen in aquatic plants. Unlike typical red Cabomba varieties, this strain displays a true violet tone, creating an extraordinary contrast when paired with vibrant green aquascapes.

Its delicate fan-shaped foliage forms soft, bushy stems that sway gently in the current, producing a lush underwater forest effect. When grown under strong lighting, the upper tips can develop an intense glowing magenta bloom, making it a dramatic focal plant for high-end aquascapes.

Common Names: Purple Cabomba, Magenta Furcata (Rare Variant)

products.title: Limnophila Aromatica
products.body_html:
Limnophila Aromatica, also known as Rice Paddy Herb, is a highly prized aquarium plant known for its aromatic leaves and vibrant green to purple coloration. This versatile plant is perfect for adding texture and color to midground and background areas in your aquarium, making it a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Limnophila Aromatica
  • Common Name: Rice Paddy Herb
  • Family: Plantaginaceae
  • Leaf Color: Green to purple
  • Growth Form: Upright, bushy

Unique Characteristics

Limnophila Aromatica is renowned for its aromatic leaves that emit a pleasant scent when submerged. It originates from Southeast Asia, where it is commonly found in rice paddies and wet areas. This plant transitions from green to purple under high light conditions, creating a stunning visual effect in aquariums.

Ideal Conditions

To ensure the best growth and vibrant color for Limnophila Aromatica, provide the following conditions:

  • Light: Moderate to high intensity
  • CO2: Beneficial for optimal growth
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate preferred
  • Water Temperature: 20-28°C (68-82°F)
  • pH Range: 5.5-7.5

Care and Maintenance

This plant is relatively easy to care for and is suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists:

  • Lighting: Ensure moderate to high light levels to maintain its vibrant coloration.
  • CO2 Injection: Regular CO2 supplementation can enhance growth and color intensity.
  • Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its form and encourages bushier growth.
  • Fertilization: Occasional fertilization supports vibrant growth and overall health.

Aquascaping Uses

Limnophila Aromatica is versatile and can be used in various ways:

  • Midground Plant: Ideal for adding a splash of green to purple in the midground area.
  • Background Plant: Can be used to create a lush, vibrant backdrop.
  • Accent Plant: Its unique color transition makes it an excellent focal point in aquascapes.

The green to purple leaves of Limnophila Aromatica provide excellent contrast and visual interest, making it a great choice for both Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes.

Propagation

Propagation of Limnophila Aromatica is straightforward. This plant propagates through stem cuttings. Simply cut a healthy stem and replant it in the substrate, where it will develop roots and grow into a new plant.

Limnophila Aromatica is a must-have for any aquarist looking to enhance their aquarium with a vibrant and aromatic plant. Its stunning color transition, aromatic leaves, and easy maintenance make it a standout choice for creating a beautiful, natural underwater landscape.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in three convenient formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Potted: About 6 fresh-cut stems without roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.

products.title: Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen is a rare and highly sought-after aquatic plant that brings a touch of regal elegance to any aquarium. Known for its distinctive skeletal-like leaf structure and vibrant hues, this plant is a favorite among aquarists looking to create a unique and stunning aquascape. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced aquarist, Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen is an ideal choice for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of your tank.

Key Features of Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen

  1. Striking Leaf Structure: Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen features uniquely patterned leaves that resemble a skeleton, with vibrant colors ranging from deep green to shades of blue and purple. This intricate leaf structure adds a dramatic and eye-catching element to any aquascape.

  2. Compact and Versatile: This plant has a compact growth habit, making it perfect for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a foreground or midground plant, providing depth and texture to your aquarium.

  3. Hardy and Adaptable: Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen is known for its resilience and adaptability. It thrives in a range of water conditions and requires minimal maintenance, making it suitable for both low-tech and high-tech aquarium setups.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. Its natural growth habit makes it an excellent choice for creating biotope aquariums that mimic its natural environment.

  5. Easy Propagation: This plant is easily propagated through rhizome division. Simply cut a section of the rhizome with leaves and roots, and attach it to a new location in your aquarium. Each section will quickly establish itself and continue to grow.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen

  • Lighting: Low to moderate lighting is ideal for Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen. While it can tolerate higher light levels, too much light can promote algae growth on its leaves.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in both soft and moderately hard water.
  • Substrate: Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen often does best when attached to rocks or driftwood, mimicking its natural growing conditions. It can also be planted in the substrate if desired.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer supports healthy growth and vibrant colors. CO2 supplementation can enhance its development but is not strictly necessary.
  • Water Flow: Moderate to strong water flow helps mimic the plant’s natural environment, keeping its leaves clean and free from debris.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The unique skeletal-like leaves of Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen create a stunning visual effect, enhancing the beauty of any aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides natural hiding spots and surfaces for biofilm, benefiting fish and invertebrates.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Like other aquatic plants, Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water.

Bucephalandra Skeleton Queen is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.

products.title: Cabomba Furcata
products.body_html:
Cabomba Furcata (Red Cabomba), also known as Fanwort or Cabomba Plant, is a striking aquatic plant that adds a vibrant splash of color and texture to freshwater aquariums. With its delicate, feathery leaves and stunning pink, reddish-purple hues, this plant creates a beautiful contrast against green foliage, enhancing the overall aesthetic of your aquascape.

Key Features:

  • Vibrant Red Coloration: Displays eye-catching reddish-purple leaves under optimal lighting, making it a standout feature in any aquarium.
  • Feathery Appearance: The finely divided, fan-like leaves create a soft and elegant look, perfect for background planting or as a midground accent.
  • Rapid Growth: A fast-growing plant that helps absorb excess nutrients, improving water quality and reducing algae growth.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Requires moderate to high lighting to achieve its vibrant red coloration. Stronger lighting enhances its reddish hues.
  • Water Parameters: Thrives in slightly acidic to neutral water with a pH range of 6.0-7.5 and temperatures between 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C).
  • Nutrients: Benefits from nutrient-rich substrate and liquid fertilizers to support healthy growth. CO2 supplementation enhances its color and vitality but is not strictly necessary.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Natural Habitat: Provides shelter and spawning areas for fish and shrimp, promoting a more natural and stress-free environment.
  • Improves Water Quality: Absorbs excess nitrates and other nutrients, helping to maintain a balanced aquarium ecosystem.
  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds height, movement, and a vibrant red focal point to your aquascape, making it ideal for background planting in larger tanks.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Easy to Moderate Care: While relatively undemanding, it thrives best in well-lit setups with a nutrient-rich environment.
  • Pruning: Trim regularly to maintain shape and encourage bushy, compact growth. Plant cuttings can easily propagate to create new plants.

Conclusion:

Cabomba Furcata (Red Cabomba) is the perfect choice for aquarists looking to elevate their aquarium with a splash of red and a touch of elegance. Its vibrant color, delicate texture, and fast growth make it a versatile and visually stunning addition to freshwater aquascapes. Whether used as a background feature or a midground accent, this plant is sure to enhance the beauty and balance of your underwater world.

Bring home Cabomba Furcata today and transform your aquarium into a lush, vibrant aquatic paradise!

products.title: Bolbitis Difformis
products.body_html:
Bolbitis heteroclita 'Difformis,' also known as Mini Bolbitis or Davallia sp., is a captivating miniature aquatic fern ideal for adding natural elegance to your aquarium or terrarium. Originating from the Philippines, this fern is prized for its delicate, finely divided leaves that create a lush, carpet-like effect.

Appearance

This miniature fern features finely divided, delicate leaves that form a lush, carpet-like cover. Its unique texture and vibrant green color add a touch of natural beauty to any aquascape.

Care Requirements

  • Lighting: Thrives in low to moderate light conditions.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) and a pH range of 5.5-7.5.
  • Substrate: Can be attached to driftwood or rocks, or planted in nutrient-rich substrates.

Propagation

Propagates easily through rhizome division. Simply cut the rhizome and replant the segments to encourage new growth.

Benefits and Placement

products.title: Mayaca Fluviatilis
products.body_html:
🌿 Mayaca fluviatilis – Elegant Soft-Textured Aquarium Stem Plant

Mayaca fluviatilis is the quintessential “soft” plant for the sophisticated aquascape. Known for its fine, needle-like bright green leaves and delicate, flexible stems, this South American native creates a shimmering, moss-like thicket that moves gracefully with water flow. Its gentle texture and vibrant coloration bring a sense of calm, depth, and refinement to planted aquariums.

Whether you are designing a structured Dutch-style street or a more organic Nature Aquarium layout, Mayaca fluviatilis delivers a unique softness that few other stem plants can replicate.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Mayaca fluviatilis
  • Plant Type: Stem plant
  • Color: Bright to yellow-green
  • Leaf Shape: Fine, needle-like foliage
  • Growth Rate: Fast
  • Placement: Midground to background
  • Origin: South & Central America

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting: Low to high
  • CO₂: Not required (recommended for best density and color)
  • Care Level: Beginner-friendly

products.title: Eleocharis Vivipara
products.body_html:
Eleocharis vivipara, commonly known as Giant Hairgrass, Tall Hairgrass, or Umbrella Hairgrass, is a unique and striking aquatic plant ideal for creating dramatic backgrounds in aquariums. Unlike other Eleocharis species, Eleocharis vivipara does not propagate via runners.

Key Features:

  • Tall Growth: Grows tall to the very top of the aquarium, creating a dramatic jungle-style effect.
  • Swaying Motion: The long, slender leaves sway with the water current, adding dynamic movement to your tank.

Propagation:

  • Splitting and Replanting: Can be propagated by splitting the plant and replanting the segments into the substrate.
  • Daughter Plants: The leaf tips often produce baby plants that can be cut off and replanted to encourage further growth.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives in moderate to high lighting conditions, which supports its tall, robust growth.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.5 to 7.5) and temperatures between 68°F to 82°F (20°C to 28°C).

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Ideal for creating lush, tall backgrounds that enhance the overall visual appeal of the aquarium.
  • Unique Growth Pattern: Its ability to grow tall and produce baby plants at the leaf tips adds a unique and natural look to aquascapes.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Easy Propagation: Propagation through splitting and replanting makes it easy to manage and expand your plant collection.
  • Regular Trimming: Regular trimming may be necessary to maintain the desired height and appearance.

Conclusion:

Eleocharis vivipara (Giant Hairgrass, Tall Hairgrass, Umbrella Hairgrass) is an excellent choice for aquarists looking to create a dramatic and lush background in their aquariums. Its tall growth, swaying motion, and unique propagation method make it a standout addition to any planted tank. Whether you're aiming for a jungle-style effect or simply want to add height and movement to your aquascape, Eleocharis vivipara is the perfect plant to achieve your vision.

Enhance your aquarium with the elegant and towering beauty of Eleocharis vivipara and enjoy the dynamic, natural landscape it creates.

products.title: Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Pink'
products.body_html:
Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Pink' is a vibrant terrarium, vivarium, or bog plant that is sometimes used as an aquatic plant decoration, despite not being a true aquatic species. This plant can be submerged for several months under the right conditions, but it requires periodic emersed growth to restore its energy and continue thriving.

Key Features:

  • Striking Appearance: Features vibrant pink leaves that add a splash of color to any setup, whether terrestrial or aquatic.
  • Dual Habitat: Can survive underwater for several months but needs to be grown emersed for a few months to recover and regain strength.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Submerged Growth: Can be submerged in aquariums for up to several months. After this period, it should be transitioned to emersed growth.
  • Emersed Growth: Requires high humidity and wet soil when grown above water, especially during recovery phases.
  • Lighting: Thrives in moderate to high lighting conditions, which helps maintain its vibrant color.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Versatile Use: While not a true aquatic plant, its ability to survive underwater makes it a popular alternative for hobbyists seeking unique decorations.
  • Temporary Aquatic Plant: Provides an attractive and colorful addition to aquariums for several months, developing roots and sometimes growing a few inches taller during its submerged period.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Periodic Transition: To ensure longevity, alternate the plant between submerged and emersed conditions every few months.
  • High Humidity: When grown emersed, maintain consistently high humidity and keep the soil wet to support recovery and growth.

Conclusion:

Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Pink' is a versatile and eye-catching plant that can enhance the beauty of terrariums, vivariums, bog gardens, and aquariums. Its ability to survive underwater for extended periods makes it a unique choice for aquarists looking to add a splash of color to their setups. By alternating between submerged and emersed growth, you can enjoy the vibrant presence of Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Pink' in various environments.

Enhance your terrarium, vivarium, or aquarium with the stunning and versatile Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Pink', and enjoy its vibrant colors and adaptability.

products.title: Rotala Indica (Ammannia Bonsai)
products.body_html:
Rotala Indica 'Bonsai,' also known as the true Rotala Indica, is often confused with Rotala Rotundifolia, a completely different species. Originally introduced as Ammannia sp. 'Bonsai,' its true identity was confirmed by Cavan Allen in 2009.

Distinctive Features: Rotala Indica 'Bonsai' is characterized by its thick, round leaves, with the tips turning a beautiful reddish hue under strong lighting. Unlike Rotala Rotundifolia, which tends to creep under intense light, Rotala Indica 'Bonsai' grows straight and upright, making it a striking addition to any aquascape.

Growth and Care: This plant is highly versatile, thriving in various aquarium settings. It is particularly popular in Street/Dutch-style aquariums and is ideal for small to nano tanks. Rotala Indica 'Bonsai' looks best when planted in small to medium groups, creating a lush and vibrant display.

Aquascaping Uses:

  • Dutch-Style Aquariums: Perfect for creating structured, layered layouts.
  • Nano Tanks: Its compact size makes it an excellent choice for smaller aquariums.
  • Group Planting: Grows beautifully in clusters, adding depth and color to your aquascape.

Enhance your aquarium with the unique beauty and growth habit of Rotala Indica 'Bonsai'. Shop now to add this true Rotala Indica to your collection and elevate your aquascaping designs.

products.title: Subwassertang Moss (Round Pellia)
products.body_html:
Subwassertang Moss – Unique Liverwort for Naturalistic Aquascapes

Subwassertang Moss (Lomariopsis lineata), also known as Round Pellia or Loma Fern, is a captivating, beginner-friendly aquatic plant prized for its translucent, ruffled fronds and gentle, flowing appearance. Though often mistaken for a moss or seaweed, this plant is technically the prothallus (juvenile stage) of a fern. First gaining popularity in Southeast Asia, Subwassertang is now beloved worldwide for its low-maintenance care and versatile aquascaping applications.

Ideal for planted aquariums of all sizes, it attaches easily to wood, rock, or decor, forming lush, bushy clusters over time. Whether used in nano tanks, shrimp setups, or nature-style layouts, Subwassertang brings a soft, forest-floor look and provides valuable shelter for aquatic invertebrates.

🌿 Key Features of Subwassertang Moss

  • Scientific Name: Lomariopsis lineata

  • Common Names: Subwassertang, Round Pellia, Loma Fern

  • Plant Type: Liverwort / Prothallus (fern gametophyte)

  • Leaf Form: Rounded, frilly, translucent green fronds forming dense, tangled mats

  • Epiphytic Nature: Can be tucked or glued onto driftwood, rocks, or decor—eventually self-attaching

  • Ideal for Beginners: Grows in a wide range of water parameters and lighting levels

✅ Optimal Growing Conditions

  • Lighting: Low to medium

  • CO₂: Not required (optional for faster, denser growth)

  • Substrate: Not needed—attach to hardscape or let it float

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 6.0–7.8

    • Temperature: 59–82°F (15–28°C)

🐠 Aquascaping Benefits

  • Natural Look: Adds texture and visual interest with its soft, flowing form

  • Shelter for Shrimp & Fry: Provides hiding spaces and grazing areas for shrimp, baby fish, and microfauna

  • Minimal Maintenance: Slow-growing and resistant to algae—ideal for long-term scapes

  • Floating or Attached: Can be used as a floating plant or fixed to stone, wood, or mesh

🌱 Why Choose Subwassertang Moss?

Unlike traditional mosses, Subwassertang Moss offers a unique visual and textural experience with its rounded, vibrant fronds. It stands out in shaded aquascapes, complements rock and wood layouts, and requires little maintenance, making it a favorite for low-tech and nature-style setups alike. For aquarists who want something different—yet easy—this is the perfect choice.

📦 What You’ll Receive

  • Loose Portion: Includes a small, loose portion of Subwassertang Moss (approx. 2" x 2")

  • Ready to Use: Can be attached directly to your aquascaping elements with glue, thread, or simply tucked into crevices

🔒 Disclaimer

Photos shown are sample representations. Actual portions may vary slightly in size and density. Unless stated otherwise, plants may arrive in either emersed or submerged form and may carry natural variability in color and shape. Not guaranteed to be free of pests or pesticides.

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Never release Subwassertang Moss—or any aquarium plant—into natural waterways. Doing so can disrupt local ecosystems. Dispose of any unwanted portions responsibly to help preserve aquatic biodiversity.

products.title: Hydrocotyle ranunculoides ‘Variegated’
products.body_html:
Hydrocotyle ranunculoides ‘Variegated’ – Floating Pennywort

Hydrocotyle ranunculoides ‘Variegated’ is a stunning aquatic plant admired for its clover-like, scalloped leaves with vibrant green centers and creamy-white margins. The distinctive umbrella-shaped foliage floats gracefully on the water’s surface or creeps along edges, adding a fresh, luminous accent to ponds, aquariums, and container water gardens.

The variegated form offers a refined ornamental look, while its larger clover-shaped leaves resemble those of Hydrocotyle sibthorpioides but are noticeably broader and more striking. Whether floating freely or rooted in shallow margins, it instantly adds a natural, elegant touch to any aquascape.

At Aquarium Plants Factory®, each plant is carefully cultivated for optimal health and vibrant variegation, ensuring that it arrives ready to thrive and beautify your setup with its distinctive charm.

  • Growth Habit: Floating or creeping stems that root at nodes

  • Height: Low-growing, averaging around 6 inches tall

  • Native Range: North America (native aquatic species)

  • Complements lilies, lotus, and other marginal aquatics

products.title: Rotala Indica (Ammannia Bonsai) Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Rotala Indica (Ammannia Bonsai) Vitro Culture® – Compact Stem Plant for Aquascaping

Rotala Indica (Ammannia Bonsai) Vitro Culture® is a beautiful compact stem plant prized for its small rounded leaves, upright growth, and clean aquascaping appearance. Often known in the hobby as Ammannia Bonsai, this plant is excellent for creating structured midground layouts, Dutch-style aquariums, and detailed planted tank designs.

Grown in a sterile 4-inch Vitro Culture® bag, this plant is pest-free, algae-free, and snail-free, making it a clean and reliable choice for freshwater aquariums. Each bag yields many small plants, allowing you to separate and plant multiple portions throughout your aquascape.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Rotala indica
Synonyms: Ammannia sp. ‘Bonsai’, Ammannia Bonsai
Common Name: Rotala Indica, Ammannia Bonsai
Growth Form: Compact stem plant
Placement: Midground / Background
Difficulty: Intermediate

Rotala Indica is known for its slow, compact growth and small rounded leaves that can develop attractive green to reddish tones under strong lighting.

Its upright growth habit makes it ideal for creating neat plant groups, structured aquascapes, and colorful contrast against finer or broader-leaf aquarium plants.

This plant is especially popular in Dutch-style and high-tech planted tanks where controlled trimming and dense grouping are desired.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

Lighting Needs: Medium to High
CO₂ Requirement: Recommended
Growth Rate: Slow to Moderate
Nutrient Substrate: Recommended
Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

For best results, provide strong lighting, CO₂ supplementation, and a nutrient-rich substrate. Under optimal conditions, Rotala Indica can develop more compact growth and reddish coloration near the tips.

Propagation is simple by trimming healthy stems and replanting the cuttings into the substrate. Regular trimming encourages bushier, denser growth.

🧫 Vitro Culture® Advantages

Vitro Culture® plants are grown in a sterile laboratory environment, making them one of the cleanest ways to start or upgrade a planted aquarium.

Benefits include:

Pest-free, snail-free, and algae-free.

Grown in nutrient gel for clean and healthy development.

Easy to separate into many small portions.

Excellent for new aquascapes, shrimp tanks, and high-end planted aquariums.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

Rotala Indica is perfect for creating compact midground bushes, structured stem plant groups, and colorful highlights in planted aquariums.

Its small leaves and upright form make it useful for scale-sensitive aquascapes, nano tanks, Dutch-style layouts, and detailed aquascaping designs.

When planted in groups, it creates a dense, refined appearance that contrasts beautifully with carpeting plants, mosses, and broad-leaf species.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Rotala Indica (Ammannia Bonsai) Vitro Culture® grown and prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®.

Sold in a 4-inch Vitro Culture® bag.

Each bag yields many small plants that can be separated into multiple planting portions.

Plant size, portion count, and density may vary naturally due to growth stage and cultivation conditions.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Vitro Culture® plants are grown in nutrient gel and should be rinsed thoroughly before planting. Gently remove the plant from the bag, rinse away the gel, separate into smaller portions, and plant into the substrate using aquascaping tweezers.

Some melting or transition may occur after planting as the plant adapts to aquarium conditions. With proper lighting, nutrients, and CO₂, healthy new submerged growth should develop.

products.title: Ludwigia Sedioides
products.body_html:
Ludwigia sedioides – The Mesmerizing Mosaic Plant for Ponds & Aquatic Displays

Ludwigia sedioides, also known as the Mosaic Plant, is one of the most visually captivating aquatic plants in the hobby. Its unique geometric leaf arrangement, where diamond-shaped leaves form symmetrical rosettes, creates the illusion of a living mosaic that floats elegantly on the water’s surface. As the plant matures, the outer leaves transition into red or bronze hues, contrasting beautifully with the green center.

Native to Central and South America—especially widespread in countries like Venezuela and Mexico—Ludwigia sedioides thrives in stagnant or slow-moving warm waters and is often found growing in dense colonies in ditches and shallow ponds.

Synonyms: Ludwigia sedoides, Jussiaea sedoides Humb. & Bonpl. (1805), J. sedoides H.B.K. (1823)
Family: Onagraceae
Etymology: Ludwigia honors C. F. Ludwig, while sedoides means "similar to Sedum" due to its rosette leaf pattern.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Common Name: Mosaic Plant

  • Scientific Name: Ludwigia sedioides

  • Leaf Shape: Rosette-like, diamond-shaped leaves (1.5–4 cm long)

  • Coloration: Green with bronze-red margins, more intense under full sun

  • Flowers: Small, solitary yellow flowers (~2 cm diameter) appear on stems

  • Growth Form: Floating with occasional rooting at nodes in shallow substrate

  • Chromosome Number: n = 8

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Full sun to high light (required for red coloration and optimal growth)

  • Temperature: 72–86°F (22–30°C); native habitats reach up to 34°C (93°F)

  • Water Chemistry:

    • pH: 6.3–6.9

    • GH: 2.5–4 dH

    • KH: 2 dH

    • NH₄⁺: ≤0.5 mg/L

    • CO₂: Beneficial, though not always required

  • Substrate: Loamy, nutrient-rich soil if planted; optional for floating setups

  • Water Flow: Prefers still to slow-moving water

  • Winter Survival: Not frost-hardy; overwinter indoors in greenhouse or paludarium

🪷 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Provides a visually striking floating pattern ideal for ponds and water gardens

  • Naturally helps reduce algae by shading the water’s surface

  • Offers cover for fry, amphibians, and surface-dwelling species

  • Bright yellow flowers add seasonal interest during summer growth

🌱 Propagation & Growth:

Ludwigia sedioides is propagated by cutting stems and replanting or floating them. Roots will form at the nodes when allowed to contact substrate. Best planted in shallow, calm areas of a pond or floated where sunlight is abundant. Regular trimming promotes denser rosette formation.

  • Nutrient Substrate: ✅ Required if planting

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: ✅ Yes

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Enhance your aquatic display with our premium cuttings:

  • Loose Fresh-Cut Plants: Approx. 3 unrooted stems of Ludwigia sedioides — ideal for floating or planting in shallow pond margins

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner


Never release this plant into natural waterways. It is fast-growing in warm conditions and may spread aggressively. Check local regulations before purchase.

products.title: Bucephalandra Theia
products.body_html:
🌸 Bucephalandra ‘Theia’ – Luminous Golden Goddess of Borneo

Bucephalandra ‘Theia’ is an exquisite and rare Bucephalandra variety cherished for its warm golden-bronze hues and radiant iridescence. Named after the Greek goddess of light, ‘Theia’ lives up to its name with shimmering leaves that glint in tones of gold, olive, and copper under aquarium lighting. Native to the lush, fast-flowing streams of Borneo, this elegant plant features slender, gently curved leaves and a compact growth form—making it perfect for midground or accent placement. Slow-growing yet striking, ‘Theia’ brings refined warmth and natural brilliance to any aquascape.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bucephalandra sp. ‘Theia’

  • Common Name: Buce Theia

  • Brilliant Coloration: Warm golden to copper-bronze leaves with subtle iridescent highlights.

  • Leaf Form: Narrow, elongated leaves with soft curves and smooth texture.

  • Growth Form: Compact to medium-sized clumps—ideal for driftwood, stones, or as a midground accent.

  • Hardy & Adaptable: Tolerates a wide range of water parameters and lighting levels.

  • Epiphytic Nature: Easily attaches to hardscape—keep the rhizome above substrate.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium light; stronger lighting enhances golden and copper tones.

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Optional, but recommended for faster growth and richer coloration.

  • Growth Method: Rhizome-based; the rhizome must remain above substrate to prevent rot.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required—absorbs nutrients from the water column.

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – excellent for paludariums and humid terrarium conditions.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds soft golden warmth that beautifully contrasts with green or dark Bucephalandra varieties.

  • Perfect for highlighting driftwood or rock features in aquascapes.

  • Compact yet radiant—ideal for nano tanks or as a focal midground accent.

  • Complements both naturalistic and artistic aquascape layouts.

📍 Cultivation & Sustainability

At Aquarium Plants Factory’s nursery, all of our Bucephalandra species are 100% farm-raised—never taken from the wild. Each plant originates ex-vitro (grown from sterile lab culture) before being transitioned to hydroponic growth in our controlled environment. This ensures exceptional quality, pest-free plants, and sustainable preservation of Borneo’s native ecosystems.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as loose Bare-Root or Potted plants:

  • 1 Bare-Root: Approximately 5+ leaves

  • 5 Bare-Root: Approximately 25+ leaves total

  • 10 Bare-Root: Approximately 50+ leaves total

  • 1 Potted: Approximately 10+ leaves

  • 5 Potted: Approximately 50+ leaves total

  • 10 Potted: Approximately 100+ leaves total

⚠️ Disclaimer

Like most Bucephalandra species, minor leaf melt may occur after introduction as the plant adjusts to new water parameters. This is normal and temporary—new submersed leaves will regrow from the rhizome once the plant stabilizes.

products.title: Nymphaea Director George T. Moore | Violet-Purple Tropical Water Lily
products.body_html:
Nymphaea 'Director George T. Moore' is a stunning tropical water lily known for its captivating violet-purple flowers and robust growth. This aquatic plant is perfect for adding a splash of vibrant color to any pond or water garden.

Key Features:

  • Vibrant Violet-Purple Flowers: Boasts large, star-shaped flowers that are a striking violet-purple, adding a dramatic and elegant touch to your water garden. Each bloom measures 4-6 inches in diameter and is highly fragrant.
  • Lush Foliage: Features broad, rounded green leaves that provide a beautiful contrast to the vivid flowers, enhancing the overall aesthetic of your pond.
  • Prolific Blooming: Blooms profusely from June to frost, ensuring a long-lasting display of color throughout the growing season.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Sunlight: Thrives in full sun, requiring at least 6 hours of direct sunlight daily for optimal blooming.
  • Water Depth: Best planted at a depth of 12 to 24 inches in still or slow-moving water.
  • Soil Type: Prefers heavy clay or loam soils. Planting in containers submerged in the pond helps manage its growth and makes seasonal care easier.

Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Enhances the visual appeal of any pond with its vibrant, showy flowers and lush foliage.
  • Wildlife Attraction: Attracts beneficial wildlife such as bees and dragonflies, contributing to a healthy pond ecosystem.
  • Fragrant Blooms: The flowers emit a pleasant fragrance, adding an extra sensory delight to your garden.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization during the growing season with aquatic plant fertilizer promotes healthy growth and abundant blooming.
  • Pruning: Remove yellowing leaves and spent flowers to encourage new growth and maintain plant health.
  • Winter Care: In cooler climates, treat this tropical lily as an annual or store the tubers in a cool, frost-free area until spring. This involves removing the tubers after the first frost, hosing them off, allowing them to dry for several days, and storing them in jars of distilled water or moist sand at temperatures around 50-55°F.

Conclusion:

Nymphaea 'Director George T. Moore' is a magnificent addition to any water garden, offering vibrant color, fragrant blooms, and a long blooming season. Its ease of care and stunning appearance make it a favorite among both novice and experienced gardeners. Add the captivating beauty of Nymphaea 'Director George T. Moore' to your pond today and enjoy the dynamic transformation it brings to your aquatic environment.

  • You will get 1 young and freshly harvested water lily tuber
  • All of our water lilies tubers/rhizomes have been inspected before ship out and guaranteed they are viable and perfect to grow in a proper pond environment

PLANTING INSTRUCTIONS FOR WATER LILY

 

There are two main types of water lilies, tropical and hardy. The differences between tropical and hardy water lilies are a growing habit. Tropical water lilies don't do well in winter. Hardy water lilies, on the other hand, can tolerate the cold. Tropical water lilies tubers must be removed from the pond and stored in moist conditions to come back in the future years. In warm climates, they can remain in the pond.

products.title: Ludwigia Cuba
products.body_html:
🌿 Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata ‘Cuba’ – Cuban Ludwigia

Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata ‘Cuba’, commonly known as Cuban Ludwigia, is one of the most captivating and colorful stem plants in the aquarium world. Originating from the Isla de la Juventud (Isle of Youth) in Cuba, this elegant species is prized for its whorled, narrow leaves and its ability to display a stunning gradient of yellow, orange, and deep red hues under optimal aquarium conditions.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata ‘Cuba’

  • Common Name: Cuban Ludwigia

  • Family: Onagraceae

  • Origin: Isla de la Juventud, Cuba

  • Growth Rate: Fast

  • Difficulty: Medium

✨ Highlight Traits:

  • Brilliant Coloration: Displays vibrant yellow-to-red tones with coppery new growth under strong light and CO₂ supplementation.

  • Fine-Textured Foliage: Narrow leaves arranged in symmetrical whorls create a refined, feathery appearance.

  • Dual Growth Forms: Produces green, oval leaves and yellow blooms in its emersed form, while submerged growth reveals its full red-orange beauty.

  • Perfect for Aquascaping: Provides a stunning focal point for midground to background areas in planted tanks.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Moderate to high (0.5 – 1 watt per liter or equivalent intensity)

  • CO₂ Requirement: Recommended for optimal coloration and growth speed

  • Nutrients: Thrives with balanced macronutrients and trace elements; iron supplementation enhances red tones

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 4.0 – 7.0

    • KH: 0 – 8°dKH

    • GH: 0 – 30°dGH

    • Temperature: 18°C – 30°C (64°F – 86°F)

  • Propagation: Easily propagated through cuttings; replant trimmed tops for best results

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🪴 Tip: Avoid excessive trimming of the base stem—replant healthy tops instead, as the remaining lower portion may deteriorate.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds rich color contrast to green aquascapes, especially in Dutch or Nature-style layouts.

  • The fine-leaved structure softens transitions between plant groupings.

  • Creates a dynamic color gradient when grown in clusters, adding natural visual flow to the aquarium.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as loose fresh-cut plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Fresh Cut Plants: Approximately 6 individual, loose-cut stems without roots

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 6 stems without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 6 stems without roots, potted in rockwool and net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer

Color intensity may vary depending on lighting, CO₂ levels, and nutrient availability. The plant may appear more green upon arrival and will gradually develop red and orange hues under proper aquarium conditions.

products.title: Bacopa Lanigera 'Variegata'
products.body_html:
Bacopa Lanigera 'Variegata': The Stunning Variegated Aquarium Plant

Bacopa Lanigera 'Variegata' is a captivating and unique addition to any aquarium. This variegated variant of Bacopa Lanigera features striking leaves with a mix of green and white, adding a touch of elegance and diversity to your aquatic setup. Perfect for aquarists of all levels, Bacopa Lanigera 'Variegata' is both visually appealing and easy to care for.

Key Features of Bacopa Lanigera 'Variegata'

  1. Variegated Foliage: The standout feature of Bacopa Lanigera 'Variegata' is its beautiful variegated leaves, which display a stunning combination of green and white. This unique coloration creates a striking contrast in your aquarium.

  2. Low Maintenance: Like its green counterpart, Bacopa Lanigera 'Variegata' is hardy and adaptable, thriving in a range of water conditions. Its minimal maintenance requirements make it an excellent choice for both novice and experienced aquarists.

  3. Versatile Placement: Ideal for background or midground placement, Bacopa Lanigera 'Variegata' adds depth and visual interest to your aquarium layout. Its upright growth and variegated leaves make it a standout feature in any aquascape.

  4. Enhances Water Quality: By photosynthesizing, Bacopa Lanigera 'Variegata' helps oxygenate the water, promoting a healthier environment for your aquatic life.

  5. Easy Propagation: Propagating Bacopa Lanigera 'Variegata' is simple and straightforward. Cuttings can be replanted to encourage new growth, allowing you to expand your plant collection effortlessly.

Care Tips for Bacopa Lanigera 'Variegata'

  • Lighting: Moderate to high lighting will help Bacopa Lanigera 'Variegata' maintain its vibrant variegation and overall health.
  • Substrate: While a nutrient-rich substrate can enhance growth, Bacopa Lanigera 'Variegata' can also absorb nutrients from the water column.
  • Water Conditions: This plant thrives in temperatures ranging from 72-82°F (22-28°C) with a pH level between 6.0 and 7.5.
  • Fertilization: Regular use of a balanced aquarium fertilizer will support robust growth and vivid foliage.

Benefits of Adding Bacopa Lanigera 'Variegata' to Your Aquarium

  • Introduces a unique and eye-catching element with its variegated leaves.
  • Provides natural shelter and breeding grounds for fish.
  • Contributes to the overall health of the aquarium by oxygenating the water.

Bacopa Lanigera 'Variegata' is a must-have for aquarists seeking to add a touch of elegance and uniqueness to their aquatic environment. Its distinctive variegated foliage, coupled with its ease of care, makes it a perfect choice for enhancing the beauty and health of any aquarium.

products.title: Ludwigia Tornado
products.body_html:
🌪️ Ludwigia Tornado — Twisted Red Stem Aquarium Plant

Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata ‘Curly’, commonly known as Ludwigia Tornado or Ludwigia Twister, is one of the most visually striking stem plants in the aquarium hobby. Famous for its dramatic spiral-shaped leaves and vibrant reddish coloration, this rare aquatic plant creates intense movement and texture in planted aquariums. Under strong lighting and proper nutrients, Ludwigia Tornado develops tightly curled foliage with stunning shades of orange, red, and bronze that instantly become the centerpiece of any aquascape.

Ideal for experienced aquascapers, this unique Ludwigia species thrives in high-tech planted aquariums with stable CO₂ injection and nutrient-rich conditions.

🌟 Key Features

  • Common Name: Ludwigia Tornado

  • Vibrant red, orange, and bronze coloration under high light

  • Rare collector’s stem plant highly sought after by aquascapers

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Strongly recommended for best coloration and compact growth

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

Ludwigia Tornado propagates easily through stem cuttings. Regular trimming encourages fuller growth and helps maintain its attractive compact shape. Stable water parameters and consistent nutrients are important to preserve its twisted leaf structure and vivid coloration.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Creates dramatic movement and texture with twisted foliage

  • Adds vibrant warm coloration to planted aquariums

  • Excellent contrast against green carpeting and moss species

products.title: Dracaena Sanderiana – Lucky Bamboo
products.body_html:
🎋 Dracaena sanderiana – Lucky Bamboo
Semi-Aquatic Ornamental Plant | Can Grow in Aquariums with Emerged Foliage

Dracaena sanderiana, widely known as Lucky Bamboo, is a beautiful and resilient ornamental plant often used in aquariums, paludariums, and indoor decorative displays. Despite its common name, Lucky Bamboo is not a true bamboo but a member of the Dracaena family. Its smooth green stems and elegant narrow leaves create a clean, minimal aesthetic that pairs beautifully with modern aquascapes and indoor plant arrangements.

One unique feature of Dracaena sanderiana is that the stem can safely grow in aquarium water, making it popular for aquarists who want a plant that bridges both aquatic and terrestrial environments. The stems tolerate full submersion, but the leaves are not adapted for underwater life.

When fully submerged, the leaves will typically decline and melt after a short period of time. For long-term health, the leaves should grow above the water line, where they will continue to develop normally and can thrive permanently in that position. This makes Lucky Bamboo ideal for aquariums where the stem is placed in water while the foliage grows above the tank.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Dracaena sanderiana
Common Name: Lucky Bamboo
Plant Type: Semi-aquatic ornamental houseplant

• Aquarium Compatible Stem: The stem can grow submerged in aquarium water.
• Emergent Growth Habit: Leaves must grow above the water surface for long-term health.
• Elegant Upright Structure: Smooth green stalks with narrow pointed leaves create a clean decorative look.
• Versatile Placement: Perfect for aquariums, paludariums, ripariums, and indoor plant displays.

🌱 Growth & Care

Lighting: Low to moderate light
Water Parameters: Adaptable to most aquarium conditions
Placement: Stem can be placed underwater, but leaves must remain above water
Growth Form: Upright stem plant with emergent foliage

Important:
While the stem tolerates submersion, the leaves will only survive a short time underwater. For permanent growth in an aquarium environment, position the plant so that the foliage grows above the water line.

🪴 Aquarium & Decorative Benefits

• Natural Aquascape Accent: Adds vertical structure and a natural look to aquariums.
• Perfect for Open-Top Tanks: Ideal for aquariums where plants can grow above the water surface.
• Low Maintenance: Hardy and adaptable, making it suitable for beginners and experienced hobbyists alike.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Dracaena sanderiana (Lucky Bamboo) carefully prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®:

Fresh Cut Plant:
• 1 healthy stem cutting approximately 8–12 inches tall with attached leaves.

⚠️ Care Disclaimer

Dracaena sanderiana is commonly used in aquariums, but only the stem should remain submerged.
Leaves are not aquatic and will not survive permanently underwater.

For best long-term results, position the plant so the stem grows in water while the leaves grow above the aquarium.

products.title: Red Root Floater
products.body_html:
🌿 Red Root Floater (Phyllanthus fluitans) – Vibrant Floating Aquarium Plant

Red Root Floater (Phyllanthus fluitans) is a beautiful floating aquatic plant prized for its striking red roots and round floating leaves. Under proper lighting, the leaves can develop attractive reddish tones while the long red roots create a dramatic visual effect beneath the water surface.

This floating plant is popular in planted aquariums, shrimp tanks, and ponds because it adds natural color while helping maintain a healthy aquatic environment. Its roots provide shelter for shrimp and fish fry, while the floating leaves help reduce excess light and absorb nutrients from the water.

Red Root Floater is easy to grow and spreads naturally across the water surface, making it a great choice for both beginner and experienced aquarists.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Phyllanthus fluitans
Common Name: Red Root Floater
Plant Type: Floating Plant

• Attractive floating plant with vibrant red roots
• Leaves can develop red coloration under strong lighting
• Each plant contains multiple leaves, forming a fuller cluster
• Helps absorb excess nutrients and reduce algae growth
• Provides natural shade and shelter for fish, shrimp, and fry

🌱 Growth & Care

Lighting: Medium to High (strong lighting enhances red coloration)
CO₂: Not required
Growth Rate: Moderate to Fast

Placement: Floating on the water surface
Propagation: Natural division as plants multiply across the surface

For best growth and coloration, allow Red Root Floater to float freely in calm water with good lighting. Avoid strong surface agitation, as gentle water movement helps the plant maintain healthy roots and vibrant color.

🖼️ Aquascaping & Aquarium Benefits

• Adds vibrant red color and contrast to planted aquariums
• Helps reduce algae by limiting excess light
• Ideal for shrimp tanks, breeding tanks, and nano aquariums
• Roots provide natural shelter for small fish and shrimp

📦 What You’ll Receive

A small portion (approximately 2” × 2”) of healthy Red Root Floater containing approximately 6–8 plants.

Each plant includes multiple leaves, forming small clusters ready to grow and spread on the water surface.

Plants are carefully harvested and packed to ensure they arrive fresh and ready to add directly to your aquarium or pond.

products.title: Hygrophila Pink Chai (Small Size)
products.body_html:
Hygrophila lancea 'Pink Chai' is a rare and captivating natural hybrid, derived from Hygrophila lancea 'Araguaia' and discovered at South Island Aquarium farm in Singapore. Named 'Chai' after the owner Chai Luan Lim, this extraordinary plant stands out with its vibrant colors and unique appearance, making it a prized addition to any aquarium.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Hygrophila lancea 'Pink Chai'
  • Common Name: Hygrophila sp. Chai
  • Family: Acanthaceae
  • Leaf Color: Neon pink and green hues
  • Growth Form: Elongated leaves

Unique Characteristics

Hygrophila sp. Chai boasts elongated leaves with stunning neon pink and green hues, providing a striking visual element to any aquarium. This stable hybrid ensures baby plants will also exhibit the same vivid pink coloration, although some stems may occasionally revert to the original reddish-brown color of Hygrophila lancea 'Araguaia.'

Care Requirements

To maintain the vibrant colors and healthy growth of Hygrophila lancea 'Pink Chai,' provide the following conditions:

  • Lighting: Moderate to high light
  • Water Parameters: Prefers temperatures between 72-82°F (22-28°C) and a pH range of 6.0-7.5. Sensitive to environmental changes.
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrates are preferred
  • Maintenance: Regular trimming ensures compact growth

Propagation

Hygrophila lancea 'Pink Chai' is easily propagated through stem cuttings. Simply cut and replant healthy stems to encourage new growth.

Benefits and Placement

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds dynamic color and texture to your aquarium
  • Versatility: Suitable for midground and background placement
  • Low Maintenance: Easy to care for with proper conditions

Conclusion

Enhance your aquarium with the rare and vibrant Hygrophila lancea 'Pink Chai.' Its unique appearance, ease of care, and adaptability make it a must-have for any aquascape. Order today and transform your tank into a visually stunning aquatic paradise.

  • Stunning pink, magenta color
  • Sold as young, miniature size Hygrophila Chai
  • Fresh and clean, grow direct from our high technology lab farm

products.title: Limnophila Heterophylla
products.body_html:
Limnophila Heterophylla, a striking yet easy-to-care-for aquatic plant, is a fantastic addition to any freshwater aquarium. Originating from Southeast Asia, it is renowned for its fast growth and feathery, vibrant green leaves, making it a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts.

This plant is highly adaptable, thriving in a range of water conditions and light levels, though it flourishes best in medium to high lighting. Limnophila Heterophylla is also known for its ability to absorb excess nutrients, helping to maintain water quality and reduce algae growth.

Propagation is simple and efficient; just trim the top of the plant and replant the cutting into the substrate. This process allows for easy cultivation and shaping of your underwater garden.

Limnophila Heterophylla is not only aesthetically pleasing but also creates a healthy environment for your fish and invertebrates, providing shelter and enhancing the overall biodiversity of your aquarium.

Whether you're a seasoned aquarist or just starting, Limnophila Heterophylla is an excellent choice for adding beauty and balance to your aquatic setup.

products.title: Dracaena sanderiana 'Variegatus' – Variegated Lucky Bamboo
products.body_html:
🎋 Dracaena sanderiana 'Variegatus' – Variegated Lucky Bamboo

Dracaena sanderiana 'Variegatus', commonly known as Variegated Lucky Bamboo, is an elegant and low-maintenance plant that brings a touch of serenity and natural beauty to any space. Despite its common name, it’s not a true bamboo but a resilient species from the Dracaena family, prized for its striking green leaves edged with creamy white or yellow variegation.

Perfect for both aquariums and decorative indoor arrangements, this plant thrives in water or moist soil, making it ideal for homes, offices, or feng shui displays.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Dracaena sanderiana 'Variegatus'

  • Common Name: Variegated Lucky Bamboo

  • Appearance: Elegant green stalks with glossy leaves featuring creamy white margins

  • Versatile Growth: Can be grown in water, hydroponic setups, or soil

  • Symbolism: Represents luck, prosperity, and positive energy in feng shui culture

  • Low Maintenance: Ideal for beginners and busy plant owners

🌱 Growth & Care

  • Lighting: Prefers bright, indirect light but tolerates low-light conditions

  • Watering: Keep roots submerged in clean, chlorine-free water; change weekly

  • Temperature: Thrives in 65–90°F (18–32°C)

  • Humidity: Moderate to high humidity preferred

  • Substrate: Can grow in soil, pebbles, or water with proper support

  • Fertilization: Use a mild liquid fertilizer monthly during growing season

🖼️ Display Ideas

  • Perfect for modern home decor, office desks, or zen garden arrangements

  • Combine multiple stems in glass vases or decorative pots for a lush, elegant look

  • Ideal for feng shui placement to invite harmony and prosperity

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as fresh-cut, unrooted individual plants:
1 Plant: Approximately 10+ inches tall, ready for water or soil placement.

⚠️ Disclaimer

This plant is shipped fresh-cut and unrooted. Upon arrival, place the stem in clean, chlorine-free water to encourage root development. Avoid direct sunlight to prevent leaf burn.

products.title: Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy
products.body_html:
🌿 Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy – Compact, Wavy-Leaf Aquatic Plant

Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy is a slow-growing, compact aquarium plant distinguished by its beautifully curled, ruffled leaf edges. This hardy selection of the Cardinal Flower has a very bushy underwater growth form, making it ideal for smaller aquariums and precise aquascaping layouts. While its leaf size is similar to the original Lobelia cardinalis, its denser, more compact habit offers a unique texture and structure for your tank.

At Aquarium Plants Factory nursery, we cultivate Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy in its emersed form, where the tops of the leaves display a fresh green hue while the undersides show a striking red-purple color. When grown submerged, the foliage transitions to lush green, with its distinctive wavy edges adding visual interest. This variety is still relatively new and rarely available in the aquarium trade, making it a sought-after choice for collectors and aquascaping enthusiasts.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Unique Wavy Leaves: Curled, ruffled edges create a textured and decorative look.

  • Compact & Bushy Growth: Perfect for small tanks and foreground to midground placement.

  • Color Variation: Green tops with red-purple undersides in emersed growth; transitions to green when submerged.

  • Rare in Trade: A newer Lobelia cardinalis selection not commonly available.

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Medium to high lighting for healthy, compact growth.

  • CO₂ & Nutrients: Benefits from CO₂ injection and balanced fertilization, though not strictly required.

  • Growth Method: Cultivated emersed at Aquarium Plants Factory nursery.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended for strong root development.

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes.

  • Propagation: Easily propagated by cuttings, like other stem plants. Trimming the main shoot encourages multiple side shoots.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds refined texture with curled leaf edges.

  • Works beautifully in dense groupings for Dutch-style layouts.

  • Single, well-ramified plants also make attractive focal points.

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose fresh-cut plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Approximately 3 individual, loose-cut stems without root

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 3 stems without root bundled with foam and a plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 3 stems without root potted in rockwool and net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Minor leaf melting or color transition is normal when adjusting from emersed to submerged growth. Healthy stems will regrow and adapt to their underwater environment.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner
Please do not release Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy or any aquatic plants into natural waterways. Always dispose of trimmings responsibly. Check your local regulations before purchase.

products.title: Flame Moss
products.body_html:
Taxiphyllum sp. Flame (Flame Moss) is a unique and captivating moss that adds an artistic touch to any freshwater aquarium. Renowned for its flame-like growth pattern, where the fronds grow upward in twisting, wavy shapes, this moss creates an eye-catching vertical effect that enhances the aquascape design.

Key Features:

  • Distinctive Growth Pattern: Flame Moss grows vertically with curling fronds resembling dancing flames, offering a stunning visual texture and movement within the tank.
  • Versatile Aquascaping: Perfect for attaching to driftwood, rocks, or any other aquarium decor, making it an excellent choice for aquascaping enthusiasts looking to add a touch of natural elegance.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives under low to moderate lighting conditions, making it suitable for both beginner and advanced aquarists.
  • Water Parameters: Flame Moss prefers temperatures between 68°F to 77°F (20°C to 25°C) and a pH range of 5.0 to 7.5, adapting well to a variety of freshwater setups.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Natural Shelter & Breeding Ground: Provides excellent cover for shrimp, fry, and other small aquatic creatures, serving as a safe haven and breeding ground.
  • Easy Maintenance: Requires minimal care, making it a hassle-free option for those looking to add greenery to their aquariums without extensive upkeep.
  • Nutrient Absorption: Helps improve water quality by absorbing excess nutrients, contributing to a balanced aquatic environment.

Propagation and Care:

  • Simple Propagation: Can be easily propagated by dividing and attaching small sections to new surfaces in the tank.
  • Low Maintenance: Does not require CO2 supplementation and thrives in various conditions, making it a hardy and adaptable moss for all aquascapers.

Conclusion:

Taxiphyllum sp. Flame (Flame Moss) is an ideal moss for aquascaping enthusiasts seeking to add a dynamic and striking vertical element to their aquarium. Its unique flame-like growth pattern, easy care requirements, and benefits to aquatic life make it a popular choice for enhancing both the beauty and health of freshwater aquariums. Add Flame Moss to your aquascape for a visually stunning and low-maintenance addition that will captivate and charm.

  • Perfect to attached to driftwood, rock, or stone
