products.title: Limnophila Hippuridoides
products.body_html:
Limnophila Hippuridoides, an eye-catching stem plant native to the Malayan Peninsula, is a standout in the aquatic gardening world. Often mistaken for its relative, Limnophila aromatica, Limnophila Hippuridoides sets itself apart with its distinctive structure, featuring significantly more than three leaves per whorl. This lush, vibrant plant infuses any aquarium with an exotic flair, making it a top choice for aquascaping professionals and enthusiasts alike.

Renowned for its striking coloration and intricate leaf configuration, Limnophila Hippuridoides is a prized addition to any aquascape. Originating from Southeast Asia, it enhances aquariums with its dynamic and exotic presence, ensuring a visually appealing and lively aquatic environment. This plant's popularity in the aquascaping community underscores its beauty and versatility in creating natural underwater landscapes.

Key Features:

  • Stunning Coloration: Under optimal conditions, Limnophila Hippuridoides develops a rich range of colors, from green to a deep reddish-purple, especially on the undersides of its finely feathered leaves. This makes it a striking focal point in any aquascape.

  • Unique Leaf Structure: The plant’s narrow, elongated leaves are arranged in whorls along the stem, creating a dense, bushy appearance. This structure not only adds visual interest but also provides excellent cover for small fish and invertebrates.

  • Rapid Growth: Limnophila Hippuridoides is known for its fast growth, making it an excellent choice for filling in background spaces quickly. It can reach impressive heights under the right conditions, contributing to a lush, full appearance in the aquarium.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Requires medium to high lighting to bring out its full color potential and encourage robust growth. Under lower light, the plant may lose some of its vibrant coloration and become more green.

  • CO2 and Nutrients: While it can grow without CO2 supplementation, adding CO2 and providing a nutrient-rich substrate or liquid fertilizers will significantly enhance its growth rate and coloration.

  • Water Parameters: Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0 to 7.5) and thrives in water temperatures between 22°C and 28°C.

Aquarium Placement:

  • Background Placement: Due to its height and dense growth habit, Limnophila Hippuridoides is best suited for background placement in the aquarium, where it can create a lush, colorful backdrop.

  • Group Planting: Planting in clusters will maximize its visual impact, creating a vibrant, bushy area that can serve as a contrast to other plants with different textures and colors.

Care Level: Moderate. While it’s generally easy to grow, maintaining its vibrant color and compact growth requires good lighting, CO2 supplementation, and a consistent nutrient supply.

Propagation:

  • Stem Cuttings: Propagating Limnophila Hippuridoides is simple and effective through stem cuttings. Cuttings can be replanted in the substrate, where they will quickly develop roots and grow into new plants.

Conclusion:

Limnophila Hippuridoides is a fantastic choice for aquarists looking to add a splash of color and texture to their aquarium. Its rapid growth, stunning coloration, and ease of care make it a popular choice for creating dynamic and visually appealing aquascapes. Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced aquarist, this plant is sure to enhance the beauty of your underwater garden.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in three convenient formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Includes approximately 6+ loose, fresh-cut stems without roots. Perfect for flexible planting arrangements.
  • Fresh-Cut Bunched: Approximately 6+ fresh-cut stems without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Fresh-Cut Potted: About 6+ fresh-cut stems without roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.

products.title: Pogostemon Sampsonii
products.body_html:
Pogostemon sampsonii is a captivating and rare aquarium plant from southern China, prized for its fine, willow-like foliage and flowing growth habit. Once misidentified as Limnophila punctata 'Blume' and Pogostemon cf. pumilus, this species was accurately classified by aquatic botanist Christel Kasselmann. A member of the Lamiaceae family, Pogostemon sampsonii thrives in both planted aquariums and nature aquascapes, where its soft, delicate texture creates contrast and movement underwater.

Key Features & Benefits

  • 🌿 Elegant Fine-Leaved Structure – Adds soft, flowing texture to the foreground or midground.

  • 💧 Ideal for Aquascaping – Perfect for nature aquariums, Iwagumi layouts, and Dutch-style tanks.

  • 💡 Thrives with CO₂ and Strong Light – Vibrant, lush growth with proper lighting and fertilization.

  • 🐠 Fish and Shrimp Friendly – Provides gentle cover for small aquatic animals.

  • 🌱 Rare Species – A collector's gem recently reclassified and gaining popularity in the hobby.

Transform Your Aquascape with Graceful Movement

Pogostemon sampsonii is ideal for aquarists seeking a plant that combines rarity with visual impact. Its delicate, elongated leaves sway gently in water flow, adding lifelike motion to your fish tank. When cultivated with CO₂ supplementation, nutrient-rich substrate, and high-intensity lighting, this plant forms dense, bushy clusters that enhance the natural feel of your freshwater planted aquarium.

Whether you're a beginner experimenting with aquatic plants or an expert designing a high-end aquascape, Pogostemon sampsonii offers something exceptional—rare lineage, soft texture, and vibrant green coloration that stands out against hardscape or red-leaved background species.

Originally discovered near Belem, Brazil, this stem plant is distinguished by its long, narrow, serrated leaves that are naturally green but can shift into vibrant shades of red to deep purple depending on lighting intensity, CO₂ supplementation, and nutrient availability. Its fine-leafed structure makes it an excellent choice for adding contrast, color variety, and depth to aquascapes.

Cryptocoryne Parva is famous because of its small size and ease of maintenance in the planted aquarium.

products.title: Cryptocoryne Tonkinensis Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Cryptocoryne crispatula 'Tonkinensis', also known as Cryptocoryne tonkinensis, is a beautiful aquatic plant that is highly sought after by aquarium enthusiasts. It is native to Southeast Asia and is commonly found in slow-moving streams and marshes. The plant has long, narrow leaves that can range in color from green to reddish-brown, depending on the lighting and nutrient levels in the water. Cryptocoryne crispatula 'Tonkinensis' is a relatively low-maintenance plant that can adapt to a wide range of water parameters, making it an excellent choice for both beginners and experienced hobbyists.

In addition to its ornamental value, Cryptocoryne crispatula 'Tonkinensis' also provides practical benefits to aquariums. It can help to absorb excess nutrients and provide shelter for aquatic inhabitants, creating a more balanced and healthy aquatic environment. Due to its unique appearance and practical benefits, Cryptocoryne crispatula 'Tonkinensis' has become a popular choice for aquascaping projects and is highly valued among aquatic plant collectors.

products.title: Dwarf Sagittaria Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Sagittaria subulata Vitro Culture® – Versatile Foreground to Midground Grass-Like Plant for Planted Aquariums

Sagittaria subulata, also known as Dwarf Sagittaria, is a resilient, grass-like aquatic plant loved for its versatility and ease of care. Whether you're creating a lush carpet in the foreground or adding soft, vertical movement to the midground, this adaptable species brings structure and vitality to any planted aquarium. In high light, it stays short and compact; in lower light, it grows taller—offering natural layering and dimension to your aquascape.

Cultivated through our sterile Vitro Culture® process, this plant is guaranteed to arrive free from snails, algae, and harmful contaminants—making it the ideal choice for shrimp tanks, high-clarity layouts, and precision aquascaping.

🌿 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Sagittaria subulata

  • Common Name: Dwarf Sagittaria

  • Growth Form: Rosette, grass-like with narrow ribbon leaves

  • Light Demand: Low to medium

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required, but enhances compact growth

  • Growth Rate: Fast

  • Placement: Foreground to midground

  • Leaf Color & Height: Bright green; 2–4 inches depending on lighting

🧫 Vitro Culture® Advantages

  • Sterile & Safe: Cultivated in vitro—completely free of pests, algae, and pesticides

  • Shrimp-Safe: Ideal for sensitive environments

  • Ready to Plant: Simply rinse, separate, and plant directly into your aquascape

🏞 Aquascaping Benefits

  • Layering Versatility: Stays short with intense light, grows taller in shade—excellent for natural transitions

  • Low Maintenance: Spreads through runners for easy propagation

  • Nature-Style Essential: Complements biotope, jungle, and Iwagumi layouts with a flowing, grassy appearance

🌾 Propagation & Growth

Nutrient Substrate:
✅ Required – Performs best with a nutrient-rich substrate or supplemented with root tabs

Can Be Grown Emersed:
✅ Yes – Thrives in high humidity setups like terrariums and paludariums

📦 What You’ll Receive:

One 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag or 2.5-inch cup containing healthy Sagittaria subulata plantlets—carefully grown, ready to rinse, acclimate, and plant.

products.title: Samolus Parviflorus Red Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Samolus parviflorus 'Red' Vitro Culture® – Rare Rosette with Soft Pink-Red Tones

Grown in a 4-inch sealed bag | Yields multiple small plants | 100% Tissue Culture

Samolus parviflorus 'Red', commonly referred to as Red Water Pimpernel, is a delicate and rare rosette plant distinguished by its soft pink to reddish hues and compact growth form. Originating from the wetlands of North and Central America, this plant has become increasingly popular among aquascapers for its gentle coloration and ability to contrast beautifully against bright green carpets or dark driftwood layouts.

This variety of Samolus parviflorus is cultivated through sterile Vitro Culture® technology, ensuring a completely algae-free, pest-free, and pesticide-free start—perfect for sensitive environments such as shrimp tanks and precision aquascapes.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Samolus parviflorus 'Red'

  • Common Name: Red Water Pimpernel

  • Coloration: Pink to reddish leaves, especially under high light and CO₂

  • Growth Form: Compact rosette with rounded, slightly wavy foliage

  • Placement: Ideal for foreground to midground in nano to medium tanks

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Moderate to high for best color development

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Strongly recommended to enhance redness and promote compact growth

  • Growth Method: Submersed

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – great for paludariums and terrarium-style setups

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Introduces soft red and pink hues to contrast against green carpets

  • Perfect for minimalist or nature-style aquascapes

  • Compatible with shrimp and sensitive livestock thanks to sterile culture

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as a Vitro Culture® cup in a sealed 4-inch bag:

  • 4-inch Bag: Contains multiple young plants, ready for propagation and planting

⚠️ Disclaimer:

As with most rosette plants, Samolus parviflorus 'Red' may experience a brief adjustment period (known as “melt”) after planting. Trim away melted portions and provide stable lighting, CO₂, and nutrients to encourage healthy new growth.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner:

Never release Samolus parviflorus—or any aquarium plant—into natural waterways. Doing so can disrupt local ecosystems. Dispose of unwanted plant matter responsibly to help preserve aquatic biodiversity.

products.title: Vallisneria Americana
products.body_html:
🌿 Vallisneria americana 'Asiatica' – Twisting Corkscrew Val for Flowing Foreground & Midground Texture

Vallisneria americana 'Asiatica', also known as Corkscrew Vallisneria or Twisted Val, is a unique and eye-catching variety of Vallisneria with narrow, bright green leaves that twist along their length. Unlike the towering growth of other Vallisneria species, this compact cultivar reaches only 8–12 inches (20–30 cm) in height, making it perfect for midground or even large foreground placements in aquascapes.

Native to North America and widely cultivated in Asia, Vallisneria americana 'Asiatica' forms elegant spirals of foliage that create subtle motion in the water column. When planted in dense groups, the twisting leaves add beautiful texture and contrast to an otherwise linear layout. It’s a hardy, beginner-friendly plant that spreads quickly via runners and adapts to a wide range of tank conditions.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Vallisneria americana 'Asiatica'

  • Synonyms: Twisted Vallisneria, Corkscrew Vallisneria

  • Common Name: Corkscrew Val

  • Twisted Green Leaves: Narrow, spiraled leaves create dynamic visual texture

  • Compact Size: Grows to 8–12 inches, ideal for midground planting

  • Beginner-Friendly: Easy to grow, fast to propagate, and low maintenance

  • Runner Propagation: Quickly forms a thick colony with regular offshoots

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Moderate to high light enhances leaf form and color

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ not required; grows best with root tabs or nutrient substrate

  • Growth Method: Spreads via runners; forms dense spiraled clusters over time

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds movement and texture to midground or foreground zones

  • Provides contrast against broadleaf or red-stemmed species

  • Perfect for softening the transition between low and tall background plants

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose fresh-cut plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Bare-root Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual loose, bare-root plants

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual bare-root plants bundled with foam and a plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual bare-root plants potted in rockwool and net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Leaf twist intensity and coloration may vary slightly depending on lighting and nutrients. Some initial melting is normal upon introduction. Stable conditions promote quick propagation and healthy regrowth.

Always follow your local guidelines and never release aquarium plants into natural water systems. Dispose of plant trimmings responsibly.

products.title: Hygrophila Difformis | Bulk Pack
products.body_html:
🌿 Hygrophila Difformis – Water Wisteria Bulk Pack | Fast-Growing Aquarium Stem Plant

Hygrophila difformis, commonly known as Water Wisteria, is one of the most popular and beginner-friendly aquarium plants in the hobby. This fast-growing aquatic stem plant is prized for its beautiful lace-like, fern-shaped leaves and vibrant green coloration that instantly adds lush density to planted aquariums.

Native to the Indian subcontinent, Water Wisteria is extremely adaptable and can thrive in a wide range of aquarium conditions. Its rapid growth helps absorb excess nutrients from the water column, making it an excellent natural ally for maintaining balanced and algae-controlled aquariums.

This Bulk Pack of Hygrophila Difformis is perfect for aquascapers, breeders, and planted tank enthusiasts who need large quantities of healthy stems to create dense backgrounds, jungle-style aquascapes, or nutrient-absorbing plant mass in aquariums.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Hygrophila difformis
Common Name: Water Wisteria
Plant Type: Freshwater stem plant

• Fast Growing: One of the fastest growing aquarium plants, ideal for stabilizing new tanks.
• Beautiful Leaf Structure: Features delicate, deeply lobed leaves that resemble aquatic ferns.
• Beginner Friendly: Extremely hardy and adaptable to various aquarium conditions.
• Excellent Nutrient Absorber: Helps reduce algae by consuming excess nutrients.
• Versatile Placement: Can be used as a midground or background plant in aquascapes.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

Lighting: Low to High lighting
CO₂ Requirement: Not required, but growth improves with CO₂
Growth Rate: Fast
Placement: Midground to Background
Nutrient Substrate: Not Required
Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

Propagation is extremely simple. Just trim the stems and replant the cuttings into the substrate. Each cutting will quickly develop new roots and continue growing.

Because of its rapid growth, Hygrophila difformis is commonly used in new aquariums to help establish biological balance and improve water quality.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Creates lush jungle aquascapes with dense, feathery foliage
• Great for filling large aquarium spaces quickly
• Provides natural shelter for shrimp, fry, and small fish

Water Wisteria can also develop different leaf shapes depending on whether it grows submerged or emersed, giving aquascapers unique visual variety.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Hygrophila Difformis (Water Wisteria) cultivated and prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®, available in bulk stem packs:

Fresh Cut Plants: Approximately 20 loose fresh-cut stems without roots.
Fresh Cut Plants: Approximately 40 loose fresh-cut stems without roots.

These are fresh cuttings grown above water (emersed) and will quickly adapt and produce new submerged growth once planted in your aquarium.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Live aquatic plants naturally vary in size, shape, and coloration depending on growing conditions and season. Plants may be grown emersed at our nursery and will transition to submerged growth once placed in an aquarium. Some leaf melt during this transition is normal as the plant adapts.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner.

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Anubias Peacock | Bulk Pack
products.body_html:
🌿 Anubias Peacock – Bulk Pack Aquarium Rhizome Plants

Anubias Peacock is a beautiful and distinctive Anubias variety prized for its elegant leaf shape and textured foliage. Its leaves feature slightly elongated tips and a graceful, fan-like arrangement that resembles the elegant spread of a peacock’s tail, giving this plant its unique name.

This hardy rhizome aquarium plant is perfect for aquascapers looking to quickly add structure and lush greenery to their aquariums. The Bulk Pack option (5, 10, or 20 plants) allows hobbyists, aquascapers, and aquarium stores to instantly create dense clusters of Anubias on driftwood, rocks, or decorative hardscape.

Like most Anubias species, Anubias Peacock is extremely adaptable and beginner-friendly, thriving in both low-tech and high-tech aquariums. Its slow growth and durable leaves make it resistant to fish nibbling and suitable for a wide range of freshwater setups.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Anubias barteri “Peacock”
Common Name: Anubias Peacock
Plant Type: Rhizome / Epiphyte

• Elegant elongated leaves arranged in a fan-like pattern
• Extremely hardy and adaptable freshwater aquarium plant
• Perfect for attaching to driftwood, rocks, or aquarium décor
• Ideal for beginners and low-tech aquariums
• Bulk pack allows quick creation of lush aquascapes

🌱 Growth & Propagation

Lighting: Low to Moderate
CO₂: Not required, but beneficial
Growth Rate: Slow
Placement: Foreground to midground when attached to hardscape
Nutrient Substrate: Not Required
Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

Anubias Peacock grows from a thick horizontal rhizome. The rhizome should not be buried in substrate; instead, attach the plant to driftwood or rocks using aquarium glue or thread. Over time, the roots will naturally anchor the plant to the surface.

Propagation is simple—just divide the rhizome once the plant has grown several leaves.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Creates beautiful texture on driftwood and stone layouts
• Perfect for natural aquascapes and jungle-style aquariums
• Excellent for shrimp tanks and community aquariums
• Provides shelter and resting areas for fish and shrimp

📦 What You’ll Receive

Healthy Anubias Peacock plants cultivated by Aquarium Plants Factory®, available in bulk pack sizes:

5 Plants: Loose bare-root plants with approximately 4+ leaves per plant
10 Plants: Loose bare-root plants with approximately 4+ leaves per plant
20 Plants: Loose bare-root plants with approximately 4+ leaves per plant

Plant size may vary depending on the season and growing conditions, but every plant will have at least 4 healthy leaves.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Live aquarium plants may experience minor cosmetic changes during shipping or when transitioning between emersed and submerged growth forms. This is completely normal, and plants typically adapt quickly once placed in proper aquarium conditions.

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

Water Lettuce (Pistia stratiotes) is a classic floating plant known for its beautiful, rosette-shaped leaves that resemble small heads of lettuce. Its soft, velvety foliage and long dangling roots provide excellent cover and natural filtration for ponds and aquariums. Water Lettuce thrives in calm, nutrient-rich waters, helping to shade your aquatic environment and reduce algae growth.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Natural Filtration: Absorbs excess nutrients, improving water quality.

  • Floating Growth: Perfect for ponds, water gardens, and outdoor tubs.

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Velvety green leaves form charming rosettes atop the water surface.

  • Fish & Wildlife Benefits: Provides shade and protection for fish, frogs, and other aquatic life.

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Medium to high; prefers full sun in outdoor ponds.

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.5; temperature 70–85°F (21–29°C)

  • Placement: Floating on the water surface; no planting needed.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Reduces algae by blocking light and absorbing excess nutrients.

  • Creates a natural, lush surface for ponds and outdoor water features.

  • Provides refuge for fry, shrimp, and aquatic critters.

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as a small portion of loose,  Lettuce rosettes (approximately 1–2 plants, depending on size and season).

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Water Lettuce is sensitive to frost and should not be used in outdoor settings during freezing temperatures. In tropical and subtropical climates, this plant can spread rapidly. Always monitor and maintain responsibly.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

We will NOT ship Water Lettuce (Pistia stratiotes) to the following states or territories due to legal restrictions:

Alabama (AL), California (CA), Florida (FL), Louisiana (LA), Mississippi (MS), South Carolina (SC), Texas (TX), Wisconsin (WI), Ohio (OH), Maine (ME), Puerto Rico (PR).

Please respect local regulations and do not release this plant into natural waterways. Always follow state laws and practice responsible plant care.

Care and Maintenance:

Enhance your aquarium with the beauty and vibrancy of Hygrophila salicifolia 'Purple Leaf' and enjoy the dynamic and colorful underwater environment it creates.

products.title: Alternanthera Rosanervig
products.body_html:
Alternanthera Rosanervig is a captivating aquatic plant celebrated for its striking pink veins and vibrant coloration. This unique plant is a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts, adding a splash of color and dynamic contrast to any freshwater aquarium.


Key Features:

  • Distinctive Veining: Alternanthera Rosanervig features bright green leaves with prominent pink veins, creating a stunning visual effect that enhances any aquascape.
  • Vibrant Coloration: Under optimal conditions, the leaves can develop shades of pink, red, and even deep magenta, making this plant a focal point in your aquarium.

Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Requires moderate to high lighting to maintain its vibrant colors and healthy growth. Adequate lighting will highlight the plant's unique veining and overall coloration.
  • CO2 and Nutrients: CO2 supplementation and regular fertilization with both macro and micronutrients are beneficial for enhancing the plant's growth and vibrant colors. A nutrient-rich substrate is also recommended.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0-7.5) and soft to moderately hard water. Ideal water temperature ranges from 22-28°C (72-82°F).

Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The striking pink veins and colorful leaves of Alternanthera Rosanervig add a dramatic and beautiful element to any aquarium, creating a vibrant and dynamic underwater landscape.
  • Versatility: Suitable for various aquascaping styles, this plant can be used as a midground or background plant, providing depth and contrast in your aquarium layout.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides excellent cover and hiding spots for fish and shrimp, contributing to a more natural and secure environment for your aquatic inhabitants.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Pruning: Regular pruning helps maintain the plant's shape and encourages bushier growth. Trimmed stems can be replanted to propagate new plants.
  • Planting: Can be planted directly in the substrate or anchored to rocks and driftwood, offering flexibility in aquascaping.

Conclusion:

Alternanthera Rosanervig is a stunning and versatile plant that can significantly enhance the beauty of your aquarium. Its unique pink veins, vibrant coloration, and ease of care make it an excellent choice for both novice and experienced aquarists. Create a breathtaking underwater landscape with this remarkable plant and enjoy the vivid splash of color it brings to your aquarium.

products.title: Cryptocoryne Green Gecko
products.body_html:
Cryptocoryne Wendtii 'Green Gecko' commonly known as Cryptocoryne Green Gecko, is a stunning aquarium plant known for its lighter green coloration and unique leaf texture. This variety is ideal for adding a touch of vibrant green to the foreground and midground areas in your aquarium, making it a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Cryptocoryne Wendtii 'Green Gecko'
  • Common Name: Cryptocoryne Green Gecko
  • Family: Araceae
  • Leaf Color: Light green
  • Growth Form: Compact, rosette

Unique Characteristics

Cryptocoryne Green Gecko is prized for its bright, light green leaves, which provide a striking contrast against the darker green foliage typically found in aquariums. Its unique leaf texture adds visual interest and depth, making it perfect for creating intricate and visually captivating aquascapes.

Ideal Conditions

To ensure the best growth and vibrant color for Cryptocoryne Wendtii 'Green Gecko' provide the following conditions:

  • Light: Low to moderate intensity
  • CO2: Beneficial but not essential
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate preferred
  • Water Temperature: 22-28°C (72-82°F)
  • pH Range: 6.0-7.5

Care and Maintenance

This plant is relatively easy to care for and is suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists:

  • Lighting: Ensure low to moderate light levels to maintain its vibrant light green coloration.
  • CO2 Injection: Regular CO2 supplementation can enhance growth and color intensity.
  • Trimming: Minimal pruning is required; simply remove any decaying leaves to maintain plant health.
  • Fertilization: Occasional fertilization supports vibrant growth and overall health.

Aquascaping Uses

Cryptocoryne Wendtii 'Green Gecko' is versatile and can be used in various ways:

  • Foreground Plant: Ideal for creating a lush, green carpet in the foreground area.
  • Midground Plant: Can be used to add a touch of green to the midground area.
  • Accent Plant: Its bright light green leaves make it an excellent focal point in aquascapes.

The vibrant light green leaves of Cryptocoryne Green Gecko provide excellent contrast and visual interest, making it a great choice for both Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes.

Propagation

Propagation of Cryptocoryne Wendtii 'Green Gecko' is straightforward. This plant propagates through runners. Simply separate the runners and replant them in the substrate, where they will develop roots and grow into new plants.

Cryptocoryne Wendtii 'Green Gecko' is a must-have for any aquarist looking to enhance their aquarium with a vibrant and eye-catching plant. Its stunning light green leaves, compact growth, and easy maintenance make it a standout choice for creating a beautiful, natural underwater landscape.

  • Sold as a small bare-root plants
  • Young plants grow in a controlled laboratory environment

products.title: Ammannia Crassicaulis
products.body_html:
🌿 Ammannia Crassicaulis – Bronze-Red Stem Beauty

Ammannia crassicaulis (formerly sold under the name Nesaea) is an elegant stem plant admired for its graceful form and rich, warm coloration. At Aquarium Plants Factory®, we cultivate this species in its emersed form, where the leaves appear green. Once transitioned to submerged growth under intense lighting, CO₂ injection, and nutrient-rich conditions, the plant develops stunning bronze, orange, and red tones, making it a beautiful focal plant for advanced aquascapes.

While similar to Ammannia gracilis, this variety displays a softer, golden-bronze hue instead of bright red. It thrives best when stems are spaced apart to allow proper water movement and light penetration.

🌟 Key Features

• Scientific Name: Ammannia crassicaulis
• Common Name: Red Ammannia
• Family Name: Lythraceae
• Native To: Africa and Madagascar
• Growth Form: Upright stem plant with narrow lanceolate leaves
• Coloration: Green (emersed) → Bronze-Red (submerged, high light)
• Lighting: Intense lighting required for best color
• Requirements: Demanding – requires CO₂ and nutrient-rich substrate
• Growth Rate: Moderately fast under good conditions

🌱 Growth & Propagation

• Lighting Needs: High to intense lighting for vivid coloration
• CO₂ & Fertilization: Strongly recommended for lush and vibrant growth
• Spacing: Avoid crowding; allow space for light and flow between stems
• Substrate: Prefers nutrient-rich substrate for strong root development
• Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes, cultivated emersed at Aquarium Plants Factory®

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Provides a warm bronze-red contrast in planted aquariums
• Ideal for mid to background placement in Dutch or Nature-style layouts
• Adds texture, height, and color variation to aquascapes

📦 What You’ll Receive

• Fresh Cut Plants: Includes approximately 6+ loose, fresh-cut stems without roots.
• Fresh Cut Bunched: Approximately 6+ stems bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
• Fresh Cut Potted: About 6+ stems potted in a net pot with rockwool, providing stability and healthy growth.

⚠️ Transition Note

When transitioning from emersed to submerged growth, older leaves may melt back. This is normal as the plant adapts. New underwater growth will soon appear with brilliant bronze-red coloration under strong light and CO₂ conditions.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️

Be a responsible aquatic plant owner. Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

Tall, Spiraling Leaves for Striking Background Effect — Grown in 4-inch Bag

Cryptocoryne retrospiralis is an elegant and distinctive background plant prized for its tall, ribbon-like leaves that exhibit beautiful spiraling patterns. Native to India, this classic species of Cryptocoryne adds vertical movement and texture to any aquascape. Under good lighting, the leaves can develop bronze to reddish hues, making it a versatile choice for both natural-style and Dutch-style aquariums.

Cultivated using our sterile Vitro Culture® method, each 4-inch bag is free from pests, algae, and pesticides—perfect for sensitive aquarium setups and shrimp tanks. This ensures you receive healthy, vibrant plants ready to thrive in your aquascape.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Cryptocoryne retrospiralis

  • Common Name: Retrospiralis Crypt, Cryptocoryne spiralis 'Red', Crypt Spiral Water Trumpet

  • Growth Form: Tall, spiraling ribbon-like leaves

  • Light Demand: Medium to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: Optional, but enhances coloration and growth

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

  • Placement: Background

  • Color: Green to bronze-red under high light

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes — suitable for emersed or immersed culture, ideal for paludariums as well

Propagation: Easily propagated by runners; once established, the plant will send out new shoots around the mother plant.

Never release Cryptocoryne retrospiralis—or any aquarium plant—into natural waterways. Doing so can disrupt local ecosystems. Dispose of any unwanted portions responsibly to help preserve aquatic biodiversity.

products.title: Water Hyacinth
products.body_html:
🌿 Water Hyacinth – Floating Pond Plant

Water Hyacinth (Eichhornia crassipes) is a fast-growing floating aquatic plant admired for its striking lavender-blue flowers and lush, glossy green foliage. This plant is highly effective at improving pond health while adding a vibrant tropical feel to water gardens.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Eichhornia crassipes

  • Type: Free-floating aquatic plant

  • Leaves: Rounded, glossy, bright green with spongy stalks that allow them to float

  • Flowers: Beautiful lavender to violet blooms with a yellow accent spot (seasonal)

  • Growth Habit: Rapid growth, multiplies easily under warm, sunny conditions

  • Benefits: Provides shade, shelter for fish, and absorbs excess nutrients from the water

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Full sun to partial shade (6+ hours recommended for best flowering)

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Not required — absorbs nutrients directly from the water

  • Growth Method: Free-floating, no planting or substrate required

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: No — strictly aquatic and free-floating

🖼️ Aquascaping & Pond Benefits

  • 🌞 Natural Shade: Large floating leaves help reduce algae growth by shading pond surfaces

  • 🐟 Fish Shelter: Roots provide hiding spots and breeding areas for koi, goldfish, and other pond fish

  • 🌊 Water Quality: Excellent at filtering and removing excess nutrients, helping keep water clear

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as fresh, healthy Water Hyacinth plants carefully inspected before shipping:

  • Sold as a portion with 2-3 leaf
  • Beautiful flower when established

⚠️ Disclaimer

Water Hyacinth grows quickly in warm conditions. While beneficial in controlled ponds, it can become invasive if released into natural waterways. Always manage growth responsibly.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium or pond plants into the wild.

products.title: Dwarf Parrot’s Feather
products.body_html:
🌿 Myriophyllum papillosum

Dwarf Parrot’s Feather

Myriophyllum papillosum, commonly known as Dwarf Parrot’s Feather, is a finely textured aquatic stem plant prized for its soft, feathery foliage and vibrant green color. Its delicate, whorled leaves create excellent movement and depth in aquariums, making it a standout choice for midground to background placement.

This plant is easy to grow, adaptable to a wide range of conditions, and does not require CO₂ to thrive—making it suitable for both beginners and experienced aquascapers. When trimmed regularly, it develops dense, bushy growth that enhances natural and planted aquascapes alike.

🌟 Key Features

  • Fine, feather-like green foliage with elegant texture

  • Ideal for midground or background planting

  • Beginner-friendly and adaptable

  • Grows well without CO₂ (optional for faster growth)

  • Responds well to trimming and replanting

🌱 Growth & Care

products.title: Water Mimosa
products.body_html:
Below is a clean APF-style product description tailored for Water Mimosa (Neptunia oleracea) and your variants 2 Plants / 10 Plants – Rooted & Unrooted.

🌿 Water Mimosa (Neptunia oleracea) – Unique Sensitive Floating Aquarium Plant

Water Mimosa (Neptunia oleracea) is a fascinating floating aquatic plant admired for its delicate, fern-like foliage and its remarkable sensitive leaf movement. When touched or disturbed, the leaves gently fold inward, giving this plant its charming “sensitive plant” behavior.

Native to tropical regions, Water Mimosa forms light floating mats on the water surface while developing soft, spongy stems that help the plant stay buoyant. Its bright green foliage creates a beautiful natural canopy that adds texture, shade, and biological balance to aquariums, ponds, and water gardens.

This unique species is highly sought after by aquarists and pond keepers who want something visually different from common floating plants.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Neptunia oleracea
Common Name: Water Mimosa, Sensitive Water Mimosa

• Sensitive Leaves: The delicate foliage folds inward when touched or disturbed.
• Floating Growth: Naturally floats on the water surface while producing trailing stems.
• Natural Surface Cover: Provides gentle shade that helps reduce algae growth.
• Unique Appearance: Soft fern-like leaves create a distinctive tropical look.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

Lighting Needs: Moderate to High
CO₂ Requirement: Not required
Growth Rate: Moderate to Fast under warm conditions
Placement: Floating plant (surface)

Water Mimosa thrives in nutrient-rich water with good lighting. It grows by extending stems that naturally produce new plantlets along the nodes. Simply separate and float the new sections to propagate additional plants.

This plant performs best in warm aquariums, ponds, and water gardens where it has space to spread naturally across the water surface.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Creates a lush floating canopy that softens lighting in the aquarium.
• Provides natural shelter for fish, shrimp, and fry.
• Adds movement and texture to planted aquascapes and pond environments.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Healthy Water Mimosa plants carefully prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®.

Variants available:

2 Plants – Unrooted
Fresh-cut floating plants without roots.

2 Plants – Rooted
Fresh-cut floating plants with visible developing roots.

10 Plants – Unrooted
Bulk portion of fresh-cut floating plants without roots.

10 Plants – Rooted
Bulk portion of fresh-cut floating plants with visible developing roots.

All plants are carefully selected and shipped fresh to ensure healthy arrival.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Aquatic plants may experience minor cosmetic changes during shipping due to temperature, handling, and transit time. Leaves may temporarily fold or show slight stress but typically recover quickly once placed in suitable aquarium or pond conditions.

products.title: Bucephalandra Green Wavy Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌿 Bucephalandra Green Wavy Vitro Culture® – Wavy-Leaf Microform for Detail Aquascaping

Sold as a 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag, yields approx. 3–4 small healthy plants

Bucephalandra Green Wavy is a compact and highly ornamental variety from the Bucephalandra genus, beloved for its lush green, ruffled leaves and naturally clumping growth. Native to the riverbanks of Borneo, this species thrives under waterlogged conditions and is a favorite among aquascapers for nano tanks and foreground detailing. Its vibrant, wavy foliage offers rich texture and a calm visual rhythm—perfect for shaded areas, driftwood accents, or low-tech setups.

Now available in our 100% sterile Vitro Culture® format, this version offers a clean, pest-free, and algae-free start—ideal for shrimp tanks and precision layouts where quality and consistency are critical.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Bucephalandra sp. 'Green Wavy'

  • Common Name: Green Wavy Buce

  • Leaf Form: Small, elongated, and deeply wavy leaves in vibrant green

  • Growth Habit: Clumping, slow-growing rhizome plant; ideal for driftwood and rocks

  • Care Level: Easy to moderate; great for both beginners and seasoned hobbyists

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ optional but enhances color and growth; responds well to liquid ferts

  • Growth Method: Rhizome-based; do not bury in substrate

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds fine detail and texture to hardscape

  • Ideal for shaded or lower-light zones in your layout

  • Naturally clumps to form miniature green islands on wood or stone

📦 What You’ll Receive:

One 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag containing approximately 3–4 small Bucephalandra Green Wavy plantlets—ready to rinse, acclimate, and attach to your aquarium’s hardscape.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Bucephalandra species are slow growers and may take time to adapt after planting. Some melting of older leaves is normal during transition. Avoid burying the rhizome to prevent rot.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Never release Bucephalandra or any aquarium plant into natural waterways. Doing so may harm native ecosystems. Dispose of trimmings responsibly and keep our waters safe.

products.title: Eleocharis Acicularis
products.body_html:
Eleocharis acicularis, commonly known as Hairgrass, is a delicate, grass-like plant perfect for creating lush carpet foregrounds in fish tanks and aquariums. With its fine, needle-pointed leaves, this plant typically reaches a height of 5-20 cm, making it ideal for adding a natural look to your aquascape. We also carry a mini version of Eleocharis Acicularis, behold Eleocharis Acicularis 'Mini'.

Optimal Conditions:

Eleocharis acicularis is an excellent choice for aquarists seeking to add a delicate, grass-like carpet to their aquarium. Its fine, needle-pointed leaves and easy propagation through runners make it a popular choice for creating natural-looking aquascapes. Whether you choose the standard or 'Mini' version, Eleocharis acicularis will enhance the beauty and functionality of your aquarium, providing both aesthetic appeal and practical benefits for your aquatic life.

products.title: Bucephalandra Pandora Queen
products.body_html:
🌸 Bucephalandra ‘Pandora Queen’ – Iridescent Royal Jewel of Borneo

Bucephalandra ‘Pandora Queen’ is a magnificent and rare Bucephalandra variety admired for its striking iridescence and regal appearance. Native to the riverbanks of Borneo, this elegant plant showcases medium-sized leaves that shimmer in shades of blue, violet, and emerald depending on lighting and angle—earning it the name “Pandora Queen.” Its graceful form and radiant color make it a centerpiece in any aquascape, while its hardy, slow-growing nature ensures easy care and long-lasting beauty.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bucephalandra sp. ‘Pandora Queen’

  • Common Name: Buce Pandora Queen

  • Royal Iridescence: Leaves display stunning blue, green, and violet tones that shift under high or RGB lighting.

  • Elegant Leaf Shape: Medium-length, slightly wavy leaves with a soft metallic luster.

  • Growth Form: Compact clumping habit, perfect for driftwood, stones, or as a midground accent.

  • Hardy & Adaptable: Tolerates a wide range of aquarium conditions; suitable for both beginners and collectors.

  • Epiphytic Nature: Easily attaches to hardscape—rhizome should not be buried.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium light; stronger lighting enhances iridescent colors.

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Optional, but recommended for faster growth and more vibrant tones.

  • Growth Method: Rhizome-based; allow the rhizome to rest above the substrate.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required—absorbs nutrients directly from the water column.

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – excellent for paludariums, terrariums, and emersed displays.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds a luxurious iridescent glow and royal color shift to planted aquariums.

  • Perfect for creating contrast and highlights among green or red aquatic species.

  • Excellent centerpiece for nano and mid-sized aquascapes.

  • Enhances both naturalistic and collector-style layouts with depth and visual richness.

📍 Cultivation & Sustainability

At Aquarium Plants Factory’s nursery, all of our Bucephalandra species are 100% farm-raised—never taken from the wild. Each plant originates ex-vitro (grown from sterile lab culture) before being transitioned to hydroponic growth in our controlled environment. This ensures exceptional quality, pest-free plants, and sustainable preservation of Borneo’s native ecosystems.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as loose Bare-Root or Potted plants:

  • 1 Bare-Root: Approximately 5+ leaves

  • 5 Bare-Root: Approximately 25+ leaves total

  • 10 Bare-Root: Approximately 50+ leaves total

  • 1 Potted: Approximately 10+ leaves

  • 5 Potted: Approximately 50+ leaves total

  • 10 Potted: Approximately 100+ leaves total

⚠️ Disclaimer

Like most Bucephalandra species, minor leaf melt may occur after introduction as the plant adjusts to new water parameters. This is normal and temporary—new submersed leaves will regrow from the rhizome once the plant stabilizes.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Plant Weights
products.body_html:
Flexible Plant Weights – Soft Bendable Anchors for Live Aquarium Plants

Despite being commonly called 'lead weights' in the hobby, our weights are crafted from a Zinc Magnesium alloy, making them entirely harmless to every resident of your aquarium.

Use plant weight to wrap around the base, and bottom part of the plants. For best results, use an  APF White Foam™ to protect the stem of aquarium plants from direct contact with a lead weight. Bury the base of aquarium plants in the substrate to promote root growth. 

  • APF Plant Weights for Aquarium Plants
  • Safe for Aquarium use, not toxic to fish, snail, or shrimp
  • Easy to keep plants weighted, anchors down
  • Won't rust, flexible & easy to bent, reuse, and fully recyclable
  • Tips: Use more lead weight to anchor the larger plant

products.body_html:
Littorella Uniflora, commonly known as American Micro Sword or US Micro Sword, is a versatile and attractive plant native to the United States. This grass-like plant is perfect for terrariums, ponds, and aquariums, making it a popular choice among aquarists and hobbyists.

Key Features:

  • Grass-like Appearance: Resembles the well-known Lilaeopsis in appearance, adding a natural, green touch to your aquatic or terrestrial setups.
  • Versatile Growth: Suitable for terrariums, ponds, and aquariums, providing flexibility in various environments.
  • Easy Propagation: Propagates easily via runners, allowing it to develop a full lawn carpet over time.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Planting: Each rosette can be planted a few millimeters apart to encourage spreading and growth.
  • Lighting: Thrives in moderate to high lighting conditions, which help maintain its vibrant green color.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0 to 7.5) and temperatures between 68°F to 78°F (20°C to 26°C).

Aquarium Benefits:

Care and Maintenance:

  • Nutrient-Rich Substrate: Grows well in nutrient-rich substrates that provide essential nutrients for healthy growth.
  • Moderate Maintenance: Requires moderate care, making it suitable for both beginner and experienced aquarists.

Conclusion:

Littorella Uniflora (American Micro Sword, US Micro Sword) is an excellent choice for aquarists and hobbyists looking to create a lush, green carpet in their aquariums, terrariums, or ponds. Its grass-like appearance and easy propagation make it a standout addition to any setup. By providing the right conditions and care, you can enjoy the natural beauty and versatility of Littorella Uniflora in your aquatic or terrestrial environments.

Enhance your aquarium, terrarium, or pond with the natural charm of Littorella Uniflora and enjoy the lush, green landscape it brings to your space.

products.title: Cuphea Anagalloidea
products.body_html:
Cuphea anagalloidea is a delicate small stem plant that requires a lot of care.
The brilliant shade of scarlet that may be found on the upper surface of the submerged leaves is brought out to its fullest potential when exposed to bright light. This plant was initially introduced into the aquarium hobby under the name Rotala sp. Araguaia; however, it was later determined that it belongs to the Cuphea species. Cuphea Anagalloidea can only grow vertically. It looks best when used in a group in the middleground as a color accent. The upper edges of the leaves turn a bright crimson to orange-red color under high lighting, but the stem remains green. Sufficient lighting, with plenty CO2 and nutrients, and soft water are all recommended.

products.title: Bucephalandra Theia Green
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Theia Green is a captivating and rare aquatic plant known for its striking green leaves and unique texture. This plant is highly sought after by aquarists for its beauty and versatility, making it a prized addition to any freshwater aquarium. Bucephalandra is a rheophyte/epiphyte plant, naturally growing over rocks and wood in fast-flowing waters. It takes a very long time to develop into a thick, compact carpet in nature.

Key Features:

  • Striking Green Leaves: Bucephalandra Theia Green boasts vibrant green leaves with a unique, wavy texture. Its lush foliage adds a touch of elegance and a splash of color to any aquascape.

  • Compact Growth: This plant grows in a compact, bushy form, making it ideal for small tanks or as a foreground and midground plant in larger aquariums.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Versatile Attachment: Bucephalandra Theia Green can be easily attached to driftwood, rocks, and other aquarium decorations using fishing line or thread until it establishes its roots. This versatility allows for creative and diverse aquascaping designs.

  • Lighting and Water Parameters: Thrives in low to moderate lighting conditions. It prefers temperatures between 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C) and slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0 to 7.5).

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Enhancement: The vibrant green color and textured leaves of Bucephalandra Theia Green enhance the visual appeal of any aquarium, creating a lush and dynamic underwater landscape.

  • Habitat Creation: This plant provides excellent hiding spots and breeding grounds for small fish and invertebrates, contributing to a balanced and healthy aquarium ecosystem.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Minimal Care Requirements: Bucephalandra Theia Green is relatively easy to care for, requiring only occasional trimming and basic water parameter monitoring.

  • Nutrient Absorption: While it benefits from a nutrient-rich water column, this plant does not require CO2 supplementation. However, CO2 can enhance its growth and vibrancy.

Commitment to Sustainability:

Due to the popularity of Bucephalandra species, they have been heavily collected from the wild, leading to a risk of extinction. At Aquarium Plants Factory, we are committed to providing farm-raised and tissue culture Bucephalandra species to save forests and protect the natural rivers and rainforests in Indonesia.

Conclusion:

Bucephalandra Theia Green is a stunning and versatile plant that brings a touch of natural beauty and elegance to any freshwater aquarium. Its unique foliage and easy care make it a standout addition to any aquascape. Perfect for attaching to driftwood, rocks, and other decorations, Bucephalandra Theia Green is a must-have for aquarists looking to create a lush and vibrant underwater environment.

Transform your aquarium with the exquisite Bucephalandra Theia Green and support sustainable aquascaping practices. Enjoy the serene and captivating beauty it provides.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in convenient formats:

  • Rooted Bunched: Approximately 1-2 plants with some roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Rooted Potted: About 1-2 plants with some roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.

Bucephalandra Lalina is an extraordinary and rare aquatic plant from the Bucephalandra genus, admired for its deep green to bluish leaves with subtle ripples and a gentle metallic sheen. products.title: Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong is a rare and captivating aquatic plant that adds a unique touch of elegance to any aquarium. Known for its velvety, dark green leaves and easy care requirements, this plant is a favorite among aquarists looking to enhance their aquascapes with a distinctive and eye-catching element. Perfect for both beginners and experienced aquarists, Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong is a must-have for any planted tank.

Key Features of Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong

  1. Velvety Dark Green Leaves: Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong boasts rich, velvety leaves that display a deep green hue, sometimes with subtle iridescent highlights. This unique texture and coloration make it a standout feature in any aquascape.

  2. Compact and Versatile: This plant has a compact growth habit, making it ideal for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a foreground or midground plant, providing depth and structure to your aquarium.

  3. Hardy and Adaptable: Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong is known for its resilience and adaptability. It thrives in a range of water conditions and requires minimal maintenance, making it suitable for both low-tech and high-tech aquarium setups.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. This natural habitat makes it an excellent choice for creating biotope aquariums that mimic its natural environment.

  5. Easy Propagation: This plant is easily propagated through rhizome division. Simply cut a section of the rhizome with leaves and roots, and attach it to a new location in your aquarium. Each section will quickly establish itself and continue to grow.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong

  • Lighting: Low to moderate lighting is ideal. While it can tolerate higher light levels, too much light can promote algae growth on its leaves.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in both soft and moderately hard water.
  • Substrate: Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong often does best when attached to rocks or driftwood, mimicking its natural growing conditions. It can also be planted in the substrate if desired.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer supports healthy growth and vibrant colors. CO2 supplementation can enhance its development but is not strictly necessary.
  • Water Flow: Moderate to strong water flow helps mimic the plant’s natural environment, keeping its leaves clean and free from debris.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The velvety, dark green leaves of Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong add a stunning visual effect, enhancing the beauty of any aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides natural hiding spots and surfaces for biofilm, benefiting fish and invertebrates.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Like other aquatic plants, Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water.

Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.

products.title: Ceratopteris Siliquosa
products.body_html:
Ceratopteris Siliquosa, affectionately known as Water Sprite, is a fantastic choice for any aquarium enthusiast. This versatile and fast-growing plant not only adds a lush green touch to your tank but also helps in maintaining a healthy aquatic environment. Whether you're new to the hobby or an experienced aquarist, Water Sprite is an excellent addition to your aquarium setup.

Key Features of Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite)

  1. Beautiful Fern-Like Leaves: Water Sprite is prized for its delicate, lace-like leaves that create a light, airy look. The fresh green foliage brings a touch of nature's elegance into your tank, making it a visual treat.

  2. Versatile Growing Options: Whether you want it floating on the water surface or anchored in the substrate, Water Sprite adapts beautifully. This flexibility allows for creative aquascaping, letting you design a tank that's uniquely yours.

  3. Rapid Growth: Known for its fast growth, Water Sprite quickly fills in gaps, providing cover and helping to keep algae at bay by absorbing excess nutrients. It's a great plant for those looking to establish a lush aquarium in no time.

  4. Natural Habitat: Originating from tropical regions worldwide, Water Sprite thrives in warm, freshwater environments. Its ability to adapt to different conditions makes it ideal for tropical biotope aquariums.

  5. Easy Propagation: Growing your collection of Water Sprite is simple. New plants sprout from the leaves, making it easy to propagate and share with friends or spread throughout your tank.

Care Tips for Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite)

  • Lighting: Moderate to high lighting is best for Water Sprite, ensuring robust growth and vibrant green leaves.
  • Water Conditions: This adaptable plant prefers temperatures between 68-82°F (20-28°C) and a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It thrives in various water conditions, making it a resilient choice.
  • Substrate: While Water Sprite can float, it also does well anchored in the substrate. A nutrient-rich substrate is beneficial but not essential since it absorbs nutrients from the water.
  • Fertilization: Regular use of a balanced aquarium fertilizer promotes healthy growth. CO2 supplementation isn't necessary but can enhance growth.
  • Water Flow: Moderate water flow keeps the leaves clean and simulates its natural environment, helping to prevent debris accumulation.

Benefits of Adding Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite) to Your Aquarium

  • Stunning Visuals: The fern-like leaves of Water Sprite add a striking natural element to your tank, creating a serene and attractive underwater landscape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: It provides excellent cover for fish and invertebrates, making your aquarium a more dynamic and engaging environment.
  • Improved Water Quality: Water Sprite helps maintain a clean tank by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water, reducing the likelihood of algae growth.

Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite) is an outstanding choice for anyone looking to enhance their aquarium with lush greenery and improve the overall health of their aquatic environment. Its rapid growth, ease of care, and beautiful appearance make it a standout addition to any tank.

products.title: Ludwigia Meta
products.body_html:
🌿 Ludwigia Meta – Rare Red Stem Plant from Colombia

Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'Meta', commonly known as Ludwigia Meta, is a rare and magnificent stem plant native to the Departamento del Meta in central Colombia. Admired for its fine, whorled leaves and breathtaking color transformation, this plant is still considered a true rarity in the aquarium trade.

At the Aquarium Plants Factory nursery, Ludwigia Meta is cultivated in its emersed form, where the leaves are green in color. Once transitioned into the aquarium, it develops its signature orange to deep red hues, depending on lighting and nutrient conditions. This makes it an extraordinary choice for aquascapers seeking vibrant contrast and a truly exotic centerpiece.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'Meta'

  • Origin: Departamento del Meta, central Colombia

  • Appearance: Narrow leaves, about 5 cm long and 2 mm wide, arranged in elegant whorls of ~11 leaves

  • Coloration: Brilliant orange to deep red under strong lighting and nutrient-rich conditions

  • Growth Habit: Upright stems reaching up to 30 cm tall and 8 cm wide, with little branching

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Strong lighting required for vibrant coloration

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ injection and balanced macro- and micronutrients are highly recommended

  • Water Parameters: Prefers soft water with carbonate hardness below 4 °dH

  • Growth Method: Submersed stem plant; emersed form produces green leaves and striking yellow flowers

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required for healthy growth and coloration

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – produces elliptical green leaves and yellow flowers on reddish-brown stems when grown above water

  • Propagation: Easily propagated by cuttings; floating stems may develop emersed shoots and flowers

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds spectacular red-orange tones to mid- or background layouts

  • Contrasts beautifully against light green plants, creating dramatic depth

  • Offers a rare centerpiece plant with colors similar to Ludwigia “Pantanal” but with easier care requirements

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Choose the format that suits your aquascaping needs:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Approximately 6+ unrooted stems

  • Bunched Plants: 6+ fresh-cut stems without root, bundled with foam and plant weight

  • Potted Plants: 6+ fresh-cut stems potted in rockwool within a 2-inch net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Ludwigia Meta ships in its emersed green form. Once submerged and provided with strong light and nutrients, it will gradually develop its iconic orange to red hues. Slight leaf melt during transition is normal.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Please check your local regulations before introducing new plants. Never release aquarium plants into natural waterways.

products.title: Ludwigia sp. White
products.body_html:
Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'White', commonly known as Ludwigia sp. White or Ludwigia sp. Pink, is a stunning and versatile aquatic plant that thrives in high-tech planted aquariums. This plant is known for its delicate, pink, and white leaves that add a touch of elegance and color to any aquascape.

Key Features:

  • Striking Appearance: Features delicate leaves that develop beautiful pink and white hues when grown submerged, creating a visually appealing contrast in your aquarium.
  • Versatile Growth: Can be grown both emersed and submerged, adapting its leaf structure accordingly.

Optimal Conditions:

  • High Light: Requires high lighting conditions to maintain its vibrant coloration and healthy growth.
  • CO2 Supplementation: Benefits significantly from ample CO2 supply, promoting robust and vibrant growth.
  • Nutrient-Rich Substrate: Thrives in soil-based nutrient substrates that provide essential nutrients for optimal development.
  • Natural Sunlight: Grows best with plenty of natural sunlight, making it ideal for aquariums with good natural light exposure.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a unique and colorful element to aquascapes with its pink and white leaves, enhancing the overall visual appeal of the aquarium.
  • Versatile Placement: Suitable for midground or background placement, providing depth and contrast in high-tech planted aquariums.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Delicate Handling: Handle with care when transitioning from emersed to submerged growth to avoid damaging the delicate leaves.
  • Regular Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its shape and encourages bushier growth.
  • Consistent Conditions: Ensure stable water quality, CO2 levels, and nutrient availability for the best growth results.

Conclusion:

Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'White' (Ludwigia sp. White/Pink) is an excellent choice for aquarists looking to add a touch of elegance and vibrant color to their high-tech planted aquariums. Its delicate pink and white leaves and unique growth patterns make it a standout addition to any aquascape. With proper care, including high light, CO2 supplementation, and a nutrient-rich substrate, this plant will thrive and transform your aquarium into a stunning underwater garden.

Enhance your aquarium with the captivating beauty of Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'White' and enjoy the lush, vibrant transformation it brings to your aquatic environment.

products.title: Dwarf Watermelon (Lindernia Rotundifolia Variegated)
products.body_html:
Lindernia rotundifolia 'Variegated' – Dwarf Watermelon Stem Plant with Unique Striped Leaves

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we affectionately call this beautiful variety “Dwarf Watermelon” because of its distinctively patterned round leaves, which resemble tiny watermelons. Lindernia rotundifolia 'Variegated' (syn. Lindernia parviflora variegated) is a graceful and fast-growing stem plant perfect for adding visual interest to your aquascape. Its creamy white stripes and strictly upright growth make it an ideal midground focal point or compact background bush when maintained properly.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Lindernia rotundifolia 'Variegated' (syn. Lindernia parviflora variegated)

  • Common Names: Dwarf Watermelon, Variegated Lindernia

  • Unique Appearance: Small, rounded leaves with elegant white longitudinal striping, like a miniature watermelon

  • Growth Habit: Upright stem growth with moderate to high light branching

  • Height: Can reach up to 40 cm; best trimmed to 15–30 cm to prevent uprooting

  • Light Needs: Moderate to high; better light results in bushier, fuller growth

  • CO₂: Not required, but enhances growth and density

  • Water Range: pH 5–8; soft to moderately hard water (0–14° dKH)

  • Temperature Tolerance: Very broad (4–34°C), with 22–26°C being ideal

🧪 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Medium to high; more light = more bushy, compact form

  • Temperature: Performs best at 22–26°C; tolerates a wide range

  • Water Hardness: Suitable for soft to moderately hard water

  • CO₂: Optional but highly beneficial

  • Nutrients: Grows well with general liquid fertilization; not nutrient-sensitive

💡 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Striking Contrast: The variegated leaves pop against green plants and hardscape

  • Midground Favorite: Upright form makes it great for filling space between foreground and background

  • Emersed Growth: Excellent for paludariums or Wabi-Kusa; will produce charming blue-white flowers

  • Fast Coverage: Quick to establish and fill space with proper trimming

  • Easy to Work With: Great for beginners or seasoned aquascapers

🌱 Propagation & Growth:

  • Growth Speed: Fast

  • Propagation Method: Stem cuttings—replant trimmed tops or side shoots

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Enhance your aquarium with our fresh, high-quality plants, available in convenient formats:

  • Loose Bare-Root Plants: Approx. 6+ variegated stems, trimmed and ready for planting

  • Bunched Plants: Approx. 6+ stems bundled with foam and plant weight for easy anchoring

  • Potted Plants: Approx. 6+ stems securely rooted in rockwool within a net pot

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner
Please never release Lindernia rotundifolia—or any aquatic plant—into natural waterways. Always dispose of trimmings responsibly. This species is not currently restricted in the U.S., but please check your local regulations before ordering.

products.title: Bucephalandra Belindae
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Belindae (Bucep / Buce plant) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Araceae. There are more than 30 species of Bucephalandra that have been discovered mostly by S.Y. Wong and P.C. Boyce. Most of the species are found in Borneo. Bucephalandra is usually found growing as dense mats over stones or rocks in streams or rivers in moist tropical forests. The colors of the leaf range from dark blue to green, often with white, yellow, red tinges or spots on the back of the leaves. Bucephalandra grows best when attached to the stone, rock, or driftwood.
  • Sold as a Bunched Plants or Potted Plants.

products.title: Echinodorus Aflame Vitro Culture (Purple Knight Sword)
products.body_html:
🛡️ Echinodorus Aflame Vitro Culture – Purple Knight Sword in 4-Inch Bag

Echinodorus Aflame, commonly known as the Purple Knight Sword, is a captivating compact sword plant admired for its deep burgundy to dark purple foliage and striking upright rosette form. Cultivated in a sterile Vitro Culture environment, this plant arrives in a sealed 4-inch bag, containing approximately 3–4 small, healthy plantlets—ready to bring a bold, regal presence to your aquascape.

Perfect as a midground or background accent, the Purple Knight Sword thrives under high light and enriched substrate, with CO₂ supplementation helping it reach its full crimson potential. Its slow to moderate growth makes it manageable, while its color and structure create a commanding focal point.

✨ Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Echinodorus 'Aflame'

  • Common Name: Purple Knight Sword

  • Growth Form: Upright, compact rosette with sword-like leaves

  • Coloration: Deep red to purple-black under optimal light

  • Light Demand: Medium to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: Recommended for best color development

  • Growth Rate: Medium

  • Placement: Midground to background

🌿 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Temperature: 72–82°F (22–28°C)

  • pH Range: 6.0–7.5

  • Water Hardness: Soft to moderately hard

  • Lighting: Strong lighting enhances deep red coloration

  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate is required for optimal performance

🌊 Vitro Culture Advantages:

  • Pest-Free & Clean: 100% free from snails, algae, and pesticides

  • High Yield: Each 4-inch bag provides approximately 3–4 plantlets

  • Safe for Shrimp & Fry: Excellent for sensitive or display tanks

  • Compact & Adaptable: Easy to plant and acclimate into new setups

🔁 Propagation & Growth:

  • Propagation: Produces adventitious plantlets on submerged flower stalks

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – thrives in humid paludariums and terrariums

📦 What You’ll Receive:

  • You will receive one sealed 4-inch Vitro Culture bag containing approximately 3–4 young Echinodorus Aflame plantlets

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Plant coloration may vary based on lighting, nutrients, and CO₂ levels. Initial growth may appear greenish and gradually develop intense red or purple hues. Avoid burying the rosette crown to prevent rot.

products.title: Eleocharis Montevidensis Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌱 Eleocharis Montevidensis Vitro Culture® – Giant Hairgrass / Tall Hairgrass

Eleocharis montevidensis, commonly known as Giant Hairgrass or Tall Hairgrass, is a striking grass-like aquatic plant valued for its tall, flowing blades and natural meadow-style appearance. Compared to dwarf hairgrass varieties, this species grows significantly taller, making it ideal for adding vertical movement, depth, and contrast in foreground to midground areas of planted aquariums and shallow aquascapes.

This plant is grown and delivered in a 4-inch Vitro Culture® bag, cultivated in a sterile, nutrient-rich gel environment that is 100% algae-free, pest-free, and snail-free, ensuring a clean and reliable start for sensitive aquariums.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Eleocharis montevidensis
  • Common Names: Giant Hairgrass, Tall Hairgrass
  • Plant Type: Stolon-forming grass plant
  • Growth Form: Upright, flowing hair-like blades
  • Color: Bright to deep green
  • Placement: Foreground to midground
  • Growth Rate: Medium

💧 Optimal Growing Conditions

  • Lighting: Medium to high
  • CO₂: Recommended for best growth and density
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich aquarium soil required
  • Water Temperature: 68–82°F (20–28°C)
  • Nutrient Substrate: Required
  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

Under stable conditions, Eleocharis montevidensis spreads via runners and forms attractive clumps that sway naturally with water movement.

🧪 Vitro Culture® Advantages

  • Grown in a sealed sterile environment
  • Free from snails, algae, pests, and pesticides
  • Safe for shrimp and sensitive livestock
  • No quarantine required
  • Easy to divide into multiple planting portions
  • High success rate when transitioning to submerged growth

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds height and gentle motion to planted layouts
  • Creates a natural grassland or shoreline effect
  • Softens hardscape edges and open spaces
  • Ideal for nature-style, biotope, and minimalist aquascapes

📦 What You’ll Receive

One 4-inch Vitro Culture® bag containing multiple healthy portions of Eleocharis montevidensis (Giant Hairgrass / Tall Hairgrass) grown in sterile nutrient gel and ready to rinse, divide, and plant.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Vitro Culture® plants must be rinsed thoroughly to remove gel before planting. Minor melting or adjustment may occur as the plant transitions to submerged conditions. This is normal under proper care.

products.title: Lagenandra Meeboldii Red Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Lagenandra meeboldii is a freshwater aquatic plant that is native to Southeast Asia, particularly found in India, Sri Lanka, and Bangladesh. It belongs to the family Araceae, which includes other popular aquarium plants like Anubias and Cryptocoryne.

Lagenandra meeboldii is a relatively small plant, with leaves that typically grow to around 10-20 cm in length. The leaves are dark green in color and have a slightly glossy texture, with prominent veins that run from the base to the tip of the leaf. The plant is characterized by its unique shape, with the leaves growing in a rosette pattern that gives it a distinctive and attractive appearance.

In aquariums, Lagenandra meeboldii is often used as a midground or background plant, as its size and shape make it an excellent choice for filling in empty spaces and adding depth to the aquascape. It is a relatively hardy plant that can tolerate a wide range of water conditions, including moderate to high lighting and a variety of substrates.

One of the unique features of Lagenandra meeboldii is its ability to change the color of its leaves based on the conditions in which it is grown. Under high light conditions, the leaves can take on a reddish or purplish hue, while under lower light conditions, they may appear more green.

Overall, Lagenandra meeboldii is a beautiful and versatile aquatic plant that can add a unique and attractive element to any aquarium setup. Its hardy nature, ease of care, and ability to adapt to different lighting conditions make it a popular choice among hobbyists and professionals alike.

products.title: Utricularia Graminifolia
products.body_html:
Utricularia Graminifolia is celebrated as one of the most beautiful and popular foreground plants for planted aquariums. Its small size, fluffy appearance, and grass-like lawn effect make it a top choice for aquascaping, especially in nano tanks.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Utricularia Graminifolia
  • Common Name: UG, Grass-leaved Bladderwort
  • Family: Lentibulariaceae
  • Leaf Color: Bright green
  • Growth Form: Dense, carpet-like

Unique Characteristics

Utricularia Graminifolia stands out for its bright green, grass-like leaves that form a lush, dense carpet, creating a stunning visual effect in the foreground of any aquarium. Its compact size and fluffy texture make it ideal for intricate aquascape designs.

Ideal Conditions

To thrive, Utricularia Graminifolia requires specific conditions:

  • Light: High intensity
  • CO2: Beneficial for optimal growth
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich soil
  • Water Temperature: 22-28°C (72-82°F)
  • pH Range: 6.0-7.5

Care and Maintenance

This plant is relatively easy to care for, given the right conditions:

  • Lighting: Ensure high light levels to promote healthy growth.
  • Anchoring: Due to its tiny root system, use stainless steel T-pins to anchor the plant to the substrate until it becomes established.
  • Dry Start Method (DSM): The DSM is highly effective for quickly establishing Utricularia Graminifolia, allowing it to access natural CO2 and grow rapidly.

Growth and Propagation

Once adapted to the environment, Utricularia Graminifolia develops a deep root system, spreading through runners to form a continuous carpet. The dry start method (DSM) is particularly effective, as it allows the plant to access an unlimited amount of natural CO2, boosting growth rates and ensuring a fully grown carpet in a short time.

Safety in Aquariums

Despite being a carnivorous plant, Utricularia Graminifolia is safe for aquarium inhabitants. Its tiny traps are designed to capture microscopic organisms and pose no threat to fish, shrimp, or other larger tank inhabitants. This makes it a unique and harmless addition to your aquascape.

Aquascaping Uses

Utricularia Graminifolia is versatile and ideal for various aquascaping applications:

  • Foreground Plant: Perfect for creating a dense, green carpet in the foreground.
  • Nano Tanks: Ideal for small tanks due to its compact and fluffy growth.
  • Carpet Plant: Its grass-like appearance makes it an excellent choice for lush carpeted areas.

The bright green, dense foliage of Utricularia Graminifolia provides excellent contrast and visual interest, enhancing the overall aesthetics of both Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes.

Utricularia Graminifolia is a must-have for any aquarist looking to enhance their aquarium with a beautiful and unique foreground plant. Its vibrant green color, fluffy texture, and easy maintenance make it a standout choice for creating a stunning underwater landscape.

  • Sold as a small loose bare-root portion
  • Approx. 2" x 2" in size
