products.title: Nymphaea Georgia Peach | Pink Hardy Water Lily
products.body_html:
🌸 Nymphaea Georgia Peach – Pink Hardy Water Lily

Nymphaea Georgia Peach is a beautiful hardy water lily admired for its soft peach-pink blooms and classic lily pad foliage. This variety offers a gentle, elegant color palette that blends naturally into ponds and water gardens while still standing out as a refined focal plant.

Georgia Peach is a dependable bloomer once established and performs well in a wide range of outdoor pond conditions. Its flowers open under bright sunlight and float gracefully above the water surface, creating a timeless and peaceful pond display.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Nymphaea ‘Georgia Peach’

  • Plant Type: Hardy Water Lily

  • Flower Color: Peach-pink

  • Growth Habit: Floating leaves with surface-level blooms

  • Sunlight Requirement: Full sun

  • Suitable For: Outdoor ponds and water gardens

🌱 Growth & Care

  • Thrives in cool to moderate water temperatures

  • Best planted in a nutrient-rich aquatic soil or clay-based substrate

  • Requires full sunlight for consistent flowering

  • Hardy water lily suitable for long-term outdoor pond growth

🖼️ Aquascaping & Pond Benefits

  • Adds soft color and elegance to ponds

  • Lily pads provide natural shade that helps reduce algae

  • Creates shelter and cover for fish

  • Ideal as a centerpiece plant in hardy pond setups

📦 What You’ll Receive

Choose the pack size that fits your pond:

Pack Options:

  • 1 Tuber

  • 5 Tubers

Each tuber sent is healthy, young, and includes approximately 1–2 new sprouting leaves, carefully selected and prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory® to ensure strong establishment.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Live aquatic plants may vary slightly in size, leaf shape, and coloration due to natural growing conditions and seasonal factors. Proper planting depth, sunlight, and water conditions are essential for best results.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium or pond plants into the wild.

PLANTING INSTRUCTIONS FOR WATER LILY

 

There are two main types of water lilies, tropical and hardy. The differences between tropical and hardy water lilies are a growing habit. Tropical water lilies don't do well in winter. Hardy water lilies, on the other hand, can tolerate the cold. Tropical water lilies tubers must be removed from the pond and stored in moist conditions to come back in the future years. In warm climates, they can remain in the pond.

featured_image.alt: Close-up of a healthy hardy water lily rhizome showing new leaf growth, held over a pond background with floating pads.

products.title: Echinodorus Aflame (Purple Knight Sword)
products.body_html:
🔥 Echinodorus Aflame – Deep Red Sword Plant for Vibrant Midground Layouts

Unlike many larger Echinodorus varieties, Aflame maintains a more moderate size, making it ideal for midground placement in medium to large aquariums.

This beautiful sword plant adds bold color and structure to aquascapes and thrives in nutrient-rich environments with medium to high lighting. When provided with CO₂ and root fertilization, its foliage becomes intensely pigmented and lush, making it a true centerpiece in any freshwater setup.

✨ Key Features:

products.title: Cryptocoryne Undulatus Red Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Cryptocoryne undulatus 'Red' is a variant of the popular aquatic plant species Cryptocoryne undulatus. Cryptocoryne undulatus is a species of aquatic plants belonging to the family Araceae. It is commonly known as water trumpet or wavy leaf crypt.

The 'Red' variety of Cryptocoryne undulatus is characterized by its reddish or bronze-colored leaves. This variation in leaf coloration can be influenced by factors such as lighting conditions, nutrient availability, and water parameters in the aquarium. Generally, higher lighting and nutrient levels can lead to more intense red coloration in the leaves.

Cryptocoryne undulatus 'Red' is a popular choice among aquarium enthusiasts for planted tanks due to its distinctive appearance and relatively easy care requirements. It can add a splash of color and visual interest to aquatic setups. Like other Cryptocoryne species, 'Red' varieties prefer stable water conditions, moderate to low lighting, and a nutrient-rich substrate. They can thrive in a range of water parameters, making them suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists.

In terms of propagation, Cryptocoryne undulatus 'Red' can reproduce through runners, which are horizontal stems that produce new plants. As the runners grow, they develop new plantlets at their tips, which can be separated from the parent plant and replanted to propagate the species.

It's important to note that plant names and variations can sometimes be subject to changes and updates within the aquarium hobby, so I recommend consulting with reputable sources or your local aquarium community for the most accurate and up-to-date information about Cryptocoryne undulatus 'Red' or any other aquatic plants you're interested in.

  • Sustainable growth aquarium plants species

products.title: Vallisneria Contortionist
products.body_html:
Vallisneria Contortionist, also known as Corkscrew Val, Contortion Val, or Twisted Val, is a striking and versatile addition to any freshwater aquarium. With its unique twisted leaves and impressive height, this plant brings a dynamic, natural aesthetic to aquatic landscapes, making it a favorite among aquarists.

Key Features:

  • Unique Appearance: Features narrow, twisted leaves that grow straight upward, adding texture and movement to your aquarium.
  • Tall Growth: Reaches heights of 20 inches or more, making it an excellent background plant to create depth and dimension.
  • Rapid Propagation: Easily propagates through runners, quickly filling in large spaces with lush greenery.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Substrate: Thrives in sandy or soil substrates, which promote healthy growth and efficient reproduction via runners.
  • Lighting: Performs well under moderate to high lighting, enhancing its vibrant green color and overall growth rate.
  • Water Parameters: Adapts to a wide range of water conditions but prefers slightly alkaline to neutral pH levels (6.5–8.0) and temperatures between 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C).

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Natural Shelter: Provides shelter for fish and aquatic creatures, helping to reduce stress and create a natural habitat.
  • Oxygenation and Filtration: Contributes to improved water quality by oxygenating the water and absorbing excess nutrients, reducing algae growth.
  • Visual Appeal: Its twisted leaves and tall stature create an eye-catching focal point, perfect for aquascaping.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Easy to Grow: Low-maintenance plant suitable for beginners and experienced aquarists alike.
  • Regular Trimming: Occasional trimming of runners and tall leaves helps maintain its shape and prevent overcrowding.

Conclusion:

Vallisneria Contortionist (Corkscrew Val) is an excellent choice for aquarists looking to enhance their aquarium with a tall, distinctive background plant. Its twisted leaves and fast propagation make it ideal for creating a lush, natural underwater environment while improving water quality.

Elevate your aquascape with the vibrant and unique beauty of Vallisneria Contortionist today!

products.title: Hyptis Lorentziana (Purple Mint)
products.body_html:
Hyptis Lorentziana, commonly known as Purple Mint, is a rare and captivating aquarium plant known for its vibrant purple-red leaves. This plant is ideal for adding a unique touch of color to the background areas in your aquarium, making it a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts who seek rare and exotic flora.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Hyptis Lorentziana
  • Common Name: Purple Mint
  • Family: Lamiaceae
  • Leaf Color: Deep purple-red
  • Growth Form: Moderate height with bushy growth

Unique Characteristics

Hyptis Lorentziana has been a subject of intrigue in the aquatic plant hobby. It was originally called "Hemigraphis traian" but was correctly identified in late 2009 as belonging to the family Lamiaceae. Native to South America, it thrives on the edges of ponds and wet areas, growing up to 2 meters tall in natural settings. This plant is rare in trade but is gaining popularity among hobbyists worldwide.

Ideal Conditions

To ensure the best growth and vibrant color for Hyptis Lorentziana, provide the following conditions:

  • Light: Strong lighting required
  • CO2: Beneficial for optimal growth
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate preferred
  • Water Temperature: 20-28°C (68-82°F)
  • pH Range: 6.0-7.5
  • Nitrate Level: Around 10 mg/l
  • Phosphate Level: 1-2 mg/l

Care and Maintenance

This plant is relatively easy to care for and is suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists:

  • Lighting: Ensure strong light levels to maintain its vibrant purple-red coloration.
  • CO2 Injection: Regular CO2 supplementation can enhance growth and color intensity.
  • Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its form and encourages bushier growth.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization supports vibrant growth and overall health.

Aquascaping Uses

Hyptis Lorentziana is versatile and can be used in various ways:

  • Background Plant: Ideal for adding a unique touch of purple-red to the background area.
  • Accent Plant: Its intense color makes it an excellent focal point in aquascapes.

The large purple-red leaves of Hyptis Lorentziana provide excellent contrast and visual interest, making it a great choice for both Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes.

Propagation

Propagation of Hyptis Lorentziana is straightforward. This plant propagates through stem cuttings. Cut a healthy stem directly above a leaf pair, around 8 to 10 cm above the ground, and replant it in the substrate. This encourages new growth and lateral shoot formation.

Hyptis Lorentziana is a must-have for any aquarist looking to enhance their aquarium with a vibrant and eye-catching plant. Its stunning purple-red leaves, moderate growth, and easy maintenance make it a standout choice for creating a beautiful, natural underwater landscape.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in three convenient formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Includes approximately 3+ loose, fresh-cut stems without roots. Perfect for flexible planting arrangements.
  • Fresh-Cut Bunched: Approximately 3+ fresh-cut stems without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Fresh-Cut Potted: About 3+ fresh-cut stems without roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.

products.title: Vallisneria nana 'Striped'
products.body_html:
🌿 Vallisneria nana 'Striped' – Hardy, Patterned Background Grass Plant

Vallisneria nana 'Striped', also known as Vallisneria nana 'Tiger', is a robust and undemanding background plant ideal for planted aquariums. This cultivar is a hybrid derived from Vallisneria nana, combining its narrow-leaved form with subtle variegation in the form of brown striping or speckled dot patterns on some leaves. Known for its hardiness, it is even more resilient than Vallisneria spiralis and can quickly propagate via runners to form dense, swaying backgrounds.

Reaching over 20 inches in height under optimal conditions, this Tiger Vallisneria retains its rich green coloration even under high-intensity lighting—unlike its cousin Vallisneria spiralis 'Leopard', which develops intense red hues and a more marbled texture. Its graceful, upright growth and ease of care make it a standout choice for aquascapers of all skill levels.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Vallisneria nana 'Striped'

  • Synonyms: Vallisneria nana 'Tiger'

  • Common Name: Tiger Vallisneria

  • Subtle Leaf Variegation: May display random brown stripes or speckles on green leaves

  • Hardy and Adaptable: More resilient than Vallisneria spiralis

  • Tall Growth: Can reach 20+ inches, great for background coverage

  • Runners for Propagation: Spreads easily to create lush plant groupings

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Moderate to high light

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ not required; nutrient-rich substrate or root tabs recommended

  • Growth Method: Grows vertically and propagates rapidly via runners

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Creates soft, natural movement in the background

  • Ideal for Dutch-style and jungle-style aquascapes

  • Pairs well with red or colorful stem plants for contrast

  • Helps soften hardscape transitions and background lines

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose bare-root plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Bare-root Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual loose, bare-root plants

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual bare-root plants bundled with foam and a plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual bare-root plants potted in rockwool and net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Some leaf melting may occur when transitioning to a new tank. Stripe intensity and patterning may vary between individual plants. Provide stable lighting and substrate for best performance.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Always follow your local guidelines and never release aquarium plants into natural water systems. Dispose of plant trimmings responsibly.

products.title: Hygrophila Corymbosa
products.body_html:
Hygrophila Corymbosa, commonly known as Giant Hygro, Temple Plant, Nomaphila Corymbosa, and Hygrophila Stricta, is a versatile and attractive aquatic plant. It boasts hardy stems with large, broad, vibrant green leaves that add elegance and lushness to any aquarium setup. Ideal for both novice and experienced aquarists, Hygrophila Corymbosa is renowned for its hardiness and ease of care.

Key Features:

  • Broad Green Leaves: Creates a stunning visual impact with its large, lush foliage.
  • Fast Growth Rate: Quickly fills in the midground or background, providing a natural, dense look.
  • Hardy and Adaptable: Thrives in various water conditions and light levels.
  • Dutch Style Aquascape: Often used in Dutch style aquascapes for its striking appearance and ability to create depth.
  • Versatile Planting: Suitable for midground and background placement in a wide range of aquascape styles.

Care Instructions:

  • Light Requirement: Medium to high light for optimal growth.
  • CO2 Supplementation: Beneficial but not essential.
  • Optimal Temperature: 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C).
  • pH Range: 6.0 to 7.5.

Trimming and Propagation:

  • Trimming: Regular trimming is crucial to maintain the plant's shape and prevent it from overshadowing other plants. Use sharp scissors to cut back the stems to the desired height, encouraging bushier growth.
  • Propagation: Propagate Hygrophila Corymbosa easily through stem cuttings. Cut a healthy stem with several leaves and replant it in the substrate. The new cutting will quickly develop roots and start growing.

products.title: Ludwigia Red Skeleton
products.body_html:
Ludwigia Rubin 'Red Skeleton' commonly known as Ludwigia Red Skeleton. It is a rare and captivating mutation of Ludwigia repens 'Rubin' perfect for adding a unique touch to any planted aquarium. This aquatic plant is notable for its red stems, red under-leaves, and green upper-leaves with yellow-striped variegation in a striking skeleton pattern. Its distinctive beauty sets it apart, making it a standout choice for aquascaping enthusiasts. Often confused with Ludwigia sp. Atlantis which is a mutation of Ludwigia Repens. 

Key Features:

  • Distinctive Appearance: Features red stems, red under-leaves, and green upper-leaves with yellow-striped variegation in an intricate skeleton pattern.
  • Color Variation: Exhibits stunning deep red coloration under high lighting, while the leaves remain green in low light conditions.
  • Stable Mutation: Offspring consistently inherit the variegated genes, ensuring reliable coloration and patterning.

Ideal Conditions:

  • Lighting and Water Parameters: Thrives in moderate to high lighting. In low light, the leaves stay green; in high light, they turn a striking red. Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.5 to 7.5), with temperatures between 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C).
  • Submerged Growth: Transforms into a beautiful deep red when grown submerged, adding elegance and vibrancy to your aquatic environment.

Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Eye-Catching Focal Point: The unique coloration and pattern make it a perfect centerpiece, drawing attention in any aquarium.
  • Versatile Planting Options: Can be used singly for a striking focal point or planted in groups for a dynamic and interesting background.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Easy to Grow: Adaptable and low-maintenance, making it suitable for both novice and experienced aquarists.
  • Lighting Requirements: Readily turns red under moderate lighting conditions, enhancing the visual appeal of your aquarium.

Conclusion: Ludwigia Rubin 'Red Skeleton' is a must-have for aquarists looking to add a touch of rare beauty and elegance to their freshwater aquariums. At Aquarium Plants Factory, we take pride in successfully cultivating this extraordinary species and we are the first aquatic plant nursery in the United States to release this plant to the market. The stable mutation ensures that offspring inherit the variegated genes from their parents, guaranteeing consistency and quality in every plant. Its impressive coloration, easy growth, and stable mutation make it an ideal choice for creating stunning aquascapes. Whether used as a focal point or a complementary background plant, Ludwigia Rubin 'Red Skeleton' enhances the visual appeal and ecological balance of any aquarium.

products.title: Cameroon Moss
products.body_html:
Cameroon Moss (Plagiochilaceae sp. Cameroon): The Perfect Addition for Freshwater Aquariums

Cameroon Moss, also known as Plagiochilaceae sp. Cameroon, is a popular aquatic moss originating from Cameroon, West Africa. Highly valued for its fast growth and ease of maintenance, this moss is an excellent choice for aquascaping and natural filtration in freshwater aquariums.

Key Features:

  • Rapid Growth: Cameroon Moss grows quickly, helping to maintain a healthy and vibrant aquarium environment.
  • Nutrient Absorption: It efficiently absorbs excess nutrients, enhancing water quality and reducing algae growth.
  • Versatile Use: This moss forms a dense, cushion-like green mat that can be attached to rocks, driftwood, or backgrounds. It also serves as a spawning mop for certain fish species.

Care Tips:

  • Lighting: Provide moderate to low lighting to support healthy growth.
  • Water Changes: Regular water changes are essential to maintain optimal water conditions.
  • Avoid Chemicals: Steer clear of chemical fertilizers or products that can harm the moss and negatively impact water quality.

Benefits:

Cameroon Moss not only adds a natural and aesthetic touch to your aquarium but also creates a habitat for aquatic inhabitants. Its low maintenance requirements make it an ideal choice for both beginner and experienced aquarium enthusiasts.

Enhance your freshwater aquarium with the beauty and functionality of Cameroon Moss. Its ability to improve water quality and provide a natural environment for your fish makes it a must-have for any aquascape.

products.title: Bucephalandra Round Wavy
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Round Wavy (Bucep / Buce plant) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Araceae. There are more than 30 species of Bucephalandra that have been discovered mostly by S.Y. Wong and P.C. Boyce. Most of the species are found in Borneo. Bucephalandra is usually found growing as dense mats over stones or rocks in streams or rivers in moist tropical forests. The colors of the leaf range from dark blue to green, often with white, yellow, red tinges or spots on the back of the leaves. Bucephalandra grows best when attached to the stone, rock, or driftwood.

products.title: Bucephalandra Giant Broad Leaf
products.body_html:
🌿 Bucephalandra ‘Giant Broad Leaf’ – Rare Large-Leaf Aquatic Jewel

Bucephalandra ‘Giant Broad Leaf’, commonly known as Buce Giant Broad Leaf, is a striking and rare Bucephalandra variety admired for its wide, paddle-shaped leaves and commanding presence in aquascapes. Originating from the clean, fast-flowing rivers of Borneo, this slow-growing species stands out for its thick, glossy foliage that shimmers in deep green with subtle bluish tones under aquarium lighting. Its large, dramatic leaves bring instant depth and natural elegance to any aquascape—making it a prized gem among collectors and enthusiasts.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bucephalandra sp. ‘Giant Broad Leaf’

  • Common Name: Buce Giant Broad Leaf

  • Large, Glossy Foliage: Broad, thick leaves with a smooth surface and metallic green-blue iridescence under aquarium light.

  • Hardy & Adaptable: Thrives under low to medium lighting and tolerates a wide range of aquarium conditions.

  • Easy Attachment: Naturally grows on hard surfaces—perfect for driftwood, stones, or decorative hardscape.

  • Aquascaping Centerpiece: Its impressive leaf size makes it a perfect focal point for both nano and large aquariums.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium lighting (higher intensity enhances the iridescent sheen).

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Not required but beneficial for faster growth and deeper coloration.

  • Growth Method: Rhizome-based; do not bury the rhizome in substrate—attach to hardscape instead.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required; Bucephalandra absorb nutrients directly from the water column.

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – excellent for terrarium and paludarium setups.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds mature structure and contrast to any aquascape.

  • Ideal for midground or background placement or as a showpiece plant on driftwood or rock.

  • Pairs beautifully with mosses, Anubias, or red stem plants for dynamic layouts.

  • Highly durable and easy to maintain—great for both beginners and experts.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as loose bare-root plants:

  • 5 Plants: Loose bare-root with approximately 25+ leaves total

  • 10 Plants: Loose bare-root with approximately 50+ leaves total

⚠️ Disclaimer

Like most Bucephalandra, slight leaf melt may occur after introduction as the plant adjusts to new conditions. This is temporary—new submersed leaves will regrow from the rhizome once the plant stabilizes.

products.title: Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost is an extraordinary and rare aquatic plant that captivates with its deep, dark hues and unique foliage. This plant is a favorite among aquarists looking to add a touch of mystery and elegance to their aquascapes. Known for its ease of care and adaptability, Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost is perfect for both novice and experienced aquarists.

Key Features of Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost

  1. Enchanting Dark Foliage: Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost features dark, almost black leaves with subtle iridescent highlights. These leaves create a striking contrast in your aquarium, making this plant a standout feature in any aquascape.

  2. Compact and Bushy Growth: This plant has a compact and bushy growth habit, making it ideal for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a midground or foreground plant, providing depth and texture to your aquascape.

  3. Hardy and Adaptable: Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost is known for its resilience and adaptability. It thrives in a range of water conditions and requires minimal maintenance, making it suitable for both low-tech and high-tech aquarium setups.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, this plant grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. Its natural habitat makes it an excellent choice for replicating natural biotope aquariums.

  5. Easy Propagation: This plant is easily propagated through rhizome division. Simply cut a section of the rhizome with leaves and roots, and reattach it to a new location in your aquarium. Each section will quickly establish itself and continue to grow.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost

  • Lighting: Low to moderate lighting is ideal for Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost. While it can tolerate higher light levels, too much light can promote algae growth on its leaves.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in both soft and moderately hard water.
  • Substrate: Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost often does best when attached to rocks or driftwood, mimicking its natural growing conditions. It can also be planted in the substrate if desired.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer will support healthy growth and vibrant colors. While CO2 supplementation can enhance its development, it is not strictly necessary.
  • Water Flow: A moderate to strong water flow can mimic the plant’s natural environment and help keep its leaves clean and free from debris.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The dark, iridescent foliage of Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost adds a stunning visual effect, enhancing the overall beauty of your aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides natural hiding spots and surfaces for biofilm, benefiting fish and invertebrates.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Like other aquatic plants, Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water.

Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.

Sold as Bunched Plants or Potted Plants:

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 1-2 plants bundled with a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Potted Plants: Approximately 1-2 plants potted in rockwool and a net pot for secure growth and easy placement.

products.title: Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue is a stunning and highly sought-after aquatic plant that adds a touch of elegance and uniqueness to any aquarium. Known for its striking blue hues and delicate foliage, this plant is a favorite among aquascapers and hobbyists alike. With its easy care requirements and captivating appearance, Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue is the perfect addition to both beginner and advanced planted tanks.

Key Features of Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue

  1. Striking Blue Foliage: The most distinctive feature of Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue is its beautiful blue-tinted leaves. These iridescent leaves shimmer under aquarium lighting, creating a mesmerizing effect that enhances the visual appeal of any aquascape.

  2. Compact and Versatile: This plant has a compact growth habit, making it ideal for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a foreground or midground plant, and its slow growth rate ensures it maintains its shape without frequent trimming.

  3. Easy to Care For: Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue is a hardy plant that thrives in a range of water conditions. It requires low to moderate lighting and can tolerate a variety of water parameters, making it suitable for both low-tech and high-tech setups.

  4. Unique Flowers: In addition to its striking foliage, Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue occasionally produces small, delicate flowers that add an extra layer of beauty to the aquarium. These flowers typically bloom above the water surface.

  5. Natural Habitat: This plant is native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, where it grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. This natural habitat makes it an excellent choice for replicating natural biotope aquariums.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue

  • Lighting: Low to moderate lighting is ideal for Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue. Too much light can lead to algae growth on its leaves, while too little light may reduce its vibrant coloration.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in both soft and moderately hard water.
  • Substrate: While Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue can be planted in substrate, it often does best when attached to rocks or driftwood, mimicking its natural growing conditions.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer will promote healthy growth and vibrant colors. CO2 supplementation can enhance growth but is not strictly necessary.
  • Propagation: This plant can be easily propagated by cutting and replanting its rhizomes. Ensure each cutting has a few leaves and roots for successful propagation.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The unique blue coloration and compact growth of Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue create a striking focal point in any aquarium, enhancing the overall aesthetic.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides natural hiding spots and surfaces for biofilm, benefiting fish and invertebrates.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Like other aquatic plants, Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water.

Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue is a must-have for any aquarist looking to elevate the beauty and biodiversity of their aquarium. Its unique appearance, combined with its ease of care, makes it a standout choice for creating a vibrant and healthy underwater environment.

products.title: Bucephalandra Dark Blue
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Dark Blue (Bucep / Buce plant) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Araceae. There are more than 30 species of Bucephalandra that have been discovered mostly by S.Y. Wong and P.C. Boyce. Most of the species are found in Borneo. Bucephalandra is usually found growing as dense mats over stones or rocks in streams or rivers in moist tropical forests. The colors of the leaf range from dark blue to green, often with white, yellow, red tinges or spots on the back of the leaves. Bucephalandra grows best when attached to the stone, rock, or driftwood.

  • Sold as a Bunched Plants or Potted Plants.

products.title: Pogostemon Stellatus Dassen Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Pogostemon stellatus 'Dassen' in Vitro Culture® – Rare, Starburst-Leaf Stem Plant for Advanced Aquascapes

Pogostemon stellatus 'Dassen' is a rare and stunning aquatic stem plant known for its fine, needle-like leaves arranged in a striking starburst pattern. This unique cultivar of Pogostemon stellatus originates from Asia and is prized for its ability to shift in color—ranging from light green to pink, orange, or purple under optimal conditions. Its dynamic structure and intense hues make it an ideal showpiece for Dutch-style aquascapes, color gradients, or high-tech planted tanks.

Each plant is offered in a 4-inch Vitro Culture® bag, containing multiple small, pest-free plantlets grown in vitamin-enriched gel or liquid. Our tissue culture propagation ensures a completely sterile start—100% free of snails, algae, pesticides, and pathogens—making it ideal for shrimp-safe tanks and premium aquascaping setups.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Pogostemon stellatus 'Dassen'

  • Common Name: Pogostemon 'Dassen'

  • Leaf Shape: Fine, needle-like leaves in starburst whorls

  • Coloration: Green to vivid pink, orange, or purple with strong lighting and nutrients

  • Growth Form: Upright stem plant with dense branching

  • Vitro Culture® Format: 4-inch sealed bag with multiple plantlets

  • Placement: Midground to background

  • Growth Rate: Medium to fast

  • Difficulty Level: Advanced – thrives best in stable, enriched tanks

💧 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: High (essential for vibrant coloration and compact growth)

  • CO₂: Strongly recommended for optimal form and color

  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate or root tab supplementation

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.2 | Temperature: 22–28°C (72–82°F)

  • Fertilization: Requires regular micronutrient and iron dosing to maintain coloration

  • Trimming: Regular pruning helps promote bushy, compact form

🧪 Vitro Culture® Advantages:

  • 100% Free from Snails, Algae, Pesticides, and Pathogens

  • Clean & Safe: Perfect for shrimp tanks and sensitive ecosystems

  • Long Shelf Life: Can be stored under light until ready to plant

  • Easy to Plant: Rinse, gently divide, and insert into substrate using tweezers

🐠 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Color Showpiece: Adds a brilliant pop of color and texture to your layout

  • Fine Texture: Balances well with broad-leaf plants and mosses

  • Dutch Layout Star: A must-have for advanced aquascapers building color tiers

  • High Impact: Makes an instant impression in feature spots or mass plantings

📦 What You’ll Receive:

One 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag containing multiple Pogostemon stellatus 'Dassen' plantlets, ready for acclimation and planting.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Photos are for reference. Actual coloration depends on lighting, CO₂, and nutrient availability. Some melt may occur during the transition to submerged growth, which is normal.

products.title: Limnophila Rugosa
products.body_html:
🌿 Limnophila Rugosa – Basil Leaf Limnophila

Limnophila Rugosa, commonly known as Basil-Leaf Limnophila or Limnophila Butterfly, is an elegant and distinctive aquarium plant native to tropical Asia. Unlike most Limnophila species, it features broad, textured leaves resembling basil, adding a unique charm and texture to aquascapes. Its bushy growth habit and vibrant green coloration make it a standout choice for midground or background planting.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Limnophila Rugosa

  • Common Name: Basil-Leaf Limnophila, Limnophila Butterfly

  • Family: Plantaginaceae

  • Origin: Tropical Asia

  • Leaf Color: Bright green

  • Growth Form: Bushy, dense stem plant

  • Growth Rate: Slow

  • Difficulty Level: Easy

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Medium to high

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ is recommended for optimal growth and leaf health

  • Growth Method: Submersed or emersed

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required for best results

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

  • Water Temperature: 72–82°F (22–28°C)

  • pH Range: 6.0–7.5

  • Propagation: Simple stem cuttings – replant trimmed stems into substrate for new growth

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Midground Density: Adds lush, vibrant green fullness to aquascape midsections

  • Background Accent: Works beautifully as a natural green backdrop

  • Focal Point Texture: Basil-like leaves provide unique visual contrast in planted layouts

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose fresh-cut plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Approx. 4 loose-cut stems without root

  • Bunched Plants: Approx. 4 stems without root, bundled with foam and a plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approx. 4 stems without root, potted in rockwool and net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer:
Pictures shown are a sample representation and not the exact plants you will receive. Unless specified, we do not guarantee plants to be free of pests or pesticides, nor do we guarantee they will be grown submerged.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner
Please do not release Limnophila Rugosa or any aquatic plants into natural waterways. Always dispose of trimmings responsibly. Check your local regulations before purchase. Not available to ship to states with Ludwigia restrictions.

products.title: Hygrophila Odora
products.body_html:
🌿 Hygrophila Odora – Narrow-Leaf Green Alternative to Water Wisteria & Hygrophila pinnatifida

Hygrophila odora is a rare and visually striking African stem plant that combines the delicate, finely divided look of  Water Wisteria (Hygrophila difformis) with the bold, architectural appeal of Hygrophila pinnatifida—while maintaining a clean, vibrant green coloration.

The leaf shape closely resembles Water Wisteria but is much narrower and more refined, giving the plant a sharper, more structured appearance. At the same time, its overall form and texture are very similar to Hygrophila pinnatifida, making Hygrophila odora an excellent green alternative for aquascapers who love pinnatifida’s look but prefer to avoid brown or reddish tones.

When grown submersed, Hygrophila odora produces elegant, deeply lobed green leaves on upright stems, making it ideal for midground to background placement. In emersed form, the leaves broaden slightly, develop a soft texture, and release a natural aromatic scent when touched—a rare and unique trait among aquarium plants.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Hygrophila odora
  • Common Name: Hygrophila Afrika
  • Origin: West Africa
  • Growth Rate: Medium
  • Placement: Midground to Background
  • Color: Bright to deep green
  • Leaf Shape: Narrow, finely divided (similar to Water Wisteria, but slimmer)
  • Comparable To: Hygrophila difformis (Water Wisteria), Hygrophila pinnatifida
  • Unique Trait: Aromatic foliage when grown emersed
  • Difficulty: Beginner to Advanced

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting: Medium to High
  • CO₂: Recommended for best structure and faster growth
  • Growth Method: Stem plant
  • Nutrient Substrate: Required
  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes
  • Propagation: Trim and replant healthy stem cuttings

With adequate nutrients and CO₂, Hygrophila odora grows strong, upright stems with consistent leaf development. It adapts well to submersed aquariums and also performs exceptionally in paludariums and ripariums when grown emersed.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Offers the fine, feathery look of Water Wisteria in a more compact, refined form
  • Delivers the bold structure of Hygrophila pinnatifida while staying green
  • Excellent contrast plant for red species, dark hardscape, and shadowed layouts
  • Perfect for nature-style, jungle, and collector aquascapes

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Hygrophila odora cultivated and prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®, available in convenient planting formats:

Loose Bare-Root Plants:
Approximately 1 individual plant with 4+ healthy leaves, carefully cleaned and ready for planting.

Rooted Bunched Plants:
Approximately 1 rooted plant with 4+ leaves, secured with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.

Rooted Potted Plants:
Approximately 1 rooted plant with 4+ leaves, established in a net pot with rockwool for stability and easy planting.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Photos are for reference only. Actual plants may vary in size, shape, and coloration depending on growth stage and environmental conditions. Minor melting or leaf adjustment may occur as the plant adapts to a new aquarium—this is normal for stem plants.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Java Fern Narrow Leaf
products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus ‘Narrow’ – Slender Java Fern with Upright Leaf Structure

Microsorum pteropus 'Narrow', commonly known as Java Fern Narrow, is a refined and elegant variant of the classic Java Fern, easily recognized by its long, narrow, upright leaves. This cultivar is a favorite among aquascapers for its graceful form and vertical growth, making it an excellent choice for midground or background placement in planted aquariums.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we cultivate Java Fern Narrow in its emersed form, ensuring a hardy plant ready to transition to submerged conditions. Once established, the slender green fronds gently sway in the current, adding subtle motion and height to your aquascape while maintaining a clean, open aesthetic.

Like all Microsorum species, Java Fern Narrow is a rhizome plant that must be attached to driftwood, stones, or other hardscape elements. It should never be buried in substrate, as doing so may lead to rhizome rot. This plant is slow-growing, low-maintenance, and thrives in low- to medium-light environments with no need for CO₂—ideal for beginners or those running low-tech aquariums.

🌟 Key Features:

products.title: Proserpinaca Palustris 'Cuba' (Mermaid Weed) Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌿 Proserpinaca Palustris 'Cuba' (Mermaid Weed) – Vibrant Serrated Aquarium Stem Plant

Proserpinaca palustris 'Cuba', commonly known as Mermaid Weed, is a unique and highly decorative aquarium stem plant admired for its dramatic leaf transformation and vibrant coloration. Under moderate conditions, the plant develops bright green leaves, while stronger lighting and CO₂ encourage the formation of finely serrated foliage with stunning copper, orange, and reddish tones.

This distinctive species creates beautiful vertical accents in aquascapes and offers striking texture compared to traditional smooth-leaf stem plants. Its delicate, needle-like leaves and dramatic color shifts make it a favorite choice for aquascapers seeking both contrast and visual depth in planted aquariums.

Grown in sterile laboratory conditions, this Vitro Culture® 4-inch bag provides clean, pest-free plant material ready to be divided into multiple healthy plantlets for planting in your aquarium.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Proserpinaca palustris 'Cuba'
Common Name: Mermaid Weed
Family: Haloragaceae

• Distinctive serrated foliage that adds rare texture to aquascapes
• Leaves shift from green to copper-red under strong lighting
• Elegant vertical growth ideal for midground or background placement
• Unique needle-like leaf form unlike most aquarium stem plants

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions

Lighting: Medium to High
CO₂: Recommended for best coloration and compact growth
Growth Rate: Medium
Placement: Midground to Background
Water Temperature: 68°F – 82°F (20°C – 28°C)
pH Range: 6.0 – 7.5

Under strong lighting and proper nutrients, the plant develops deeper red and copper tones while maintaining compact serrated foliage.

🧫 Vitro Culture® Advantages

Our Vitro Culture® plants are laboratory-grown in sterile conditions, offering several important benefits for aquarium hobbyists:

• Snail-free and pest-free plant material
• No algae or parasites introduced into your aquarium
• Young, healthy plants that adapt quickly to submerged growth
• Multiple plantlets that can be separated and planted individually

This allows aquascapers to create dense plant groupings and natural aquascapes with a single culture portion.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Textural Contrast: The serrated foliage creates unique visual texture among smoother plants
• Color Accent: Copper-red tones add warmth and contrast to green aquascapes
• Natural Depth: Tall vertical stems enhance aquascape layering and dimension

Mermaid Weed pairs beautifully with plants such as Rotala, Ludwigia, and other colorful stems to create a vibrant planted layout.

📦 What You’ll Receive

1 Pack Vitro Culture® (4-inch bag) containing multiple young plantlets.

Each sterile culture can be gently separated into several individual plants ready for planting throughout your aquarium.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Live aquatic plants naturally vary in size, coloration, and appearance depending on growing conditions and season. Plants may appear slightly different from photos after shipping but will adapt and develop their mature coloration once established in the aquarium.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

Under emersed or high-light conditions, its foliage transforms into a striking copper-red hue, creating a bold and captivating contrast. Perfect for midground or background placement, this plant enhances the depth and texture of your aquarium while pairing beautifully with green plants to create a vibrant visual appeal.

products.title: Anubias Petite White Marble
products.body_html:

Anubias Petite White Marble is an exceptionally rare albino cultivar of Anubias barteri var. nana ‘Petite’, prized for its marbled white to creamy foliage, miniature size, and slow, deliberate growth. Its striking lack of chlorophyll creates a dramatic contrast against dark hardscape and green plants, making it a true collector-grade centerpiece.

Due to its low chlorophyll content, this plant requires careful placement and experienced care, and is best suited for advanced aquascapers seeking a unique and prestigious addition to their layout.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Anubias barteri var. nana ‘Petite White Marble’
  • Common Name: Anubias Petite White Marble
  • Leaf Color: White to pale cream with subtle marbling
  • Leaf Size: Very small (approx. 0.5–1 inch)
  • Growth Rate: Extremely slow
  • Difficulty: Advanced
  • Plant Type: Rhizome / Epiphyte

🌱 Growth & Care Requirements

  • Lighting: Low only (avoid direct or strong light)
  • CO₂: Not required (stable CO₂ may help overall health)
  • Substrate: Not required
  • Placement: Attach to driftwood, stone, or décor
  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.5 | 68–82°F (20–28°C)

⚠️ Important: Never bury the rhizome. Excess light can cause leaf burn or decline due to the plant’s limited photosynthesis ability.

🧩 Aquascaping Use

  • Ideal for luxury nano aquariums
  • Perfect accent for bonsai trees and shaded hardscape
  • Best used sparingly to highlight rarity and contrast
  • Recommended for experienced hobbyists and collectors

📦 What You’ll Receive

  • Carefully selected and prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®

Each plant is unique. Leaf coloration, marbling pattern, and size may vary naturally.

products.title: Bacopa Salzmannii 'Purple'
products.body_html:
💜 Bacopa salzmannii 'Purple' – Singapore Variant

Bacopa salzmannii 'Purple' (Singapore Variant) is a rare and highly coveted stem plant admired for its vivid magenta to deep purple tones—a true showstopper in any planted aquarium. Unlike the standard Bacopa salzmannii, this unique Singapore form maintains its striking purple coloration both emersed and submerged, making it an exceptional centerpiece or accent plant for aquascapes of all styles.

Beyond its beauty, this plant provides ecological balance by absorbing excess nutrients, improving water quality, and supporting a healthy aquatic environment.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bacopa salzmannii 'Purple' (Singapore Variant)

  • Common Name: Purple Bacopa, Singapore Purple Bacopa

  • Growth Form: Upright stem plant

  • Coloration: Vibrant purple to magenta—retains color above and below water

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

  • Placement: Midground to background

  • Propagation: Stem cuttings from healthy growth

🔮 Unique Traits

  • Retains Purple Coloration in All Conditions: Unlike typical Bacopa salzmannii that turns green when grown emersed, this variant stays purple in both environments.

  • High-Contrast Focal Plant: Its bold color provides dramatic contrast against green plants, driftwood, and rockwork.

  • Singapore-Origin Lineage: Cultivated from a select strain renowned for its consistent hue and compact form.

💡 Optimal Conditions & Care

  • Lighting: Moderate to high—strong light enhances purple pigmentation

  • CO₂: Optional, but recommended for more vibrant color and lush growth

  • Water Parameters: 72–82°F (22–28°C); pH 6.0–7.5

  • Difficulty: Easy to moderate; ideal for most aquascapers

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds luxurious purple contrast to nature and Dutch-style layouts

  • Perfect for color transitions between red and green plant zones

  • Naturally helps reduce algae by absorbing excess nutrients

  • Creates a vibrant focal point when grouped or trimmed into dense clusters

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required for optimal color and root development

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – retains its purple hue even above water

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Bacopa salzmannii 'Purple' cultivated and prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®, available in convenient planting formats:

Fresh Cut Plants: Approximately 6+ loose, fresh-cut stems without roots.
Fresh Cut Bunched: Approximately 6+ stems bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
Fresh Cut Potted: About 6+ stems potted securely in a net pot with rockwool for stability and easy planting.

⚠️ Disclaimer

As with many stem plants, Bacopa salzmannii 'Purple' may experience brief melt or leaf loss during transition from emersed to submerged growth. This is normal—new underwater leaves will regrow once stable.

products.title: Java Fern
products.body_html:
🌿 Java Fern (Leptochilus pteropus) – Classic Hardy Aquarium Plant

Leptochilus pteropus, formerly known as Microsorum pteropus and widely recognized as Java Fern, is one of the most iconic and beginner-friendly aquatic plants in the aquarium hobby. Renowned for its durability, adaptability, and timeless appearance, this plant features deep green, elongated leaves that bring a natural and mature look to any aquascape.

Naturally found attached to rocks and driftwood in slow-moving tropical waters, Java Fern thrives in a wide range of aquarium conditions, making it an essential choice for both beginners and experienced aquarists.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Leptochilus pteropus

  • Synonyms: Microsorum pteropus

  • Common Name: Java Fern

  • Hardy & Beginner-Friendly: Extremely resilient and easy to care for

  • Epiphytic Growth: Attaches to driftwood, rocks, or décor—no substrate required

  • Timeless Appearance: Deep green leaves add a natural, lush look

  • Low Maintenance: Thrives in a wide range of water parameters

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Low to moderate (tolerates a wide range)

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ not required; benefits from liquid fertilizers

  • Growth Rate: Slow to moderate

  • Growth Method: Rhizome plant (must not be buried in substrate)

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

Java Fern propagates easily by dividing the rhizome or through plantlets that form on older leaves, making it simple to expand within your aquarium.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Perfect for Hardscape: Ideal for attaching to driftwood, rocks, and décor

  • Midground to Background Placement: Adds depth and structure to layouts

  • Natural Jungle Look: Creates a mature, established aquascape feel

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Java Fern (Leptochilus pteropus) cultivated and prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®, available in convenient planting formats:

  • Bare-root: Approximately 6+ individual leaves with some roots attached. Leaves may arrive separated, and rhizomes are not guaranteed to be connected. This is normal for Java Fern and allows easy attachment to hardscape.
  • Bunched: Approximately 6+ leaves with some roots bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement. Leaves may be individual, and rhizomes may not be connected. This is normal for Java Fern and does not affect growth.
  • Potted: Approximately 6+ leaves with some roots placed in a net pot with rockwool for stability. Leaves may be individual, and rhizomes may not be connected. This is normal and the plant will continue to grow once established.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Live aquatic plants may vary slightly in size, shape, and color depending on current stock and growing conditions. Java Fern is grown emersed and will transition naturally once placed in your aquarium. Some leaf melt may occur during this transition as the plant adapts to submerged conditions.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner.

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Anubias Golden Nana

This low-maintenance aquatic plant is perfect for both beginners and experienced aquarists, adding a pop of unique color to any freshwater aquarium setup. Its hardy nature allows it to thrive in a wide range of water conditions, making it an ideal choice for various aquascapes.

  • Compact Growth: With a small size, this plant is perfect for foreground or midground placement in your aquarium.
  • Slow Growing: Its slow growth rate reduces the need for frequent pruning, making maintenance easier.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives in low to moderate lighting conditions, perfect for low-tech setups.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0 to 7.5) and temperatures between 68°F to 82°F (20°C to 28°C).

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Versatile Placement: Can be placed on driftwood, rocks, or substrate, enhancing the aesthetic appeal of your aquarium.
  • Durable and Hardy: Resistant to fluctuating water conditions, making it ideal for both beginner and advanced aquarists.

products.title: Bucephalandra Rainbow Marble
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Rainbow Marble (Bucep / Buce plant) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Araceae. There are more than 30 species of Bucephalandra which have been discovered mostly by S.Y. Wong and P.C. Boyce. Most of the species are found in Borneo. Bucephalandra is usually found growing as dense mats over stones or rocks in streams or rivers in moist tropical forests. The colors of the leaf range from dark blue to green, often with white, yellow, red tinges or spots on the back of the leaves. Bucephalandra grows best when attached to the stone, rock or driftwood. 
  • Sold as a small loose, bare-root rhizome plant with 4+ leaves.
  • Pictures are not the actual plants you will receive, but a sample representation.

products.title: Bucephalandra Velvet Tricolor
products.body_html:
🌿 Bucephalandra sp. "Velvet Tricolor" – Rare Bushy Epiphyte

Bucephalandra sp. "Velvet Tricolor" (sometimes referred to as Velvet 3 Color) is a rare, small, and densely bushy Bucephalandra form with narrow, wavy leaves. Believed to originate from the Sanggau area in West Kalimantan, Borneo, its precise species classification and exact locality remain uncertain. Despite its rarity, this plant has proven to be highly suitable for aquariums, with excellent growth habits and steady propagation.

Young leaves emerge in shades of light brown-red, later maturing into deep dark brown-green, creating a striking multicolor effect that inspires its name. Its compact, ramified rhizome structure forms a bushy cluster, making it a unique addition to any aquascape.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Bucephalandra sp. "Velvet Tricolor"

  • Origin: West Kalimantan, Borneo (Indonesia)

  • Growth Form: Small, narrow-leaved Bucephalandra with bushy habit

  • Leaf Shape & Size: Linear, wavy leaves approx. 30 x 4.5 mm, with a 5 mm petiole

  • Coloration: Young leaves light brown-red, mature leaves dark brown-green

  • Height: Reaches around 6 cm when grown submerged

  • Rarity: Rarely available in the hobby, propagated in aquaria for over a year

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Medium lighting recommended

  • CO₂ & Fertilization: Benefits from CO₂ injection, good nutrient supply, and steady water current

  • Growth Method: Epiphyte – best attached to rocks or driftwood (avoid burying the rhizome)

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes, though usually propagated submerged in aquaria

  • Propagation: Easily propagated via rhizome cuttings, forming dense clusters over time

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Perfect as a low-growing bushy epiphyte in aquascapes

  • Offers subtle color contrast with red-brown to green hues

  • Excellent for hardscape decoration on rocks and driftwood

  • Suitable for nano aquascapes as well as larger aquariums due to its slow but steady propagation

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as Bare-root Plants:

  • 1 Plant: Loose bare-root with approximately 6+ leaves

  • 5 Plants: Loose bare-root with approximately 25+ leaves in total

  • 10 Plants: Loose bare-root with approximately 50+ leaves in total

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Like all Bucephalandra species, growth rate may vary depending on tank conditions. Ensure the rhizome is never buried in substrate, as this may cause rot. Stable water parameters and patience are key for long-term success.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Please check your local regulations regarding the import or keeping of aquatic plants. Never release aquarium plants into local waterways.

products.title: Giant Duckweed
products.body_html:
🌿 Giant Duckweed (Spirodela polyrhiza) – Fast-Growing Floating Aquarium Plant

Giant Duckweed (Spirodela polyrhiza) is a popular floating aquatic plant valued for its noticeably larger leaves and longer roots compared to common duckweed. Its bright green, round fronds float gracefully across the water surface, creating natural shade while helping maintain a balanced aquatic environment.

This hardy floating plant grows rapidly under proper lighting and nutrients. It is widely used in aquariums, shrimp tanks, and outdoor ponds where it absorbs excess nutrients, helps reduce algae, and provides shelter for fish fry and shrimp.

Giant Duckweed is extremely easy to care for and adapts well to a wide range of conditions, making it an excellent choice for both beginners and experienced aquarists.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Spirodela polyrhiza
Common Name: Giant Duckweed
Plant Type: Floating Plant

• Larger leaves than common duckweed for a more attractive floating plant appearance
• Fast-growing surface plant that naturally multiplies in nutrient-rich water
• Provides shade and shelter for fish, shrimp, and fry
• Helps reduce excess nutrients and improve overall water quality

🌱 Growth & Care

Lighting: Low to Medium
CO₂: Not required
Growth Rate: Fast

Placement: Floating on the water surface
Propagation: Natural division (rapid multiplication)

For best results, allow Giant Duckweed to float freely on the water surface. Under proper lighting and nutrient levels, the plant will reproduce quickly and form a natural floating cover that benefits both fish and shrimp.

🖼️ Aquascaping & Aquarium Benefits

• Creates natural surface coverage in planted aquariums
• Helps reduce algae by limiting excess light penetration
• Excellent for shrimp tanks, breeding tanks, and fry protection
• Great floating plant option for aquariums and outdoor ponds

📦 What You’ll Receive

A small portion (approximately 2” × 2”) of healthy Giant Duckweed containing 10+ plants.

Plants are carefully harvested and packed to ensure they arrive fresh and ready to add directly to your aquarium or pond.

Rare Tri-Color Dwarf Lotus • Aquarium Water Lily

Nymphaea micrantha is a beautiful and rare dwarf lotus prized for its unique tri-color foliage, displaying shades of green, bronze, red, and purple depending on lighting and nutrients. Native to tropical West Africa, this compact water lily makes an excellent centerpiece plant for planted aquariums without overwhelming smaller tanks.

With proper care, Nymphaea micrantha produces elegant submerged leaves and may send surface leaves if allowed, creating a natural and dynamic aquascape.

🌟 Key Features

  • Common Name: Dwarf Red Lotus, Tri-Color Lotus
  • Growth Rate: Moderate
  • Placement: Mid-ground to Background
  • Height: Approximately 8–16 inches
    (can grow taller if floating leaves are not trimmed)
  • Difficulty Level: Beginner-Friendly

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions

  • Lighting: Medium to High (enhances red and bronze coloration)
  • CO₂: Not Required

  • Water Temperature: 72–82°F (22–28°C)

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Creates a bold, colorful focal point
  • Adds texture and contrast among green plants
  • Suitable for nature, Dutch, and showcase aquariums

📦 What You’ll Receive

A healthy, sprouted Nymphaea micrantha bulb with visible leaf growth, carefully selected and packed by Aquarium Plants Factory®.

⚠️ Important Note:
Nymphaea micrantha is a naturally small lotus species.
The bulb size is tiny by nature, especially compared to larger lotus varieties. This is normal and expected. Despite its small bulb size, the plant is fully viable and will grow and develop normally once planted in a nutrient-rich substrate.

products.title: Rotala Macrandra Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Rotala Macrandra is a magnificent aquarium plant, however, it is quite demanding. This variety is a little simpler to grow and produces vibrant colors. It requires a lot of light to produce its gorgeous pink and red colors. It also preferred soft water and lots of CO2, micro, and macronutrients are essential for healthy growth.

Farm-raised Rotala Macrandra is easily broken in transit because of the fragile stems. It is also harder to adapt to the new environment when the plant reaches the mature stage. Therefore, here at Aquarium Plants Factory, we always recommended buying Rotala Macrandra in vitro, tissue culture version because they are still a young plant, sterile, and easier to adapt to your aquarium compare to the adult, farm raise version. Not to mention you get many plants in each cup, no snails, no algae, and perfect for any aquascape design.

products.title: Java Fern Large Size
products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus – Large Size Java Fern for Instant Impact

Microsorum pteropus, commonly known as Java Fern, is one of the most beloved and beginner-friendly aquatic plants in the hobby. This special large size variant, standing approximately 8–10 inches tall, is ideal for aquarists seeking a more mature, ready-to-display plant to add instant structure and greenery to their aquascape.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we cultivate these large-size Java Ferns in emersed conditions, resulting in strong, vibrant leaves and thick rhizomes that transition beautifully when submerged. The classic, sword-like leaves are rich green in color and fan outward in a graceful arch, making them perfect for midground to background placement in medium to large aquariums.

Java Fern is a rhizome-based plant and should always be attached to driftwood, rocks, or other hardscape. Never bury the rhizome in substrate, as this may lead to rot. It is incredibly hardy, low maintenance, and adaptable to a wide range of water parameters—making it suitable for both novice and experienced aquarists.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Special Large Size: Grows approx. 8–10" tall—perfect for instant visual impact

  • Hardy & Adaptable: Tolerates various lighting and water conditions

  • Attachable Rhizome: Secure to wood or stone—do not bury

  • Low-Tech Friendly: Grows without CO₂ and requires minimal fertilization

  • Classic Aquascaping Plant: A staple for nature-style and traditional aquascapes

🔎 Botanical Information:

  • Scientific Name: Microsorum pteropus

  • Synonyms: Leptochilus pteropus

  • Common Names: Java Fern

  • Plant Type: Rhizome plant

  • Placement: Midground / Background (Attach to hardscape)

  • Growth Rate: Slow

  • Lighting: Low to Medium

  • Origin: Southeast Asia

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Low to medium light preferred

  • CO₂ & Fertilization: CO₂ not required; occasional fertilization supports long-term health

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 6.0–7.5

    • Temperature: 68–82°F (20–28°C)

    • Water Hardness: Soft to moderately hard

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required

  • Can Grow Emersed: Yes

  • Propagation Method: Rhizome division

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose bare-root plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Bare-root Plants: Approximately 6+ leaf portion, loose rhizome (8–10 inches tall)

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 6+ leaf portion bundled with foam and plant weight (8–10 inches tall)

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 6+ leaf portion, potted in rockwool and net pot (8–10 inches tall)

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Photos are sample representations. Actual plants may vary in size, shape, or coloration. Unless otherwise specified, we do not guarantee plants are free of pests, pesticides, or exclusively submerged-grown.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Never release Java Fern—or any aquarium plant—into natural waterways. Always dispose of trimmings and unwanted portions responsibly to protect local ecosystems.

featured_image.alt: Java Fern (Microsorum pteropus) is a highly popular and versatile aquatic plant known for its lush, green foliage and ease of care. Native to Southeast Asia, this plant is a favorite among both beginner and experienced aquarists for its ability to thrive in various water conditions and low-light environments.

Java Fern is a resilient and attractive plant that brings numerous benefits to any freshwater aquarium. Its ease of care, adaptability, and aesthetic appeal make it a must-have for aquarists seeking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment.

products.title: Micro Sword
products.body_html:
🌾 Lilaeopsis brasiliensis – The Verdant Carpet of the Tropics

Lilaeopsis brasiliensis, known commonly as the Micro Sword, is a charming, grasslike aquatic plant native to the warm riverbanks of South America. With its fine, sword-shaped leaves and lush, vibrant green hue, it creates the perfect living carpet for aquascapes of all sizes.

Often admired for its soft texture and natural appeal, Micro Sword brings a tranquil meadow-like beauty to the foreground of aquariums. Under favorable conditions, it forms a dense, low-growing mat that sways gently with the current—evoking the serene underwater landscapes of tropical streams.

Compact, adaptable, and endlessly graceful, this species is ideal for both beginner and advanced aquascapers seeking an authentic, natural appearance.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Lilaeopsis brasiliensis

  • Common Name: Micro Sword, Brazilian Micro Sword

  • Origin: South America (Brazil, Paraguay, Argentina)

  • Form & Texture: Fine, grasslike leaves forming a vibrant green carpet

  • Growth Height: 2–7 cm – ideal for foreground placement

  • Growth Rate: Moderate – dense carpeting under bright light

  • Temperature Tolerance: 20–28°C – thrives in tropical freshwater aquariums

  • Water Conditions: pH 6.5–7.5 | Hardness 2–12°dGH | KH 2–8°dKH

  • Difficulty Level: Easy to Medium – suitable for most aquarists

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Medium to high – encourages compact, lush growth

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Optional but enhances color and density

  • Propagation: By runners – separate and replant to expand coverage

  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate recommended for best results

  • Can Grow Emersed: Yes – suitable for paludarium and humid terrarium setups

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Creates a lush, natural green carpet for freshwater aquascapes

  • Pairs beautifully with driftwood, stones, and midground species like Cryptocoryne or Anubias nana petite

  • Perfect for nature-style, Iwagumi, or minimalist layouts seeking vibrant ground cover

  • Provides a realistic and serene meadow-like appearance that complements shrimp and nano fish displays

📍 Cultivation & Sustainability

At Aquarium Plants Factory, every aquatic plant is sustainably farm-raised in controlled greenhouse environments—never collected from the wild.

  • Our Lilaeopsis brasiliensis is grown ex-vitro and hydroponically for strong roots and clean foliage

  • Ensures high-quality plants with minimal environmental impact

📦 What You’ll Receive

Choose from our selection of nursery-grown Lilaeopsis brasiliensis:

  • Loose Bare Root Plants: Approximately a small clump bare root portion

  • Bunched Plants: Small clump bundled with foam and a plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Potted in a net pot with rock wool

⚠️ Disclaimer

  • Some initial leaf melt may occur as the plant adjusts to new aquarium conditions

  • This is normal—new, submersed leaves will sprout from runners as the plant establishes itself

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plant Owner

  • Certain aquatic plants may be regulated or restricted in some U.S. states

  • Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways

  • Never release aquarium plants into the wild

This plant is perfect for adding a natural, grass-like texture to the foreground of your tank.

products.title: Staurogyne Repens Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Staurogyne repens in Vitro Culture® – Compact Foreground Plant with Rich Green, Spreading Growth

Staurogyne repens is a popular and hardy foreground plant native to the Rio Cristalino in Brazil. Known for its compact, creeping growth and vibrant green, oval leaves, it offers a lush, low-growing appearance that works beautifully in both high-tech and low-tech aquariums. Whether used to create a dense carpet or a structured midground border, S. repens provides natural contrast and plant diversity in aquascapes of all styles.

Now available in our sterile Vitro Culture® 4-inch bag, this plant is cultivated through Tissue Culture Technology to ensure a 100% algae-free, snail-free, and pesticide-free start—ideal for shrimp tanks, nano aquariums, and nature-style layouts.

  • Scientific Name: Staurogyne repens

  • Common Name: S. repens, Brazilian Creeper

  • Leaf Color: Bright to medium green with thick, rounded leaves

  • Growth Form: Low-growing, creeping stems with upright tips

  • Vitro Culture® Format: 4-inch sealed bag with multiple healthy plantlets in sterile gel or liquid

products.title: Staurogyne Repens
products.body_html:

Staurogyne Repens is a highly popular foreground aquarium plant prized for its compact growth, vibrant green leaves, and exceptional versatility in aquascaping. Native to South America, this hardy stem plant grows low and dense, making it an excellent choice for creating lush carpets or filling in the foreground of planted aquariums.

With its sturdy stems and creeping growth habit, Staurogyne Repens holds up well in both beginner and advanced setups. It adapts easily to a wide range of conditions and responds well to trimming, allowing aquascapers to shape and maintain a clean, natural look.

products.title: Helanthium Tenellum (Dwarf Chain Sword)
products.body_html:
Helanthium Tenellum (Dwarf Chain Sword) – Easy Foreground Carpet Aquarium Plant

Helanthium Tenellum, commonly known as Dwarf Chain Sword, is a popular foreground aquarium plant loved for its narrow, grass-like leaves and ability to spread into a natural green carpet over time. Formerly known as Echinodorus tenellus, this hardy plant is a great choice for aquarists who want a low-growing foreground plant that is easier to care for than many demanding carpeting plants.

With proper lighting, nutrients, and stable aquarium conditions, Helanthium Tenellum will send out runners and gradually fill open spaces in the foreground or midground of a planted aquarium.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Helanthium tenellum

  • Former Name / Synonym: Echinodorus tenellus

  • Common Name: Dwarf Chain Sword

  • Growth Form: Rosette plant that spreads by runners

  • Placement: Foreground to midground

  • Care Level: Easy to moderate

  • Color: Bright green to light green

  • Great Carpet Alternative: Easier than many high-demand carpeting plants

  • Natural Look: Creates a soft, grassy effect in aquascapes

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Medium to high light recommended for compact growth

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required, but CO₂ helps promote faster spreading and healthier growth

  • Growth Rate: Moderate to fast once established

  • Propagation: Spreads by runners, producing new small plantlets over time

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended for best root development and runner production

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

Helanthium Tenellum is a root-feeding plant and benefits from nutrient-rich substrate, aquasoil, or root tabs. After planting, it may take time to adjust before sending out new runners.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Excellent choice for creating a natural green foreground carpet

  • Works well in nature-style, low-tech, and high-tech planted aquariums

  • Great for softening hardscape areas around rocks and driftwood

  • Can be trimmed and managed to create a low, grassy appearance

  • Suitable for beginner aquascapers looking for an easier foreground plant

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as small bare-root plant portions in the following pack sizes:

1 Pack: Includes 1 small portion, approximately 2–4 small plants
5 Pack: Includes 5 small portions, each portion approximately 2–4 small plants
10 Pack: Includes 10 small portions, each portion approximately 2–4 small plants

Each portion may vary slightly in size due to natural plant growth and harvesting.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Plants may be grown emersed or submerged. Some melting, leaf loss, or transition is normal as plants adapt to a new aquarium environment.

products.title: Eriocaulon Vietnam
products.body_html:
Vietnam Eriocaulon (Eriocaulon sp. Vietnam) is a rare and highly sought-after aquarium plant, originating from Thanh Hoa, Vietnam. Known for its grass-like appearance and broad, arching medium-green leaves, this species reaches a height of 15–30 cm, making it a beautiful midground or background choice in aquascapes. Often compared to Eriocaulon sp. "Goias" due to its similar structure, Vietnam Eriocaulon stands out with its ease of care and adaptability.

Unlike the more challenging Blyxa japonica, Vietnam Eriocaulon is easier to cultivate and requires less attention, thriving in a variety of water conditions. It grows well in moderate lighting and can even flourish in hard water environments, making it more versatile than other Eriocaulon species. While it appreciates nutrient-rich substrates and CO2 supplementation, it requires minimal maintenance, making it suitable for both intermediate and experienced aquarists.

Key Features:

  • Distinctive Appearance: Grass-like plant with medium-green, arching leaves, creating a striking focal point in any aquarium.
  • Compact and Versatile: Grows to a height of 15–30 cm, suitable for midground or background placement in aquascapes.
  • Easy to Grow: Simpler to cultivate than other species, including Blyxa japonica, making it more accessible to a wider range of hobbyists.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives under moderate lighting, though it can adapt to slightly higher levels for optimal growth.
  • Water Parameters: Tolerates a wide range of water conditions, including hard water, with a pH between 6.0 and 7.5 and temperatures of 68°F to 78°F (20°C to 26°C).
  • Nutrient-Rich Substrate: Benefits from a nutrient-rich environment to support healthy growth and vibrant foliage.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Low Maintenance: Requires minimal upkeep, making it an excellent choice for aquarists looking for a beautiful yet easy-to-manage plant.
  • Aquascaping Flexibility: Works well as a midground or background plant in a variety of setups, enhancing both nano and larger aquariums.
  • Hard Water Tolerance: Unlike many other Eriocaulon species, Vietnam Eriocaulon thrives in hard water, making it an adaptable option for different aquarium environments.

Conclusion:

Vietnam Eriocaulon is a unique and rare plant that offers the beauty of grass-like foliage with the ease of low maintenance. Ideal for aquarists seeking a striking yet manageable plant, its adaptability to moderate light and hard water conditions makes it a standout addition to both beginner and advanced planted tanks. Elevate your aquascape with the natural elegance of Vietnam Eriocaulon, a plant that offers beauty without the hassle.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in three convenient formats:

  • Bare-root Plants: Includes approximately 1-2 loose bare-roots plants. Perfect for flexible planting arrangements.
  • Rooted Bunched: Approximately 1-2 rooted plants, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Rooted Potted: About 1-2 rooted plants, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.
